Port CFS billing, customs bonded warehouse compliance, and contain']
  weighing_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: SOLAS VGM regulatory compliance requires tracing which certified weighing_station captured the container\'s verified gross mass. container_visit stores vgm_weight_kg and vgm_method but has no FK to the']
  wharfage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Wharfage is charged on cargo passing over the wharf per container visit. The wharfage_schedule governs the applicable rate by cargo type, container type, and trade direction. Port revenue reporting an']
  yard_slot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.yard_slot. Business justification: container_visit has current_yard_location (STRING) and yard_block (STRING) which are denormalized representations of the container\'s current position. Should FK to yard_slot for authoritative location']
  arrival_mode varchar [note: 'Transportation mode by which the container arrived at the terminal. Determines gate processing, yard allocation, and intermodal handling requirements.']
  booking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier booking reference number for the container shipment. Links container to commercial booking and shipping instructions.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of cargo carried in the container. Determines handling requirements, equipment assignment, and yard allocation strategy.']
  consignee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the consignee (importer) for this container shipment. Sourced from BOL or booking documentation.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Unique container identification number following ISO 6346 standard format (4 letters + 7 digits). The globally unique identifier for the physical container unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this container visit record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of container damage if damage_flag is true. Documents condition for repair estimates and liability determination.']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container has reported damage. Triggers inspection workflow and potential repair work orders.']
  delivery_order_number varchar [note: 'Delivery order number authorizing release of the container to the consignee or their agent. Required document for import container gate-out.']
  demurrage_start_date date [note: 'Date when demurrage charges begin to accrue for this container visit. Calculated as arrival date plus free time days.']
  departure_mode varchar [note: 'Planned or actual transportation mode for container departure from the terminal. Null until departure is scheduled or executed.']
  dwell_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in hours that the container has remained in the terminal from arrival to current moment (or to departure if departed). Key performance indicator for terminal efficiency and customer service.']
  final_destination_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the final destination for the container cargo. May differ from POD for transshipment or intermodal movements.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of free storage time allowed before demurrage or detention charges begin. Defined by tariff or commercial agreement.']
  full_empty_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the container is loaded with cargo (full) or empty. Critical for weight calculations, handling procedures, and billing.']
  gate_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container physically entered the terminal through the gate. Marks the start of terminal custody and dwell time calculation for truck/rail arrivals.']
  gate_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container physically exited the terminal through the gate. Marks the end of terminal custody and completes dwell time calculation for truck/rail departures.']
  oog_dimensions varchar [note: 'Description of OOG measurements if oog_flag is true. Specifies overhang dimensions (front, back, left, right, top) in standard format for equipment planning.']
  oog_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container or its cargo exceeds standard ISO dimensions (out-of-gauge). Requires special handling equipment and yard allocation.']
  pod_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the container is to be discharged from the vessel. Five-character code per UN/LOCODE standard.']
  pol_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the container was loaded onto the vessel. Five-character code per UN/LOCODE standard.']
  reefer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is a refrigerated (reefer) unit requiring temperature control and power supply during terminal stay.']
  reefer_humidity_setpoint_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target relative humidity setting for refrigerated container as percentage. Required for certain cargo types (e.g., fresh produce) to prevent spoilage.']
  reefer_temperature_setpoint_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for refrigerated container in degrees Celsius. Critical for cargo preservation; monitored continuously by terminal reefer management system.']
  reefer_ventilation_setting varchar [note: 'Ventilation configuration for reefer container (e.g., closed, 10 CBM/hr, 20 CBM/hr). Controls fresh air exchange rate for perishable cargo.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier attached to the container for automated tracking and gate processing. Enables touchless identification at gate and yard checkpoints.']
  seal_number_1 varchar [note: 'Primary security seal number affixed to the container door. Used for customs verification and cargo security tracking per ISPS Code requirements.']
  seal_number_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary or customs seal number if multiple seals are applied. Common for high-security shipments or specific customs requirements.']
  shipper_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipper (exporter) for this container shipment. Sourced from BOL or booking documentation.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the container itself in kilograms, excluding cargo. Used for VGM Method 2 calculations and payload verification.']
  teu_factor decimal(3,1) [note: 'TEU conversion factor for the container. Standard values: 1.0 for 20ft containers, 2.0 for 40ft containers, 2.25 for 45ft containers. Used for capacity planning and billing calculations.']
  tos_reference_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this container visit in the source Terminal Operating System (NAVIS N4 or TOPS Expert). Used for system integration and audit trail.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the specific dangerous goods substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazmat containers per IMDG Code.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this container visit record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  vessel_discharge_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was discharged from the vessel onto the terminal. Applicable for vessel arrival mode; marks start of terminal custody for vessel-delivered containers.']
  vessel_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the container was loaded onto the vessel from the terminal. Applicable for vessel departure mode; marks end of terminal custody for vessel-bound containers.']
  vgm_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine VGM: Method 1 (weighing packed container) or Method 2 (weighing contents and adding tare weight). Required for SOLAS compliance documentation.']
  vgm_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Verified Gross Mass of the packed container in kilograms, as required by SOLAS regulation VI/2. Mandatory for all full export containers before vessel loading.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the container visit within the terminal. Tracks progression from arrival through departure, including hold states for customs or operational reasons.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a container\'s complete terminal visit lifecycle from arrival (gate-in, vessel discharge, or rail-in) to departure (gate-out, vessel load, or rail-out). Captures container number, ISO type code (per BIC/ISO 6346), size (TEU/FEU), visit status (inbound, in-yard, outbound, departed), current yard slot position, vessel visit reference, BOL reference, cargo type, VGM weight (SOLAS), seal numbers, damage flags, reefer temperature settings, IMDG class, TOS tracking identifiers, and dwell time. Core operational entity linking all terminal activities — gate transactions, equipment moves, reefer monitoring, service orders, and hazmat declarations reference this record. Aligns with DCSA Transport Event model for container tracking milestones. SSOT for real-time container position and status within NAVIS N4 and TOPS Expert.'
}

  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Gate cannot release containers under active customs hold. Real-time hold status check at gate is critical operational control point. Prevents unauthorized release of detained cargo.']

Gate-out transactions require a valid, released delivery order — a fundamental import release control. A proper FK enforces this bus']

  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trucking or transport company responsible for the container movement.']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Gate transactions require sanctions screening of trucks, drivers, and cargo at port entry per CTPAT, AEO, and port security frameworks. The gate_transaction.rpm_scan_result field confirms radiation/sc']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Gate transactions that trigger security incidents (failed credential, contraband detection, unauthorized vehicle) must be directly linked for incident investigation reports and ISPS audit. The existin']
  terminal_gate_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.gate_appointment. Business justification: Gate transactions often fulfill pre-booked appointments. The gate_transaction has appointment_reference (STRING) which should be normalized to FK to gate_appointment. Business reality: a gate transact']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Every gate transaction occurs at a specific terminal. While terminal can be derived via gate_lane -> terminal, a direct terminal_id FK on gate_transaction enables efficient terminal-level gate through']
  truck_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.truck_appointment. Business justification: Gate transactions execute pre-booked truck appointments. TOS systems match arriving trucks to appointment slots for validation, lane assignment, and processing time tracking. Critical for appointment ']
  truck_gate_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.truck_gate_booking. Business justification: A gate_transaction is the physical execution of a truck_gate_booking. Gate systems match arriving trucks to their pre-booked appointments for access authorization, no-show tracking, and dwell-time SLA']
  verified_gross_mass_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.verified_gross_mass. Business justification: Gate transactions capture weight_bridge_reading_kg. Linking to the formal VGM record enables cross-validation of gate-weighed mass against the SOLAS-compliant VGM submission — required for port author']
  weighing_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: Gate transactions record weight_bridge_reading_kg for overweight detection and SOLAS VGM. Linking to the specific weighing_station enables calibration audit trails, equipment certification compliance ']
  anpr_capture_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) system capture of vehicle license plates for automated gate processing.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'BOL (Bill of Lading) number representing the contract of carriage and receipt of goods for the container.']
  booking_reference varchar [note: 'Shipping line booking reference number associated with the container movement.']
  coparn_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the COPARN EDI message (Container Pre-Announcement) sent prior to gate arrival for pre-clearance and planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this gate transaction record was first created in the NAVIS N4 system.']
  damage_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any damage observed on the container, chassis, or cargo during gate processing.']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether visible damage to the container or cargo was detected during gate inspection.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued driver license number used for driver identity verification at gate entry.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the driver presenting at the gate for identity verification and security compliance.']
  driver_phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number for the driver for operational communication and emergency contact purposes.']
  gate_clerk_override_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the gate clerk manually overrode automated gate processing rules or validations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this gate transaction record was last updated or modified.']
  ocr_capture_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the OCR (Optical Character Recognition) system capture of container number, license plates, and other visual data at gate.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the gate clerk for any manual override or exception processing.']
  processing_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds taken to process the gate transaction from entry to exit, used for gate performance KPI measurement.']
  reefer_temperature_actual_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius from the reefer container monitoring system at gate transaction time.']
  reefer_temperature_setpoint_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setpoint in Celsius for refrigerated (reefer) containers requiring temperature control.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tag number read from the container or vehicle for automated tracking and identification.']
  rpm_scan_result varchar [note: 'Result of the RPM (Radiation Portal Monitor) scan performed at gate entry for security screening and detection of radioactive materials.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number affixed to the container door for tamper detection and cargo security verification.']
  seal_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the container seal verification check performed at gate, indicating seal integrity.']
  trailer_license_plate varchar [note: 'Vehicle registration number of the trailer or chassis unit, if separate from the tractor unit.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the gate transaction, used for operational reference and audit trails.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gate transaction indicating processing state and completion.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the container or vehicle movement through the gate occurred, representing the primary business event time.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gate movement type indicating the mode of transport and direction of movement through the terminal gate.']
  truck_license_plate varchar [note: 'Vehicle registration number of the truck or tractor unit involved in the gate transaction, captured via ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) or manual entry.']
  verified_gross_mass_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'VGM (Verified Gross Mass) of the packed container as required by SOLAS regulations for vessel loading safety.']
  weight_bridge_reading_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross weight of the container and vehicle measured at the terminal weight bridge in kilograms, used for safety compliance and billing verification.']
  yard_location_assignment varchar [note: 'Assigned yard location (block, row, tier) where the container will be or was stacked in the Container Yard (CY).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every container or vehicle movement through the terminal gate complex. Captures gate lane, transaction type (truck-in, truck-out, rail-in, rail-out), container number (per ISO 6346), truck/vehicle registration, driver identity, appointment reference, COPARN pre-announcement reference, customs release status, radiation portal monitor (RPM) scan result, weight bridge reading, OCR/ANPR capture reference, timestamp of entry/exit, and gate clerk override flags. Supports ISPS Code gate security requirements. SSOT for gate operations in NAVIS N4.'
}

Linking appointment to screening result enables pre-clearan']

  appointment_window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the scheduled appointment time slot window, defining the latest acceptable arrival time for the appointment.']
  appointment_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the scheduled appointment time slot window during which the truck is expected to arrive at the gate.']

  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container has been selected for physical inspection by customs, port state control, or security authorities.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of inspection required for the container (customs examination, security scan, quarantine check, port state control).']

  is_overweight boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container exceeds standard weight limits and requires special handling or equipment.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the gate appointment record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the gate appointment record was last modified or updated.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the truck failed to arrive during the scheduled appointment window without prior cancellation.']
  processing_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes taken to process the gate transaction from arrival to departure, used for KPI measurement.']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setting in Celsius for refrigerated containers, applicable when container_size_type indicates reefer unit.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes recorded by gate operators regarding special circumstances, issues, or observations during the appointment.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of gate transaction being performed, defining the nature of container movement through the terminal gate.']
  truck_driver_license varchar [note: 'Driver license number of the truck driver, captured for security and compliance purposes.']
  truck_driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the truck driver presenting at the gate for the appointment.']
  truck_registration varchar [note: 'Vehicle registration plate number of the truck making the gate appointment, used for access control and tracking.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance being transported, required for dangerous goods declarations.']
  verified_gross_mass_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'SOLAS-compliant verified gross mass of the packed container in kilograms, mandatory for export containers.']
  vgm_method varchar [note: 'SOLAS method used to determine VGM: Method 1 (weighing packed container) or Method 2 (weighing contents and adding tare).']

  Note: 'Master record for pre-booked truck gate appointments. Captures appointment number, appointment window (date/time slot), container number, truck registration, haulier/transport operator, transaction type (delivery, receival, empty return, empty pickup), appointment status (pending, confirmed, arrived, completed, cancelled, no-show), and COPARN/IFTMIN message reference. Enables controlled gate flow and reduces truck queuing.'
}

Table terminal.equipment_dispatch {
  equipment_dispatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the equipment dispatch work instruction record. Primary key.']
  access_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_credential. Business justification: Equipment operators require valid security credentials to access restricted zones (waterside, customs, hazmat areas) per MARSEC protocols. Tracks operator clearance for dispatch authorization, zone ac']
  container_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.container_visit. Business justification: Equipment dispatch work instructions move containers that are part of terminal visits. The equipment_dispatch table has container_number (STRING) but no FK to container_visit. Adding container_visit_i']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Equipment dispatches represent resource consumption that must be allocated to cost centers for activity-based costing, operational cost control, and productivity analysis. Each dispatch is costed to t']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: TOS systems must verify active customs holds before authorizing equipment moves on a container. Equipment dispatches against held containers require hold reference for operational control and audit tr']
  dangerous_goods_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.dangerous_goods_declaration. Business justification: Equipment dispatches for IMDG-classified containers require DGD reference for safe handling procedures (EMS, MFAG). Linking enables automated hazmat handling instruction delivery to equipment operator']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific terminal handling equipment unit assigned to execute this dispatch instruction (e.g., RTG-0012, STS-0003, ASC-0007). Format: three-letter equipment type code followed by hyphen and four-digit unit number.']
  handling_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.handling_order. Business justification: Equipment dispatches execute work instructions defined in handling orders. This is the fundamental link between terminal equipment execution and cargo handling plans — enabling gang productivity track']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Equipment dispatch for hazmat containers requires IMDG class reference to enforce certified equipment assignment (IMDG-certified cranes/vehicles) and generate dangerous goods handling records for port']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Crane and equipment dispatch operations are tracked against internal orders for CAPEX/OPEX project cost control (e.g., vessel turnaround projects, productivity improvement programs). Port asset financ']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.manifest. Business justification: Equipment dispatches (crane/RTG moves) are planned against the vessel manifest for a port call. Linking enables crane productivity reporting per manifest, discharge/load sequence validation, and manif']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the vessel visit for vessel loading/discharge operations. Null for yard-only or gate operations.']
  rail_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_visit. Business justification: Terminal equipment dispatches load/discharge containers to/from rail wagons. Linking dispatch to rail visit enables rail productivity reporting (moves per hour), rail operator billing verification, an']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Equipment dispatch work instructions are issued within a specific terminal context. While terminal can be derived via terminal_equipment -> terminal_zone -> terminal, a direct terminal_id FK enables e']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Terminal handling charges (THC) are applied per container move (load/discharge/shift). Equipment dispatch records the productive move; linking to thc_schedule enables accurate per-move billing based o']
  truck_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.truck_appointment. Business justification: Equipment dispatch for truck gate moves is directly triggered by a truck appointment slot. The TOS dispatches RTGs or reach stackers to the correct yard position based on the truck appointment window ']
  bay_position varchar [note: 'Vessel bay number for container stowage position (e.g., 012, 084). Two or three-digit bay identifier. Null for non-vessel operations.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'BOL (Bill of Lading) number for the shipment associated with this container or cargo. Legal document of title and receipt.']
  booking_reference varchar [note: 'Shipping line booking reference number associated with this container or cargo. Used for customer billing and service tracking.']
  cargo_reference varchar [note: 'General cargo identifier for non-containerized cargo (breakbulk, RoRo units, project cargo). Used when container_number is null.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work instruction was completed by the equipment operator. Null if not yet completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this equipment dispatch record was first created in the TOS system. Audit trail field.']
  customs_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container or cargo is under customs hold (True) and cannot be released until customs clearance is obtained, or cleared for release (False).']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container or cargo has reported damage (True) requiring inspection or special handling, or no damage (False).']
  destination_position varchar [note: 'Target location for the container or cargo in yard slot coordinate format: block-bay-row-tier. For vessel loading, uses vessel hatch-bay-row-tier format. For gate operations, may use gate lane identifier.']
  dispatch_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the dispatch instruction: TOS_auto_dispatch (system-generated by NAVIS N4 or TOPS Expert auto-dispatch algorithm), manual_planner_override (planner manual assignment), operator_request (operator-initiated), emergency_dispatch (urgent safety or operational requirement).']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch instruction: pending (queued for assignment), assigned (allocated to equipment), in_progress (actively executing), completed (finished successfully), cancelled (voided before execution), suspended (temporarily halted).']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work instruction was dispatched to the equipment operator or automated system. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of terminal handling equipment: RTG (Rubber Tyred Gantry), STS (Ship-to-Shore crane), QC (Quay Crane), ASC (Automated Stacking Crane), MHC (Mobile Harbour Crane), AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle), reach_stacker, forklift, empty_handler, terminal_tractor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RTG|STS|QC|ASC|MHC|AGV|reach_stacker|forklift|empty_handler|terminal_tractor — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gross_crane_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from crane hook engagement to hook release, including all delays. Used for STS/QC productivity analysis. Null for non-crane operations.']
  hatch_position varchar [note: 'Vessel hatch identifier for STS/QC crane operations (e.g., H01, H12). Format: H followed by two-digit hatch number. Null for non-vessel operations.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container or cargo contains hazardous materials (True) requiring IMDG Code compliance and special handling procedures, or non-hazardous (False).']
  idle_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Non-productive idle time in seconds during the dispatch operation due to delays, waiting, or interruptions. Calculated as gross_crane_time_seconds minus net_crane_time_seconds for crane operations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this equipment dispatch record was last modified in the TOS system. Audit trail field.']
  moves_per_hour decimal(5,2) [note: 'Equipment productivity metric calculated as moves completed per hour. Key performance indicator for terminal equipment efficiency. Typical ranges: STS/QC 25-35 MPH, RTG 15-20 MPH, ASC 20-25 MPH.']
  net_crane_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Productive crane time in seconds excluding idle time, delays, and interruptions. Used to calculate net crane productivity. Null for non-crane operations.']
  origin_position varchar [note: 'Starting location of the container or cargo in yard slot coordinate format: block-bay-row-tier (e.g., YA-12-05-03 for yard block YA, bay 12, row 5, tier 3). For vessel operations, uses hatch-bay-row-tier format.']
  oversized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo exceeds standard container dimensions (True) requiring special handling equipment or procedures, or standard size (False).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Dispatch priority ranking from 1 (highest) to 10 (lowest). Used by TOS auto-dispatch algorithms to sequence work instructions. Vessel operations typically receive priority 1-3, gate operations 4-6, yard housekeeping 7-10.']
  productive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch represents a productive move (True) or non-productive move (False). Productive moves directly contribute to vessel or customer operations; non-productive moves are internal yard housekeeping or rehandles.']
  reefer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is a refrigerated (reefer) container (True) requiring temperature-controlled storage and power connection, or non-reefer (False).']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setpoint in degrees Celsius for refrigerated containers. Typical range: -30°C to +30°C. Null for non-reefer containers.']
  rehandle_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispatch is a rehandle operation (True) - moving a container to access another container - or a direct move (False). Rehandles negatively impact terminal productivity metrics.']
  rehandle_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for rehandle operations: access_obstruction (blocking container), weight_segregation (stacking by weight class), hazmat_compliance (IMDG segregation), reefer_relocation (reefer plug availability), vessel_premarshal (pre-stow optimization), damage_inspection (damage assessment access), customs_hold (regulatory inspection). Null for non-rehandle moves. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: access_obstruction|weight_segregation|hazmat_compliance|reefer_relocation|vessel_premarshal|damage_inspection|customs_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  spreader_type varchar [note: 'Type of crane spreader attachment used for this lift: standard_20ft (fixed 20-foot), standard_40ft (fixed 40-foot), telescopic (adjustable 20-40 foot), twin_20ft (dual 20-foot), tandem_40ft (dual 40-foot for heavy lifts), breakbulk_hook (non-container cargo). Applicable to crane operations.']
  tandem_lift_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation uses tandem-lift (True) - two cranes working together on a single heavy lift - or single crane (False). Used for oversized or heavy project cargo.']
  twin_lift_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crane operation is a twin-lift (True) - lifting two 20-foot containers simultaneously - or single lift (False). Applicable only to STS/QC crane operations.']
  work_instruction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the dispatch work instruction, formatted as WI- followed by 10 digits. Used for tracking and reference across TOS systems.']
  work_instruction_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment operation being dispatched: lift (vertical movement), move (horizontal transfer), load (vessel loading), discharge (vessel unloading), shift (yard repositioning), restow (vessel re-stowage), rehandle (access movement), reshuffle (yard optimization). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lift|move|load|discharge|shift|restow|rehandle|reshuffle — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual work instructions dispatched to terminal handling equipment (RTG, STS/QC, ASC, MHC, AGV, reach stacker, forklift). Captures work instruction type (lift, move, load, discharge, shift, restow, rehandle/reshuffle), equipment unit assigned, container or cargo reference, origin and destination positions (slot coordinates), priority level, dispatch and completion timestamps, operator ID, productive/non-productive flag, rehandle reason code (access obstruction, weight segregation, hazmat compliance, reefer relocation, vessel pre-marshalling), crane-specific attributes (hatch/bay position, twin-lift/tandem-lift flag, idle time, spreader type, gross/net crane time for STS/QC operations), and moves-per-hour (MPH) productivity metric. Supports TOS auto-dispatch and manual planner override workflows. SSOT for ALL equipment movement instructions including quay crane lifts and yard rehandles in TOPS Expert and NAVIS N4.'
}

The vessel_bay_plan table has container_number (STRING) but no F']

Linking to DGD enables automated segregation validation (stowage categories, incompatible goods) — a mandatory IMDG Code r']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Vessel stowage planning for dangerous goods requires IMDG class reference to enforce segregation rules, stowage categories, and SOLAS compliance. Bay plan approval workflows validate IMDG class agains']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.manifest. Business justification: Vessel bay plans are the stowage plans for a vessel call; the manifest is the authoritative cargo document for the same call. Linking enables stowage-to-manifest reconciliation for customs, port autho']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vessel call or visit for which this bay plan applies.']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: A vessel bay plan (BAPLIE-based stowage and operations plan) is created for a vessel call at a specific terminal. While terminal can be derived via terminal_berth_allocation, a direct terminal_id FK o']
  vessel_call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Bay plans are stowage plans created for vessel call bookings. Links planning artifact to original booking for tracking booking requirements (reefer count, hazmat, TEU) against planned stowage configur']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.vessel. Business justification: Stowage coordinators building bay plans must directly reference vessel LOA, beam, bay count, and TEU capacity to validate stowage configurations. Traversing vessel_bay_plan→call→vessel is operationall']
  voyage_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.voyage_nomination. Business justification: The voyage_nomination provides expected TEU, reefer, hazmat, and OOG counts that directly drive bay plan pre-allocation. Terminal planners create the initial bay plan from the voyage nomination before']
  bay_number varchar [note: 'Longitudinal position identifier on the vessel indicating the bay location for container stowage.']
  cargo_cutoff_datetime timestamp [note: 'Deadline timestamp by which all export cargo must be delivered to the terminal for loading onto this vessel call.']
  discharge_sequence varchar [note: 'Planned sequence order for discharging this container from the vessel to optimize operational efficiency.']
  hatch_number varchar [note: 'Vessel hatch identifier for grouping bays and planning hatch cover operations and crane work sequences.']
  hatch_sequence_plan varchar [note: 'Planned operational sequence for working vessel hatches to optimize crane productivity and minimize vessel turnaround time.']
  load_sequence varchar [note: 'Planned sequence order for loading this container onto the vessel to optimize operational efficiency and vessel stability.']
  oog_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container or cargo exceeds standard ISO dimensions requiring special handling and stowage considerations.']
  oog_overhang_front_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Front overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.']
  oog_overhang_left_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Left side overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.']
  oog_overhang_rear_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Rear overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.']
  oog_overhang_right_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Right side overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.']
  oog_overheight_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height overhang dimension in centimeters for out-of-gauge cargo. Null for standard containers.']
  plan_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel bay plan was formally approved and released for operational execution.']
  plan_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel bay plan record was initially created in the terminal operating system.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vessel bay plan indicating planning and execution stage.']
  plan_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel bay plan record was last modified or revised.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the bay plan to track revisions and updates to the stowage plan throughout the vessel call lifecycle.']
  planned_crane_count varchar [note: 'Number of STS (Ship-to-Shore) or QC (Quay Crane) units allocated for this vessel operation to achieve target productivity.']
  planned_gang_count varchar [note: 'Number of stevedore gangs or labor teams allocated for vessel loading and discharge operations.']
  planned_operation_end timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for completing all vessel cargo operations including final crane move and securing.']
  planned_operation_start timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for commencing vessel cargo operations including first crane move.']
  pod_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE for the port where this container is scheduled to be discharged from the vessel.']
  pol_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE for the port where this container was loaded onto the vessel.']
  pre_marshalling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether containers require pre-marshalling or repositioning in the yard prior to vessel loading to optimize load sequence.']
  reefer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the container is a refrigerated unit requiring temperature control and power connection.']
  reefer_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setting in Celsius for refrigerated containers. Null for non-reefer units.']
  resource_allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting equipment allocation decisions, operational constraints, or special resource requirements for this vessel call.']
  restow_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this container requires restowing or repositioning on the vessel to access underlying cargo or optimize stowage.']
  row_number varchar [note: 'Transverse position identifier on the vessel indicating the row location for container stowage.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Container seal number for security and customs verification purposes.']
  stowage_instruction varchar [note: 'Special handling or stowage instructions for the container including segregation requirements, securing notes, or operational constraints.']
  teu_count decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total TEU count for this vessel call bay plan representing standardized container volume measurement.']
  tier_number varchar [note: 'Vertical position identifier on the vessel indicating the tier or stack level for container stowage.']
  total_discharge_moves varchar [note: 'Total number of container discharge moves planned for this vessel call across all bays and hatches.']
  total_load_moves varchar [note: 'Total number of container load moves planned for this vessel call across all bays and hatches.']
  transhipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this container is transhipment cargo that will be transferred to another vessel at this port.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations number identifying hazardous substances and articles for dangerous goods. Null for non-hazardous cargo.']

  Note: 'Master record of the vessel call operational and stowage plan combining BAPLIE (UN/EDIFACT) data with resource and operation planning. Captures vessel visit reference, berth allocation reference, bay/row/tier container positions, ISO types, weights, POD/POL, hazardous goods class, reefer settings, OOG dimensions, stowage instructions, total planned discharge and load moves, planned crane and gang count, planned operation start/end time, cargo cut-off datetime, hatch sequence plan, pre-marshalling requirements, operation planner ID, and resource allocation notes. Aligns with SMDG BAPLIE 3.x standard for stowage data exchange. SSOT for vessel stowage planning, load/discharge sequencing, and vessel call operational planning in NAVIS N4.'
}

  allocated_crane_count varchar [note: 'Number of Ship-to-Shore (STS) cranes or Quay Cranes (QC) allocated for cargo operations during this berth allocation. Impacts vessel turnaround time and terminal productivity.']
  allocated_quay_length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length of quay allocated for this vessel in meters. Must accommodate vessel Length Overall (LOA) plus safety margins.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments related to this berth allocation. May include operational instructions, coordination details, or special circumstances.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the berth allocation. Format: BA-YYYYMMDD-NNNN where YYYYMMDD is allocation date and NNNN is sequence number.']
  allocation_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for this specific berth allocation. May include service requirements, vessel preferences, operational constraints, or commercial agreements.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the berth allocation. Planned: initial allocation created; Confirmed: vessel and berth confirmed; Active: vessel currently berthed; Completed: vessel departed; Cancelled: allocation cancelled; Suspended: temporarily on hold.']
  atb timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel berthed at the allocated berth. Captured from Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS) or Terminal Operating System (TOS).']
  atd timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel departed from the allocated berth. Captured from Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS) or Terminal Operating System (TOS).']
  berth_draft_restriction_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable draft at the allocated berth in meters. Vessel draft must not exceed this value for safe berthing operations.']
  berth_productivity_target_mph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target container handling productivity in moves per hour for this berth allocation. Used for performance monitoring and SLA compliance.']
  berth_window_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned duration of the berth window in hours. Calculated from berth window start and end times. Used for berth utilization KPIs.']
  berth_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the allocated berth window. Defines the latest time the berth is reserved for this vessel before it must be released.']
  berth_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the allocated berth window. Defines the earliest time the berth is reserved for this vessel.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation if allocation status is cancelled. Includes vessel no-show, schedule changes, operational constraints, or customer request.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth allocation was cancelled. Null if allocation has not been cancelled.']
  cargo_operation_type varchar [note: 'Type of cargo operations planned for this berth allocation. LoLo: Lift-on Lift-off (container); RoRo: Roll-on Roll-off (vehicles); Mixed: combination; Bulk: bulk cargo; General: general cargo.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth allocation was confirmed by the berth planner and vessel operator. Represents transition from planned to confirmed status.']
  crane_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Type of cranes allocated for this berth. STS: Ship-to-Shore crane; QC: Quay Crane; MHC: Mobile Harbour Crane; Mixed: combination of crane types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth allocation record was first created in the Terminal Operating System. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  etb timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is estimated to berth at the allocated berth. Used for berth planning and resource scheduling.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is estimated to depart from the allocated berth. Used for berth window planning and next vessel scheduling.']
  expected_container_moves varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of container moves (load + discharge) planned for this vessel call. Used for resource planning and productivity forecasting.']
  expected_teu_volume varchar [note: 'Expected total TEU volume to be handled during this berth allocation. Includes both loading and discharge operations.']
  imdg_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this berth allocation involves dangerous goods cargo requiring IMDG compliance. True if dangerous goods are present; False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth allocation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  mooring_gang_count varchar [note: 'Number of mooring gangs allocated for securing the vessel to the berth. Typically ranges from 1 to 4 depending on vessel size and weather conditions.']
  pilotage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage services are required for this berth allocation. True if pilotage is mandatory; False if vessel can berth without pilot.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this berth allocation. Critical: emergency or VIP vessel; High: priority service; Normal: standard service; Low: flexible scheduling.']
  sla_turnaround_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contractual Service Level Agreement turnaround time commitment for this vessel in hours. Measured from ATB to ATD.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated this berth allocation. NAVIS_N4: NAVIS N4 TOS; TOPS_EXPERT: TOPS Expert TOS; VTMS: Vessel Traffic Management System; Manual: manually entered.']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special handling requirements for this berth allocation such as IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) cargo, refrigerated containers, oversized cargo, or security protocols.']
  tide_window_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this berth allocation is constrained by tidal conditions. True if vessel can only berth during specific tide windows; False if tide-independent.']
  towage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether towage (tug) services are required for this berth allocation. True if tugs are needed for berthing/unberthing; False otherwise.']
  vessel_draft_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum draft of the vessel in meters. Must be validated against berth depth restrictions to ensure safe berthing.']
  vessel_loa_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length Overall of the vessel in meters. Used to validate berth allocation suitability and quay length requirements.']
  weather_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this berth allocation has weather-related restrictions (wind speed, wave height, visibility). True if weather-sensitive; False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of berth assignments for vessel calls at the terminal. Captures berth identifier, vessel visit reference, planned and actual berthing time (ETB/ATB), planned and actual departure time (ETD/ATD), berth window duration, allocated quay length, draft restrictions, crane allocation count, berth planner ID, and allocation status (planned, confirmed, active, completed). SSOT for berth planning in TOPS Expert and NAVIS N4.'
}

Table terminal.reefer_monitoring {
  reefer_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for reefer_monitoring']
  charge_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Reefer monitoring sessions generate reefer electricity and monitoring surcharge charge events. Billing must trace each reefer charge_event to the originating monitoring record for reefer power billing']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Cold chain compliance validation, temperature range verification against commodity specifications, and cargo-specific monitoring protocols require HS code reference. Essential for perishable cargo qua']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the reefer container being monitored. Links to the container master data.']
  container_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.container_visit. Business justification: Reefer monitoring events track temperature and condition of refrigerated containers during their terminal visit. The reefer_monitoring table already has container_id pointing to cargo.container (cross']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Reefer monitoring operations (power consumption, technician callouts, alarm responses) generate costs allocated to a reefer operations cost centre. Port finance teams track reefer power and service co']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Reefer monitoring events are recorded within a specific terminal\'s reefer plug infrastructure. While terminal can be derived via yard_slot -> yard_block -> terminal, a direct terminal_id FK on reefer_']
  yard_slot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.yard_slot. Business justification: reefer_monitoring has yard_slot_position (STRING) which should be normalized to FK for authoritative slot reference. Monitoring events are tied to physical locations where reefer plugs are available. ']
  actual_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'The measured actual temperature inside the reefer container at the time of monitoring in degrees Celsius. Critical for cold chain integrity verification.']
  alarm_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether an alarm condition was triggered during this monitoring event. True indicates an alarm was raised, False indicates normal operation.']
  alarm_severity varchar [note: 'The severity level of the alarm condition. Critical requires immediate action, high requires urgent attention, medium requires scheduled intervention, low is informational.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'The category of alarm condition detected. Temperature deviation indicates actual temperature outside acceptable range, power failure indicates loss of electrical supply, door open indicates container door unsealed, humidity deviation indicates moisture level out of range, CO2 deviation indicates gas concentration issue, equipment malfunction indicates refrigeration unit failure.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'The classification of perishable cargo being transported in the reefer container. Examples include frozen meat, fresh produce, pharmaceuticals, dairy products, flowers.']
  co2_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'The carbon dioxide concentration measured inside the reefer container in percentage. Critical for controlled atmosphere cargo such as fruits and vegetables.']
  cold_chain_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event confirms cold chain integrity compliance. True indicates temperature and conditions within acceptable range, False indicates breach of cold chain requirements.']
  compressor_runtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The cumulative operating hours of the refrigeration compressor since last reset. Used for maintenance scheduling and equipment lifecycle management.']
  compressor_status varchar [note: 'The operational status of the refrigeration compressor unit. Running indicates active cooling, stopped indicates unit off, standby indicates ready but not cooling, fault indicates malfunction.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Free text description of the corrective action taken or required in response to this monitoring event. Populated when intervention was necessary.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event requires corrective action or intervention. True indicates follow-up action needed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this monitoring record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  defrost_cycle_status varchar [note: 'The status of the automatic defrost cycle for the refrigeration unit. Active indicates defrost in progress, inactive indicates normal cooling mode, scheduled indicates defrost pending.']
  door_status varchar [note: 'The physical status of the reefer container door. Closed indicates door shut but not sealed, open indicates door ajar or open, sealed indicates door closed with customs or security seal intact.']
  humidity_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'The relative humidity percentage measured inside the reefer container. Important for cargo types sensitive to moisture levels such as fresh produce.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this monitoring record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  monitoring_source varchar [note: 'The method or system that captured this monitoring reading. Automated sensor indicates TOS integration, manual inspection indicates technician reading, remote telemetry indicates IoT device, RFID reader indicates tag-based monitoring.']
  monitoring_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this monitoring event. Active indicates ongoing monitoring, resolved indicates issue closed, escalated indicates elevated to management, pending review indicates awaiting technician assessment.']
  monitoring_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reefer container monitoring reading was captured. Principal business event timestamp for this monitoring transaction.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether an alert notification was sent to stakeholders for this monitoring event. True indicates notification dispatched to customer or operations team.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when alert notification was sent to stakeholders regarding this monitoring event. Null if no notification was sent.']
  o2_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'The oxygen concentration measured inside the reefer container in percentage. Used for controlled atmosphere monitoring to preserve perishable cargo.']
  power_status varchar [note: 'The electrical power connection status of the reefer container. Connected indicates active power supply, disconnected indicates no power, standby indicates backup power mode.']
  power_supply_voltage decimal(6,2) [note: 'The electrical voltage being supplied to the reefer container in volts. Standard voltages are 220V, 380V, or 440V depending on terminal infrastructure.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free text field for additional notes, observations, or comments related to this reefer monitoring event. Used by technicians and operations staff for context.']
  sensor_device_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the monitoring sensor or telemetry device that captured this reading. Used for device calibration tracking and data quality assurance.']
  set_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target temperature setting configured for the reefer container in degrees Celsius. This is the temperature the refrigeration unit is programmed to maintain.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this monitoring event represents a breach of customer service level agreement for reefer monitoring. True indicates SLA breach occurred.']
  technician_inspection_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier linking this monitoring event to a technician inspection work order or maintenance record. Populated when manual intervention occurred.']
  temperature_deviation decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated difference between set temperature and actual temperature in degrees. Positive values indicate warmer than set point, negative values indicate cooler than set point.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for temperature readings. Standard is Celsius but Fahrenheit may be used in some jurisdictions.']
  ventilation_rate decimal(6,2) [note: 'The air exchange rate in cubic meters per hour when ventilation is active. Applicable for cargo requiring fresh air circulation.']
  ventilation_setting varchar [note: 'The ventilation mode configured for the reefer container. Closed for frozen cargo, open for fresh air exchange, controlled for modified atmosphere.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reefer container temperature and condition monitoring events within the terminal yard. Captures container number, yard slot position, set temperature, actual temperature reading, humidity reading, CO2 level, ventilation setting, monitoring timestamp, alarm flag, alarm type (temperature deviation, power failure, door open), and technician inspection reference. Ensures cold chain integrity for perishable cargo.'
}

Business justification: Equipment maintenance standards, certification requirements, operational specifications, and resource planning all require master equipment type reference. Removes denormalized equipment_type, manufac']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Terminal equipment (cranes, RTGs, reach stackers, forklifts) are capitalized fixed assets requiring integration between operational equipment master and financial asset register for depreciation, net ']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Equipment major overhauls and refurbishment programs are tracked against internal orders for CAPEX project cost control. Port asset managers use internal orders to accumulate crane and RTG maintenance']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Terminal handling equipment (RTG, STS, ASC, QC, MHC) is owned and operated by a specific terminal. The home_terminal attribute exists as a STRING in terminal_equipment — this is a denormalized referen']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Equipment is assigned to specific terminal zones for operational dispatch, maintenance planning, and resource allocation. The existing \'home_terminal\' text field is insufficient; equipment dispatch sy']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Equipment home location and current zone assignment required for security patrol verification, zone access control, and asset tracking. Enables security personnel to verify equipment authorization in ']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the equipment was acquired by the port authority, either through purchase, lease commencement, or transfer. Used for asset accounting, depreciation start date, and fleet age analysis.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation for equipment operation. Manual requires on-board operator, semi_automated provides operator assistance systems, fully_automated operates without human intervention (e.g., ASC, AGV), remote_controlled operated from remote control station. Critical for operational planning, safety protocols, and workforce requirements.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the equipments current safety certification or inspection certificate. Equipment must not operate beyond this date without recertification per port safety regulations and SOLAS requirements.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the equipment was commissioned and entered active operational service at the terminal. May differ from acquisition date due to installation, testing, and certification periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  current_yard_zone varchar [note: 'Current yard zone or operational area where the equipment is assigned or located. Used for dispatch optimization, equipment utilization tracking, and operational planning. Typically references yard block identifiers from terminal layout.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used for calculating depreciation of the equipment asset. Used for financial reporting, tax compliance, and asset valuation per accounting standards.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'Environmental emission standard that the equipment complies with (e.g., EPA Tier 4, Euro Stage V). Used for environmental reporting, regulatory compliance, and green port initiatives. Critical for GHG emissions tracking and air quality management.']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the terminal handling equipment unit. Used for operational dispatch, maintenance tracking, and cross-system reference. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by terminal operations or manufacturer.']
  fuel_power_type varchar [note: 'Primary energy source or propulsion type for the equipment. Critical for environmental reporting (GHG emissions), operational cost tracking, refueling/recharging infrastructure planning, and compliance with port environmental regulations.']
  gps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is equipped with active GPS tracking capability for real-time location monitoring. Used for fleet management, utilization analysis, and operational optimization.']
  home_terminal varchar [note: 'Primary terminal or facility where the equipment is permanently assigned. Used for multi-terminal port operations to track equipment allocation and inter-terminal transfers.']
  imdg_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is certified and equipped for handling dangerous goods containers per IMDG Code requirements. Includes specialized safety features, operator training requirements, and compliance documentation.']
  insurance_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the insurance policy covering this equipment. Used for risk management, claims processing, and asset protection. Links to Protection and Indemnity (P&I) or equipment insurance policies.']
  last_major_overhaul_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major overhaul or refurbishment of the equipment. Used for maintenance planning, remaining useful life estimation, and capital expenditure tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment master record was last modified. Used for data currency verification, change tracking, and audit trail maintenance.']
  maximo_asset_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier linking this terminal equipment record to the corresponding asset record in Maximo Asset Management system. Enables integration between operational equipment master (TOS) and full asset lifecycle management (EAM) for maintenance, work orders, and asset financial tracking.']
  maximum_lift_height_metres decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vertical lift height of the equipment in metres. Applicable to cranes and lifting equipment, indicates the maximum container stack height or vessel reach capability. Critical for operational planning and safety.']
  maximum_reach_metres decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum horizontal reach or span of the equipment in metres. Applicable to cranes (STS, QC, MHC, RTG, ASC) and indicates the operational envelope for cargo handling. Used for berth planning and yard layout optimization.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for the equipment. Used for maintenance scheduling, resource planning, and operational availability forecasting.']
  noise_level_db decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured operational noise level of the equipment in decibels. Used for occupational health and safety compliance, environmental impact assessment, and noise pollution management per port environmental regulations.']
  operating_hours_since_last_service decimal(10,2) [note: 'Operating hours accumulated since the last scheduled maintenance service. Used to trigger interval-based preventive maintenance activities.']
  operating_hours_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total operating hours recorded for the equipment since commissioning. Used for usage-based maintenance scheduling, depreciation calculations, and equipment lifecycle analysis.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the terminal handling equipment. Active indicates available for dispatch, under_maintenance indicates scheduled or unscheduled maintenance in progress, standby indicates operational but not currently assigned, decommissioned indicates permanently retired from service, out_of_service indicates temporarily unavailable, awaiting_repair indicates breakdown pending repair.']
  operator_certification_required varchar [note: 'Type or level of operator certification required to operate this equipment. References specific training programs, licensing requirements, or competency standards per port safety regulations and Maritime Labour Convention.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership or control arrangement for the equipment. Owned indicates port authority owns the asset, leased indicates long-term lease arrangement, rented indicates short-term rental, contractor_provided indicates equipment supplied by third-party service contractor.']
  reefer_monitoring_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is equipped with systems to monitor refrigerated container (reefer) parameters during handling or storage. Applicable to yard equipment and critical for cold chain integrity.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, operational constraints, or historical information about the equipment. Used for operational knowledge capture and equipment-specific context.']
  replacement_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current estimated replacement cost for the equipment in base currency. Used for insurance valuation, capital planning, and asset management decisions. Updated periodically to reflect market conditions.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the RFID tag attached to the equipment for automated tracking and identification within the terminal. Used for real-time location tracking, automated gate operations, and equipment utilization monitoring.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the equipment unit. Used for warranty claims, recall tracking, and asset provenance verification.']
  spreader_type varchar [note: 'Type of spreader attachment fitted to the equipment for container handling. Applicable to cranes and reach stackers. Fixed spreaders handle single container size, telescopic spreaders adjust between 20ft and 40ft containers, tandem spreaders handle two containers simultaneously. Critical for operational flexibility and productivity.']
  swl_tonnes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum safe working load capacity of the equipment expressed in metric tonnes. Critical safety parameter for operational dispatch and cargo handling planning. Must not be exceeded during operations per SOLAS and port safety regulations.']
  tandem_lift_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is capable of lifting two Forty-foot Equivalent Unit (FEU) containers simultaneously. Applicable to high-capacity cranes and represents advanced productivity capability.']
  telematics_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the telematics system or IoT device installed on the equipment for remote monitoring of performance, diagnostics, and operational parameters. Used for predictive maintenance and real-time equipment health monitoring.']
  twin_lift_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment is capable of lifting two Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) containers simultaneously. Applicable to cranes and significantly impacts productivity for vessel and yard operations.']
  year_of_manufacture varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the equipment was manufactured. Used for age-based maintenance planning, depreciation calculations, and lifecycle management decisions.']

  Note: 'Master data for all terminal handling equipment (THE) units operated within the terminal. Captures equipment number, equipment type (RTG, STS/QC, ASC, MHC, AGV, reach stacker, empty handler, forklift, terminal tractor), make, model, year of manufacture, SWL (Safe Working Load), operational status (active, under maintenance, standby, decommissioned), current yard zone assignment, fuel/power type, and Maximo asset reference. SSOT for terminal equipment identity within the terminal domain (operational profile; full asset lifecycle owned by asset domain).'
}

  appointment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vehicles must have a pre-scheduled appointment to use this lane.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation implemented in the gate lane for processing and verification activities.']

  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this gate lane configuration will cease to be active, null if currently active with no planned end date.']

  lane_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gate lane based on its operational purpose and traffic flow direction.']
  lane_width_meters decimal(5,2) [note: 'Physical width of the gate lane measured in meters, determining the size of vehicles that can be accommodated.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance or inspection was performed on the lane equipment and infrastructure.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate lane record was most recently modified in the system.']
  maximum_vehicle_height_meters decimal(4,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted height for vehicles using this lane, typically constrained by overhead structures or equipment.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the next planned maintenance or inspection is scheduled for the lane.']
  ocr_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is equipped with OCR cameras for automated license plate and container number recognition.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time for lane operations in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time for lane operations in HH:MM format (24-hour clock).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the gate lane indicating availability for processing traffic.']
  priority_lane boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is designated for priority or expedited processing of pre-approved or trusted carrier vehicles.']
  processing_capacity_per_hour varchar [note: 'Designed throughput capacity of the lane measured in number of vehicle transactions that can be processed per hour under normal operating conditions.']
  radiation_scanner_equipped boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane has radiation detection equipment for security screening of containers.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the gate lane configuration or operations.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is equipped with RFID readers for automated container and vehicle identification.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the security screening intensity applied at this lane.']
  tos_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is integrated with the terminal operating system for real-time transaction processing and data exchange.']
  traffic_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the permitted direction of vehicle traffic flow through the lane.']
  twenty_four_hour_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane operates continuously on a 24/7 basis.']
  vehicle_category varchar [note: 'Type of vehicles permitted to use this gate lane based on cargo status or vehicle configuration.']
  weighbridge_equipped boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane has an integrated weighbridge for vehicle and cargo weight verification.']
  xray_scanner_equipped boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane has X-ray scanning equipment for non-intrusive cargo inspection.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for gate_lane. Referenced by gate_lane_id.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_terminal {
  terminal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for terminal']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each terminal operates as or under a legal entity mapped to a company code for statutory financial reporting, intercompany transactions, and group consolidation.
