  number_of_cranes varchar [note: 'Total count of operational cranes available at the facility for cargo handling operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the facility (e.g., 24/7, business hours, shift-based).']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of facility ownership structure indicating whether it is publicly owned, privately operated, or under concession.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the facility address.']
  reefer_points_count varchar [note: 'Number of electrical connection points available for refrigerated containers at the facility.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the facility operations or characteristics.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the facility is located.']
  storage_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total covered or open storage area available at the facility, measured in square meters. Applicable to warehouses and yards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility record was last modified in the system.']
  utilization_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization rate of the facility expressed as a percentage of total capacity. Used for capacity planning and expansion analysis.']
  water_depth_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maintained water depth alongside the berth or facility measured in meters below chart datum. Determines maximum vessel draft.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for facility. Referenced by facility_id.'
}

Table infrastructure.port {
  port_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for port']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Port-level shared services (pilotage, VTS, port authority overhead) are tracked against a port-level cost centre. Port authority financial reporting and overhead allocation to terminals require this d']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Port country is required for sanctions screening, PSC targeting factor application, MARPOL jurisdiction, and customs regulatory framework determination. country_code is a denormalized ISO code. Linkin']
  parent_port_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on port (parent_port_id)']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: In multi-port operators, each port is a top-level profit centre for group P&L consolidation and port-level revenue reporting. Port dues, pilotage fees, and ancillary revenues are aggregated at port pr']
  un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: Ports are universally identified by UN/LOCODE in international trade, EDI messages (EDIFACT IFTMCS, COPRAR), customs declarations, and shipping manifests. port_code is a direct denormalization of un_l']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the port administrative office or main entrance.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building name, suite number, or additional location details.']
  annual_cargo_tonnage varchar [note: 'Total annual cargo throughput measured in metric tons, including containerized and bulk cargo.']
  annual_throughput_teu varchar [note: 'Annual container handling capacity or actual throughput measured in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units).']
  bunkering_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port provides bunkering (fuel supply) services for vessels.']
  channel_depth_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maintained depth of the main navigation channel providing access to the port, measured in meters.']
  city varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality where the port is located.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for port inquiries, vessel scheduling, and operational communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the port authority or operations center.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port record was first created in the system.']
  customs_facility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port has on-site customs clearance facilities for import/export processing.']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Type of environmental certification held by the port, such as EcoPort, Green Marine, or ISO 14001.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the port was officially established or began operations.']
  free_trade_zone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port operates a designated free trade zone or foreign trade zone for duty-deferred cargo.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port is certified to handle hazardous materials and dangerous goods according to IMDG Code.']
  iso_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port holds ISO certification for quality management, environmental management, or other standards.']
  isps_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port is compliant with the ISPS Code for maritime security.']
  last_dredging_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent dredging operation to maintain channel and berth depths.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the port in decimal degrees, used for navigation and vessel routing.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the port in decimal degrees, used for navigation and vessel routing.']
  max_vessel_beam_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum beam width of vessels that can be accommodated at the port, measured in meters.']
  max_vessel_draft_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum draft depth of vessels that can safely enter and berth at the port, measured in meters below waterline.']
  max_vessel_length_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum length of vessel that can be accommodated at the port, measured in meters.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port record was last modified or updated.']
  number_of_berths varchar [note: 'Total count of berths available at the port for vessel mooring and cargo operations.']
  number_of_cranes varchar [note: 'Total count of cargo handling cranes including ship-to-shore (STS), gantry, and mobile cranes available at the port.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the port, indicating whether it operates 24/7 or has specific operational windows.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the port facility indicating its availability for vessel operations and cargo handling.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership structure of the port facility indicating whether it is publicly owned, privately operated, or a hybrid model.']
  pilot_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maritime pilot services are mandatory for vessels entering or leaving the port.']
  port_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the port facility as recognized by maritime authorities and international shipping organizations.']
  port_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the port based on its primary operational function and geographic location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the port location used for mail delivery and geographic identification.']
  rail_connected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port has direct rail connectivity for intermodal cargo transfer.']
  reefer_connections varchar [note: 'Number of electrical connection points available for refrigerated containers requiring temperature control.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state/province within the country where the port is situated.']
  road_connected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port has direct road access for truck-based cargo transfer.']
  ship_repair_facilities boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port has ship repair and maintenance facilities including dry docks or floating docks.']
  storage_capacity_sqm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total covered and open storage area available for cargo, measured in square meters.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the port location used for scheduling vessel arrivals, departures, and operational planning.']
  total_area_sqm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total land area of the port facility measured in square meters, including terminals, warehouses, and operational zones.']
  total_quay_length_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Combined length of all quay walls and wharves measured in meters, indicating the port\'s vessel accommodation capacity.']
  tug_services_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tugboat services are available at the port for vessel maneuvering and berthing assistance.']
  water_area_sqm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total water area within port jurisdiction measured in square meters, including berths, anchorage zones, and navigational channels.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the port authority providing information on services, tariffs, and operations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for port. Referenced by port_id.'
}

Table intermodal.rail_visit {
  rail_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rail train visit to the port terminal. Primary key for the rail visit record. Represents the rail equivalent of a vessel call — the unit of rail service delivery.']
  call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Rail visits are scheduled to coincide with specific vessel call bookings for container interchange planning. Port planners correlate rail arrivals with call bookings for yard capacity, berth schedulin']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Rail terminal operations are cost centers; rail visit costs (track usage, handling, labor) must be captured against responsible cost center for operational cost accounting, variance analysis, and term']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Demurrage accrues when containers arriving by rail exceed free time at the terminal. The demurrage schedule applicable to a rail visit determines free time days and escalating rates — required for dem']
  handling_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.handling_order. Business justification: A rail visit at the port terminal is coordinated with a terminal handling order governing the loading/unloading of containers between rail wagons and the terminal yard. Port planners use this link for']
  import_export_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.import_export_permit. Business justification: Rail visits transporting controlled or restricted goods require import/export permits. Port and customs authorities verify permit validity before authorising rail departure or arrival, making this a m']
  manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.cargo_manifest. Business justification: Rail visits must reference the cargo manifest for customs declaration at rail-port interchange, dangerous goods compliance verification, and cargo inventory reconciliation between rail operator and te']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Rail visits are billed to a customer participant_account for THC, rail handling charges, and SLA measurement. Port billing systems require a direct account reference on the rail visit to generate invo']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.call. Business justification: Rail visits are operationally planned against specific vessel calls — port planners coordinate rail arrival/departure windows with vessel berthing schedules to ensure containers discharged from a spec']
  port_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port_gate. Business justification: Rail trains enter/exit through designated port gates. Gate assignment for rail visits is required for ISPS access control compliance, gate throughput KPI reporting, and gate scheduling — a named opera']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Rail visits occur at specific terminal track locations. Yard planning, resource allocation, and track assignment operations require proper location reference for infrastructure management and operatio']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Rail visits generate port revenue (rail handling charges, THC) that must be attributed to a profit centre for P&L reporting by business unit. Port finance P&L reporting by intermodal rail operations r']
  rail_operator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rail carrier or operator providing the train service. Links to the port community participant master data.']
  rail_terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Rail visits operate within dedicated rail-served terminal zones, enabling rail capacity planning, track allocation, and intermodal transfer coordination - rail operations planning process. Role-prefix']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Rail visits for contracted operators use negotiated rate cards for terminal services (loading, unloading, track usage). Essential for rail service billing and operator invoicing.']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.intermodal_service. Business justification: Rail visits are instances of intermodal rail services. The service defines the scheduled route and frequency; the visit is the actual train arrival/departure. No columns removed because visit has exec']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: A rail visit occurs at a specific terminal — terminal-level rail throughput reporting, billing of rail handling charges, and terminal capacity planning for rail operations all require rail visits to b']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: THC is charged per container loaded/discharged during a rail visit. The THC schedule governs terminal handling charges for rail-originated container moves — essential for billing reconciliation and TH']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Rail operations occur in designated secure rail terminal zones requiring zone-specific access control, hazmat screening protocols, and MARSEC-level security measures - intermodal facility security pla']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the train physically arrived at the port terminal rail facility. Recorded by terminal operations for performance measurement and billing.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the train departed from the port terminal rail facility. Recorded for performance measurement, billing, and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  appointment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the truck appointment system booking if this rail visit is part of an integrated intermodal appointment. Links rail and truck operations.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of billing and invoicing for this rail visit. Tracks the financial lifecycle from charge calculation through payment.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rail visit was marked as completed, indicating all loading/unloading operations finished and the train departed. Used for performance measurement and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  container_count_discharged varchar [note: 'Total number of individual containers unloaded from the train during this visit, regardless of size. Used for operational tracking and billing.']
  container_count_loaded varchar [note: 'Total number of individual containers loaded onto the train during this visit, regardless of size. Used for operational tracking and billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rail visit record was first created in the Terminal Operating System (TOS). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Geographic destination point or Inland Container Depot (ICD) to which the train will proceed after departing the terminal. Used for supply chain visibility and intermodal coordination.']
  edi_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport) or other EDI message that initiated or updated this rail visit. Used for audit trail and system integration.']
  empty_wagon_count varchar [note: 'Number of empty wagons in the train consist. Used for capacity planning and outbound loading operations.']
  estimated_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Current estimated arrival time based on real-time tracking and updates from the rail operator. Updated as the train progresses toward the terminal.']
  estimated_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Current estimated departure time based on operational progress and remaining work. Updated throughout the visit as operations proceed.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the train is carrying any hazardous materials requiring special handling per International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code. Triggers safety protocols and regulatory compliance procedures.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rail visit record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  loaded_wagon_count varchar [note: 'Number of wagons carrying containers or cargo upon arrival. Used for workload estimation and equipment planning.']
  operator_reference_number varchar [note: 'Rail operator\'s internal reference or booking number for this train visit. Used for reconciliation and communication with the rail carrier.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Geographic origin point or Inland Container Depot (ICD) from which the train departed. Used for supply chain visibility and intermodal coordination.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the rail visit. Determines sequencing and resource allocation for loading and unloading operations.']
  reefer_container_count varchar [note: 'Number of refrigerated containers on the train requiring power connection and temperature monitoring. Used for resource planning and power infrastructure allocation.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this rail visit. Used for communication between terminal staff and rail operators.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the train is scheduled to arrive at the port terminal rail facility. Used for resource planning and berth allocation.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the train is scheduled to depart from the port terminal rail facility after completing loading/unloading operations.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of rail service being provided. Unit train serves single customer/destination, manifest train carries mixed cargo, block train moves pre-assembled groups, local service provides switching.']
  teu_capacity varchar [note: 'Total container capacity of the train expressed in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU). Standard measure for rail capacity planning and performance reporting.']
  teu_discharged varchar [note: 'Actual number of TEU unloaded from the train during this visit. Used for productivity measurement and billing calculations.']
  teu_loaded varchar [note: 'Actual number of TEU loaded on the train for this visit. Used for productivity measurement and billing calculations.']
  track_assignment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rail track or siding where the train is positioned for loading and unloading operations. Critical for yard management and equipment dispatch.']
  train_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the train by the rail operator. Used for tracking the physical train consist throughout its journey.']
  train_length_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total physical length of the train consist in meters. Used for track capacity planning and safety compliance.']
  train_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the train including wagons and cargo in metric tonnes. Used for infrastructure load management and safety compliance.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the rail visit. Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the Terminal Operating System (TOS) for tracking and communication purposes.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rail visit. Tracks the visit through scheduling, arrival, operations, and departure phases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|arrived|in_progress|departed|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rail visit based on operational purpose. Inbound brings containers to port, outbound removes containers, interchange involves both, shuttle provides regular scheduled service.']
  wagon_count varchar [note: 'Total number of rail wagons (railcars) in the train consist. Used for capacity planning and operational resource allocation.']

  Note: 'Master record for each rail train visit to the port terminal, capturing train identification, operator reference, scheduled and actual arrival/departure times, track assignment, wagon count, TEU capacity, and visit status. Represents the rail equivalent of a vessel call — the unit of rail service delivery. SSOT for rail call lifecycle. Sourced from TOS Rail Operations module.'
}

Table intermodal.rail_wagon {
  rail_wagon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the rail wagon record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Rail wagons require periodic compliance audits covering safety certifications, brake systems, and hazmat handling capability. Port and rail operators must track the most recent compliance audit for ea']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Rail wagon maintenance, inspection, and operational costs must be attributed to a cost centre for port cost accounting and budget control. Port finance teams report wagon fleet operating costs by cost']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Rail wagons are capital assets tracked in the fixed asset register for depreciation, insurance, and residual value reporting. rail_wagon already carries acquisition_cost, residual_value, and insurance']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Asset tracking requires knowing physical wagon location within terminal. Critical for inventory management, dispatch planning, and wagon availability queries. Replaces denormalized current_location te']
  rail_operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: A rail wagon is owned or operated by a specific rail operator. rail_wagon currently stores operator_name as a denormalized STRING, which creates data quality risks (inconsistent naming, no referential']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original purchase or acquisition cost of the rail wagon in the base currency, used for asset valuation and depreciation.']
  axle_count varchar [note: 'The number of axles on the rail wagon. Affects weight distribution, track wear, and operational restrictions.']
  bogie_type varchar [note: 'The type or model of bogie (wheel assembly) fitted to the wagon, affecting ride quality, speed capability, and maintenance requirements.']
  brake_system varchar [note: 'The type of braking system installed on the wagon, critical for train composition and safety compliance.']
  build_date date [note: 'The specific date the rail wagon was completed and released from the manufacturing facility.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the wagon\'s operational certification expires and requires renewal.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'The date the rail wagon was first placed into operational service.']
  container_capacity_teu decimal(4,1) [note: 'The maximum container capacity of the wagon expressed in TEU. For example, a wagon that can carry two 20-foot containers or one 40-foot container would have a capacity of 2.0 TEU.']
  coupling_type varchar [note: 'The type of coupling mechanism used to connect the wagon to other rail vehicles, affecting interoperability with different rail systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rail wagon record was first created in the system.']
  deck_height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'The height of the loading deck from rail level in meters. Critical for container loading operations and determining stacking feasibility.']
  double_stack_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon design allows for double-stacking of containers, typically applicable to well wagons.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon is configured for EDI messaging integration with inland logistics partners and terminal operating systems.']
  gps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon is equipped with GPS tracking capability for real-time location monitoring.']
  gross_weight_limit_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum total weight (tare weight plus payload) in kilograms that the wagon is permitted to operate at, considering track and bridge load limits.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon is certified to transport hazardous materials according to IMDG Code standards.']
  height_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'The height of the rail wagon in meters from rail level to the highest point, used for clearance calculations and tunnel/bridge compatibility.']
  home_depot_location varchar [note: 'The primary rail depot or yard where the wagon is based for maintenance and operational purposes.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the current insurance policy for the wagon expires and requires renewal.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number of the insurance coverage for the rail wagon, used for claims and risk management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent safety or maintenance inspection performed on the wagon.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent maintenance activity performed on the wagon.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rail wagon record was most recently modified in the system.']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the current lease agreement for the wagon expires, if applicable.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon is owned outright by the operator, leased from a third party, or chartered for specific operations.']
  length_overall_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total length of the rail wagon in meters from end to end, used for train composition planning and yard space allocation.']
  manufacture_year varchar [note: 'The year the rail wagon was manufactured, used for age-based maintenance planning and depreciation calculations.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the company that manufactured the rail wagon.']
  maximum_payload_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum cargo weight in kilograms that the rail wagon is certified to carry safely. Critical for load planning and regulatory compliance.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next mandatory inspection of the rail wagon.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or historical information about the wagon.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rail wagon indicating its availability for intermodal transport operations.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the organization or entity that owns the rail wagon. May be a rail operator, leasing company, or port authority.']
  refrigeration_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wagon is equipped to carry refrigerated containers with power supply connections.']
  registration_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory body or national authority that registered and certified the rail wagon for operation.']
  registration_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the country where the wagon is registered.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'The official registration number issued by the registration authority, distinct from the wagon number and used for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  residual_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated residual or salvage value of the wagon at the end of its useful life, used for depreciation calculations.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the RFID tag attached to the wagon for automated tracking and identification in rail yards and terminals.']
  swl_rating_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The Safe Working Load rating in kilograms, representing the maximum load the wagon can handle under normal operating conditions including dynamic forces during transport.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The empty weight of the rail wagon in kilograms, excluding any cargo or containers. Used for load planning and compliance with weight restrictions.']
  wagon_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identification number assigned to the rail wagon by the owner or registration authority. This is the business identifier used in operational systems and documentation.']
  wagon_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rail wagon based on its structural design and intended cargo type. Flat wagons have a flat deck, well wagons have a recessed center for double-stacking containers, spine wagons have a central spine for container placement.']
  width_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'The width of the rail wagon in meters, important for clearance verification and loading gauge compliance.']

  Note: 'Master registry of rail wagons used in intermodal transport, including wagon number, type (flat, well, spine), tare weight, maximum payload, SWL rating, owner/operator, registration authority, and operational status. Supports container-to-wagon assignment and load planning.'
}

  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle arrived at the gate or facility. Captured by gate automation systems.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vehicle departed from the gate or facility after completing the transaction.']
  appointment_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appointment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for booking lifecycle.']
  appointment_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appointment record was last modified. Tracks amendments and updates to the booking.']
  appointment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the appointment or booking, generated by the Truck Appointment System (TAS) or intermodal booking portal. Used for customer communication and tracking.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the appointment. Tracks progression from initial request through completion or cancellation.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Type of intermodal transaction being booked. Defines the nature of the container movement through the terminal gate or rail facility.']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the appointment was booked. Indicates the interface or method used to create the reservation.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. Supports operational analysis and customer service.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the appointment was cancelled. Null if the appointment was not cancelled.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the cargo in kilograms. Used for safety verification and equipment allocation.']
  confirmed_slot_end_time timestamp [note: 'End of the confirmed time window allocated by the terminal.']
  confirmed_slot_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start of the confirmed time window allocated by the terminal. May differ from requested time based on capacity availability.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers included in this appointment. Typically 1 for truck appointments, may be multiple for rail or barge.']
  container_size varchar [note: 'Length of the container in feet. Standard sizes are 20-foot, 40-foot, and 45-foot containers.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Driver\'s license or identification number. Used for security verification and access authorization.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the driver operating the vehicle. Required for security and access control purposes.']
  driver_phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the driver. Used for communication regarding appointment changes or gate issues.']
  gate_lane_number varchar [note: 'Specific gate lane assigned for the appointment. Used for traffic management and gate capacity optimization.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification code for hazardous cargo. Required when is_hazardous is true. Determines handling and storage requirements.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cargo contains hazardous or dangerous goods requiring special handling per IMDG Code.']
  is_oversized boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cargo has out-of-gauge dimensions requiring special handling or routing.']
  is_overweight boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container exceeds standard weight limits, requiring special handling or permits.']
  is_reefer boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container is a refrigerated (reefer) unit requiring temperature control and power connection.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the booking party failed to arrive for a confirmed appointment. Used for performance tracking and penalty assessment.']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setting for refrigerated containers in degrees Celsius. Critical for cargo integrity.']
  requested_slot_end_time timestamp [note: 'End of the requested time window for the appointment. Represents the latest time within the booking window.']
  requested_slot_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start of the requested time window for the appointment. Represents the earliest time the booking party wishes to arrive.']
  teu_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total TEU capacity represented by this appointment. Used for capacity planning and throughput measurement. One 40-foot container equals 2 TEU.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport for which the appointment is made. Determines the facility and handling procedures.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance. Required for dangerous goods shipments.']
  vehicle_registration_number varchar [note: 'License plate or registration number of the truck or vehicle. Used for gate identification and access control.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle or conveyance used for the appointment. Determines handling requirements and gate procedures.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for all intermodal capacity slot bookings including truck gate appointments, rail service reservations, and barge slot allocations, made through the Truck Appointment System (TAS), intermodal booking portal, or carrier EDI channels. Captures appointment/booking reference, slot time window, vehicle/train/barge identification, container reference, transaction type (import pickup, export delivery, empty return, rail-on, rail-off, transshipment), booking party (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner), container count, TEU quantity, requested transport date, confirmed slot, and booking status lifecycle (requested, confirmed, amended, cancelled, no-show, completed). SSOT for all intermodal capacity reservation and gate slot management across road, rail, and barge modes.'
}

Table intermodal.truck_visit {
  truck_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the truck gate transaction. Primary key for the truck visit record.']
  access_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_event. Business justification: truck_visit.gate_lane_number is a denormalized reference to the security access_point used during the visit. Replacing it with a proper FK enables ISPS audit trail per access point, gate throughput an']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: A truck visit physically handles a specific container at the gate. Gate transaction systems record which container was processed during each truck visit for security screening, customs verification, a']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Actual gate transactions verify customs clearance at point of entry/exit. Gate officers validate declaration status in real-time to enforce customs authority release requirements before allowing conta']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Gate transactions verify no active holds exist before allowing container exit. Gate officers check hold status in real-time as enforcement point to prevent release of detained cargo.']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.delivery_order.   gate_transaction_id varchar [note: 'FK to terminal.gate_transaction.gate_transaction_id — Truck lifecycle tracking from intermodal appointment through gate execution requires joining truck_visit to the terminal gate_transaction. Without this, truck turnaround time analytics are impossible.']
  haulier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the drayage or trucking company operating the vehicle. Used for performance tracking and billing of terminal handling charges.']
  port_gate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port_gate.   security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident.   weighing_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: SOLAS VGM compliance requires recording which weighing station processed each truck visit for export containers. This is a regulatory audit trail requirement — port authorities must demonstrate which ']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Gate transactions occur at specific security zones requiring zone-based access clearance, ISPS compliance checks, and MARSEC-level authorization - core port security process linking physical entry to ']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the truck physically arrived at the gate entry point, typically captured by automated gate sensors or manual gate operator entry. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  container_condition varchar [note: 'Status of the container at the time of gate transaction indicating whether it is loaded with cargo (full), empty, or has visible damage requiring inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this truck visit record was first created in the Terminal Operating System. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  damage_report_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether visible damage to the container or cargo was observed and documented during the gate transaction. Triggers damage inspection workflow.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued driver\'s license number presented at the gate for identity verification. Captured for security and compliance purposes.']
  driver_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the driver\'s identity at the gate, including biometric scan, RFID access card, manual ID document check, or facial recognition system.']
  gate_in_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the truck completed all gate-in processing and was authorized to enter the terminal yard. Represents the completion of inbound gate transaction.']
  gate_out_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the truck completed all gate-out processing and exited the terminal. Represents the completion of outbound gate transaction.']
  isps_compliance_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of the ISPS security compliance verification performed at the gate, indicating whether the driver and vehicle met all required security protocols for port facility access.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this truck visit record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  license_plate_number varchar [note: 'Vehicle registration plate number of the truck, typically captured via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) system at the gate. Used for vehicle identification and tracking.']
  ocr_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence percentage (0-100) of the automated OCR system\'s license plate read accuracy. Low scores may trigger manual verification by gate operators.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the gate transaction was rejected, including documentation issues, security failures, equipment problems, or system errors. Populated when transaction_status is rejected.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments recorded by the gate operator regarding special circumstances, exceptions, or observations during the transaction.']
  seal_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the container seal inspection at the gate, indicating whether the seal was intact and matched documentation (verified), was found broken, was missing, or was not applicable for this transaction type.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gate transaction indicating whether the visit has been successfully completed, is still in progress at the gate, was rejected due to compliance or documentation issues, was cancelled by the driver or terminal, or is awaiting additional inspection.']
  turnaround_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from actual arrival at the gate to final gate-out completion. Key performance indicator (KPI) for gate efficiency and truck dwell time measurement.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the truck visit transaction indicating whether the truck is entering the terminal (gate-in), exiting the terminal (gate-out), or performing both operations in a single visit (dual transaction for drop-and-pick scenarios).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each truck gate transaction at the port, capturing actual arrival time, gate-in/gate-out timestamps, lane assignment, OCR plate read, driver identity verification, ISPS compliance check result, turnaround time, and linked appointment reference. Represents the execution record against a truck appointment booking. SSOT for truck gate throughput measurement.'
}

  dangerous_goods_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.dangerous_goods_declaration.   masterdata_container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Drayage orders move specific container types requiring chassis assignment, handling equipment selection, and route planning based on equipment dimensions and weight limits.']
  origin_berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Drayage from vessel references berth for container pickup during discharge operations, enabling vessel-to-gate coordination and berth turnaround optimization - vessel discharge process.']

Stowage planners and shipping lines require this link t']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Drayage orders apply Terminal Handling Charge schedules for container trucking between port and inland locations. Essential for drayage billing and cost calculation in port logistics.']

  verified_gross_mass_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.verified_gross_mass.   weighing_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: Export drayage orders require SOLAS VGM verification at a port weighing station before container is accepted for vessel loading. Linking the drayage order to the assigned weighing station supports VGM']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the container was delivered to the destination location. Captured via gate system, customer confirmation, or proof of delivery.']
  actual_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the container was picked up from the origin location. Captured via gate system or manual confirmation.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for drayage order cancellation if status is CANCELLED. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  container_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the container at pickup. Documented to establish liability for any damage occurring during drayage movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the drayage order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for order lifecycle tracking.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the destination location for customer premises or off-port facilities. Required for last-mile delivery coordination.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of destination location for container delivery. CY = Container Yard, CFS = Container Freight Station, ICD = Inland Container Depot.']
  drayage_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique drayage order number issued to road hauliers for container movement instructions. Externally referenced identifier for tracking and communication.']
  drayage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the drayage order. Tracks progression from order creation through assignment, execution, and completion or cancellation.']
  drayage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the drayage movement type. IMPORT = laden container from port to customer, EXPORT = laden container from customer to port, EMPTY_RETURN = empty container return, REPOSITIONING = container relocation between port facilities.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for drayage order failure if status is FAILED (e.g., container not available, access denied, address incorrect). Used for exception management.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and routing per IMDG Code.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG hazard class for dangerous goods (e.g., Class 3 - Flammable Liquids). Required for hazmat drayage movements to ensure proper routing and handling.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the drayage order record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to order details or status.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the drayage order for dispatch sequencing and resource allocation. URGENT orders receive expedited handling.']
  origin_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the origin location for customer premises or off-port facilities. Required for last-mile delivery coordination.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific origin location (yard block, CFS zone, ICD facility code, or customer site code) where the container will be picked up.']
  origin_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of origin location for container pickup. CY = Container Yard, CFS = Container Freight Station, ICD = Inland Container Depot.']
  overweight_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container exceeds standard weight limits and requires special routing or permits for road transport.']
  pod_signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the container delivery at the destination. Captured for delivery verification and dispute resolution.']
  proof_of_delivery_received boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether proof of delivery documentation has been received from the haulier confirming successful container delivery.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when proof of delivery was received and validated. Used for billing trigger and service level agreement measurement.']
  reefer_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the container is a refrigerated unit requiring temperature-controlled transport and genset-equipped truck.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for container delivery to the destination location. Used for customer appointment scheduling and receiving dock planning.']
  scheduled_delivery_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the time window for container delivery. Defines the latest time the haulier may arrive for delivery without incurring penalties.']
  scheduled_delivery_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the time window for container delivery. Defines the earliest time the haulier may arrive for delivery.']
  scheduled_pickup_date date [note: 'Planned date for container pickup from the origin location. Used for truck appointment scheduling and yard planning.']
  scheduled_pickup_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the time window for container pickup. Defines the latest time the haulier may arrive for pickup without incurring penalties.']
  scheduled_pickup_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the time window for container pickup. Defines the earliest time the haulier may arrive for pickup.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number affixed to the container. Verified at pickup and delivery to ensure container integrity and prevent tampering.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements (e.g., fragile cargo, top-loader only, escort required). Communicated to haulier for execution.']
  temperature_setting_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature setting in Celsius for reefer containers. Haulier must maintain this temperature during drayage movement.']
  truck_license_plate varchar [note: 'License plate number of the truck assigned to perform the drayage movement. Used for gate access and tracking.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazmat drayage compliance and emergency response.']
  verified_gross_mass_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'SOLAS-compliant verified gross mass of the packed container in kilograms. Required for export containers and used for route planning and vehicle selection.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for container drayage instructions issued to road hauliers, capturing drayage order number, container reference, origin/destination (CY, CFS, ICD, customer premises), haulier assignment, scheduled pickup and delivery windows, drayage status, and proof-of-delivery confirmation. SSOT for last-mile container drayage coordination.'
}

Table intermodal.icd_facility {
  icd_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system identifier for the ICD or CFS facility record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: ICD facilities undergo mandatory compliance audits covering customs bonded warehouse certification, dangerous goods handling, and ISPS compliance. Port operators and customs authorities must track the']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each ICD facility operates as a distinct cost center; all facility operating expenses (rent, utilities, labor, maintenance) are accumulated against facility cost center for facility-level P&L analysis']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Country determines customs zone applicability, sanctions screening requirements, and trade agreement eligibility for ICD facilities. The plain attribute country_code is a denormalized country referenc']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: ICD facilities have resident customs brokers for bonded operations. Inland container depots track broker assignments to provide clearance services and manage customs-bonded storage for import/export c']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Containers dwelling at ICD facilities beyond free time attract demurrage. The demurrage schedule applicable to the ICD governs free time and escalating rates — required for demurrage billing configura']
  detention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: ICD facilities are key detention accrual points — containers held at ICD beyond free time attract detention charges. The detention schedule governs applicable rates — required for detention billing co']
  facility_security_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.facility_security_plan. Business justification: Every ICD facility interfacing with port operations requires an approved ISPS-compliant facility security plan with PFSO designation, MARSEC procedures, and security assessment - ISPS Code regulatory ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: ICD facility infrastructure (warehouses, yards, equipment) must be registered in the fixed asset ledger for depreciation, insurance valuation, and CAPEX reporting. Port finance teams track facility as']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: An ICD facility is the inland extension of a specific port terminal. Linking ICD to its primary terminal supports terminal-ICD throughput reporting, drayage corridor planning, and commercial SLA manag']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: ICD facilities designated as port facilities under the ISPS Code must maintain an ISPS facility record (PFSP, security level, CSO details). Linking each ICD facility to its ISPS record is a direct SOL']
  operator_port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: ICD facilities are operated by port community participants (logistics operators, freight forwarders). Regulatory compliance, EDI connectivity setup, and commercial agreements require linking the facil']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: ICD facilities serve a specific port for inland cargo consolidation/deconsolidation. Port-level ICD network management, port authority licensing of ICDs, and port throughput statistics aggregation all']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: ICDs are physical locations within port infrastructure hierarchy. Spatial planning, distance calculations for drayage pricing, and facility master data management require proper location reference.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: ICD facilities generate revenue from storage, handling, customs services; each facility is a profit center for segment reporting, enabling management to assess facility-level profitability, investment']
  storage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.storage_tariff. Business justification: ICD facilities store containers and charge storage fees. The storage tariff governs the applicable daily storage rates, free storage days, and escalation tiers at the ICD — required for storage billin']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: ICD facilities perform container handling operations — THC applies to container moves at the ICD. The THC schedule governs applicable handling charges — required for THC billing configuration at inlan']
  un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: ICD facilities are internationally identified by UN/LOCODE in EDI messages (IFTMIN, COPRAR, BAPLIE) and customs declarations. Trade documentation and port community system routing depend on UN/LOCODE ']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date when the facility became operational and available for container handling in the port\'s intermodal network. Used for historical analysis and facility lifecycle tracking.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when the facility ceased operations or was decommissioned. Nullable for currently active facilities. Used for historical reporting and capacity planning.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the ICD or CFS facility, including building number and street name. Used for truck dispatch routing and delivery instructions.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as suite, building, or zone information. Optional field for complex facility addresses.']
  average_drayage_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average transit time for container drayage between the port and the ICD facility, measured in hours. Used for service level agreement (SLA) definition and operational planning.']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code used for billing and financial transactions with the facility operator (e.g., USD, EUR, INR). Used in invoice generation and revenue accounting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the ICD or CFS facility is located. Used for geographic routing and regional logistics planning.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current service agreement or partnership contract expires. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal planning and relationship management.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the service agreement or partnership contract between the port and the ICD operator became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management and billing period determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this facility record was first created in the master data system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  customs_bonded_facility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is authorized as a customs bonded warehouse, allowing storage of imported goods before duty payment. True if bonded status is active; False otherwise. Critical for trade compliance and duty deferral operations.']
  customs_license_number varchar [note: 'Official license or registration number issued by customs authorities for bonded warehouse operations. Required for facilities with customs_bonded_facility = True. Used in customs declarations and trade documentation.']
  dangerous_goods_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to handle IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) classified cargo. True if IMDG certification is active; False otherwise. Required for hazardous material storage and handling.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that is the authoritative source for this facility record (e.g., NAVIS N4, Port Community System, SAP ERP). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  distance_from_port_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Road distance from the main port terminal to the ICD facility measured in kilometers. Used for drayage cost calculation, transit time estimation, and last-mile delivery planning.']
  edi_connectivity_status varchar [note: 'Current status of EDI integration between the port\'s systems and the ICD facility. CONNECTED indicates active EDI messaging (COPARN, IFTMIN, BAPLIE), NOT_CONNECTED for manual processes, PENDING for integration in progress, SUSPENDED for temporarily disabled connections.']
  edi_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for EDI message exchange with the facility. EDIFACT for UN/EDIFACT standard, XML for web services, AS2 for secure file transfer, SFTP for batch file exchange, API for real-time integration.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code assigned to the ICD or CFS facility, used in operational systems and EDI messaging (COPARN, IFTMIN). Typically alphanumeric, 4-10 characters.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the ICD or CFS facility as recognized by port authorities and logistics partners.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inland facility: ICD (Inland Container Depot) for full container handling, CFS (Container Freight Station) for LCL consolidation/deconsolidation, or HYBRID for facilities offering both services.']
  fcl_service_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility provides FCL (Full Container Load) handling services. True if the facility can receive, store, and dispatch full containers; False otherwise.']
  imdg_license_number varchar [note: 'Official license or certification number for dangerous goods handling issued by maritime safety authorities. Required when dangerous_goods_certified = True. Used in hazmat compliance reporting.']
  isps_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility meets ISPS Code security requirements for port facilities. True if ISPS certification is current; False otherwise. Mandatory for facilities handling international cargo.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this facility record. Used for audit trails and data governance accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this facility record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees format. Used for GPS navigation, route optimization, and geospatial analytics. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000.']
  lcl_service_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility provides LCL (Less than Container Load) consolidation and deconsolidation services. True if the facility operates as a CFS for break-bulk cargo; False otherwise.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees format. Used for GPS navigation, route optimization, and geospatial analytics. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the facility in local time, typically formatted as day ranges and time ranges (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 08:00-18:00, Sat 08:00-14:00\'). Used for truck appointment scheduling and gate operations planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the facility in the port\'s intermodal network. ACTIVE indicates full operations, INACTIVE for temporary closure, SUSPENDED for regulatory or safety holds, UNDER_CONSTRUCTION for facilities being built, DECOMMISSIONED for permanently closed facilities.']
  operator_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for operational coordination and communication with the facility operator. Used for EDI notifications, booking confirmations, and service requests.']
  operator_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for operational coordination with the facility operator, including country code. Used for urgent operational matters and emergency contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the facility address. Used for mail delivery, geographic analysis, and logistics routing optimization.']
  rail_connectivity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has direct rail access for intermodal container transfer. True if rail siding or terminal connection exists; False for truck-only facilities. Critical for rail operations planning and modal shift strategies.']
  reefer_plug_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of electrical plug-in points available for refrigerated containers (reefers) requiring temperature control. Used for cold chain logistics planning and reefer container allocation.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Current ISPS security level of the facility. LEVEL_1 for normal operations, LEVEL_2 for heightened security risk, LEVEL_3 for imminent security threat. Determines access control and security protocols.']
  sla_turnaround_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual maximum turnaround time for container processing at the facility, measured in hours from gate-in to gate-out. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and performance measurement.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the facility is located. Used for regulatory compliance and regional operational planning.']
  storage_capacity_teu varchar [note: 'Maximum container storage capacity of the facility measured in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units). Used for capacity planning, yard management, and operational load balancing across the intermodal network.']
  truck_parking_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of trucks that can be accommodated simultaneously in the facility\'s parking and staging areas. Used for truck appointment system capacity planning and gate congestion management.']
  twenty_four_seven_operations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. True for round-the-clock operations; False for facilities with limited operating hours. Impacts truck appointment system configuration and service level agreements.']

  Note: 'Master record for Inland Container Depots (ICDs) and Container Freight Stations (CFS) linked to the port, capturing facility code, name, address, geographic coordinates, operator, storage capacity (TEU), available services (FCL, LCL, customs bonded), operating hours, and EDI connectivity status. SSOT for inland depot reference data.'
}

  transport_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transport order number issued to carrier or haulier. Externally visible identifier used in communications and documentation.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for dangerous goods. Null if cargo is non-hazardous.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing an intermodal transport instruction (IFTMIN message) issued to a carrier or haulier, capturing transport order number, IFTMIN reference, shipper, consignee, origin, destination, mode sequence (sea-rail-road), container reference, cargo description, HS code, required delivery date, and order status. SSOT for intermodal transport instruction lifecycle.'
}

  delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay time for this leg measured in minutes. Calculated as the difference between scheduled and actual times. Zero if no delay.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any delay in this transport leg (e.g., weather, equipment failure, congestion). Null if no delay occurred.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination location node. ICD = Inland Container Depot, CFS = Container Freight Station. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seaport|inland_port|rail_terminal|icd|cfs|distribution_center|warehouse — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  edi_message_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when EDI message (IFTMIN, COPARN) was sent to logistics partners for this leg. Used for integration tracking.']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated or planned cost for this transport leg in USD. Used for budgeting and cost analysis.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg involves transport of dangerous goods requiring special handling per IMDG Code.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification code for dangerous goods (e.g., Class 3 - Flammable Liquids). Null if hazmat_indicator is false.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this intermodal leg record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this transport leg within its lifecycle. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion.']
  origin_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the origin location node. ICD = Inland Container Depot, CFS = Container Freight Station. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seaport|inland_port|rail_terminal|icd|cfs|distribution_center|warehouse — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reefer_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this leg involves a refrigerated container requiring temperature-controlled transport.']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the transport leg is scheduled to arrive at the destination node. Used for planning and resource allocation.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the transport leg is scheduled to depart from the origin node. Used for planning and coordination.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this transport leg within the multimodal journey (e.g., 1 for first leg, 2 for second leg). Enables reconstruction of the complete journey path.']
  temperature_setpoint_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for refrigerated containers in degrees Celsius. Null for non-reefer containers.']
  teu_count decimal(4,2) [note: 'TEU equivalent for this leg (1.0 for 20ft, 2.0 for 40ft, 2.25 for 45ft). Used for capacity planning and volume reporting.']
  transit_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual transit time for this leg from departure to arrival measured in hours. Used for performance analysis and service level tracking.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for this leg of the journey. Distinguishes between maritime, rail, road, barge, air, and pipeline transport.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing a single transport leg within a multimodal journey, capturing leg sequence number, transport mode (sea, rail, road, barge), carrier, origin node, destination node, scheduled and actual departure/arrival, distance (km), and leg status. Enables end-to-end multimodal journey decomposition and tracking.'
}
