  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Each container loaded/unloaded on a rail wagon attracts THC. The THC schedule at wagon load level enables granular THC billing per container move — required for accurate THC charge calculation and bil']
  trade_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_document. Business justification: Each wagon load is governed by trade documents (rail waybill, packing list, certificate of origin). Port and customs staff must link wagon loads to their trade documents for cargo verification, custom']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: Rail wagon loads fulfill transport orders. Each container loaded onto a wagon is executing a transport order instruction. Linking allows retrieval of booking, BOL, and AWB from transport_order.']
  voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.voyage. Business justification: Rail wagon loads contain containers for specific vessel voyages. Rail operators and terminal planners need this link to coordinate rail arrival/departure timing with vessel schedules, prioritize wagon']
  actual_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when this container or cargo unit was physically loaded onto the wagon. Captured by TOS or gate systems. Used for performance measurement and billing.']
  consignee_reference varchar [note: 'Consignee-provided reference number for cargo receipt and delivery confirmation. Used for delivery coordination and proof of delivery documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rail wagon load record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customs_status varchar [note: 'Current customs clearance status for this load. Indicates whether cargo has been cleared for onward movement or is held pending customs inspection or documentation.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE identifying the final destination location for this cargo on the rail network. Used for routing, delivery planning, and customs clearance.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross weight of the loaded container or cargo unit in kilograms, including tare weight and cargo. Critical for rail wagon weight distribution compliance and safe working load (SWL) validation.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this load contains hazardous or dangerous goods requiring special handling and segregation per IMDG Code. True if hazardous, False otherwise.']
  is_oversized boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this load exceeds standard dimensional limits (height, width, length) for rail transport. True if oversized, False otherwise. Requires clearance validation and special routing.']
  is_overweight boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this load exceeds standard weight limits for the wagon type or rail infrastructure. True if overweight, False otherwise. Triggers special handling and route restrictions.']
  is_reefer boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a refrigerated container requiring power connection and temperature monitoring during rail transport. True if reefer, False otherwise.']
  lashing_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for securing and lashing the container or cargo unit to the wagon. Includes twist lock positions, chain requirements, and special securing equipment needed for safe rail transport.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rail wagon load record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  load_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Order in which this container or cargo unit was loaded onto the wagon. Supports operational tracking and unloading sequence planning.']
  load_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this wagon load. Tracks progression from planning through loading, transit, and final delivery. Used for operational visibility and exception management.']
  net_cargo_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net weight of the cargo inside the container in kilograms, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight. Used for billing, customs declarations, and cargo manifests.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE identifying the origin location where this cargo was loaded onto the rail network. Used for routing and customs documentation.']
  planned_load_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when this container or cargo unit is planned to be loaded onto the wagon. Used for resource planning and operational scheduling.']
  reefer_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target temperature setting for refrigerated containers in degrees Celsius. Mandatory when is_reefer is True. Used for monitoring and maintaining cold chain integrity during rail transport.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational comments related to this wagon load. Used for exception handling and operational communication.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique seal number applied to the container for security and tamper-evidence. Verified at loading and unloading to ensure cargo integrity. Required for customs and security compliance.']
  secured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the load was confirmed as properly secured and ready for rail departure. Represents completion of lashing and safety checks.']
  securing_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment used to secure the load to the wagon (e.g., twist locks for containers, chains for heavy cargo, straps for non-standard loads). Ensures proper securing method is applied.']
  shipper_reference varchar [note: 'Shipper-provided reference number for this cargo shipment. Used for customer communication and shipment tracking across systems.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating special handling requirements for this load (e.g., fragile, top load only, no stacking, temperature sensitive). Drives operational procedures and equipment selection.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the container or cargo unit in kilograms, excluding cargo. Used to calculate net cargo weight and validate weight declarations.']
  teu_count decimal(5,2) [note: 'TEU equivalent of the loaded container or cargo unit. Standard 20ft container = 1.0 TEU, 40ft container = 2.0 TEU. Used for capacity planning and utilization reporting.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit United Nations number identifying the specific dangerous substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazardous cargo and used for emergency response and regulatory compliance.']
  unload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when this container or cargo unit was unloaded from the wagon at the destination. Used for dwell time calculation and performance reporting.']
  wagon_position_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position number of this wagon within the rail consist or train formation. Used for load planning and operational sequencing.']

  Note: 'Association record linking containers or cargo units to specific rail wagons for a given rail visit, capturing wagon position, container ISO type, gross weight, hazardous goods flag (IMDG class), load sequence, and lashing/securing instructions. Supports rail load planning and weight distribution compliance.'
}

Table intermodal.haulier {
  haulier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the haulier record. Primary key for the intermodal transport carrier and operator registry.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Haulier country determines regulatory licensing authority, sanctions screening applicability, and cross-border transport permit requirements. The plain attribute office_country_code is a denormalized ']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Hauliers partner with designated customs brokers for clearance services. Transport operators track broker relationships to coordinate documentation and streamline customs processing for their drayage ']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique EDI partner identifier for the haulier used in electronic messaging and data exchange with port community systems, TOS, and PCS.']
  organizational_access_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_credential. Business justification: Haulier companies receive organizational credentials for fleet-wide port facility access, background check validation, and vendor security clearance - vendor security management and organizational acc']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Hauliers are billed through a specific participant_account for truck handling charges, gate fees, and drayage services. Port billing and credit management systems require a direct account reference on']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Hauliers are registered port community participants requiring unified participant management, accreditation tracking (ISPS compliance), EDI subscriptions, and regulatory oversight. Essential for port ']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code assigned to the haulier for operational reference and EDI messaging. Standard alphanumeric code used across port community systems.']
  commercial_terms_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the commercial terms, rate card, or pricing agreement governing the haulier\'s services with the port or terminal operator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the haulier record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit limit (in USD) extended to the haulier for services rendered before payment is required.']
  edi_message_formats varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of EDI message formats supported by the haulier (e.g., IFTMIN, COPARN, BAPLIE) for intermodal transport coordination.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: '24/7 emergency contact phone number for urgent operational issues, incidents, or safety concerns involving the haulier\'s operations.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicles, locomotives, or vessels in the haulier\'s operational fleet available for container transport.']
  haulier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the haulier in the port\'s carrier registry: active (approved and operational), inactive (not currently operating), suspended (temporarily barred), pending approval (under review), or terminated (permanently removed).']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transport service or container movement performed by the haulier.']
  licence_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the haulier\'s transport operator licence. Operations must cease if licence expires without renewal.']
  licensing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or government agency that issued the transport operator licence.']
  network_access_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the network access agreement or contract that grants the haulier operational access to port facilities, rail networks, or inland terminals.']
  office_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the haulier\'s registered office or headquarters street address.']
  office_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the haulier\'s registered office or headquarters street address (suite, building, floor).']
  office_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the haulier\'s registered office or headquarters is located.']
  office_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the haulier\'s registered office or headquarters address.']
  office_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the haulier\'s registered office or headquarters is located.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the haulier was first onboarded and registered in the port\'s intermodal carrier system.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the haulier operator type: road haulage company, owner-operator (independent), rail freight operator, barge service provider, or integrated logistics provider.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days for invoices issued to the haulier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance tier rating assigned to the haulier based on KPIs such as on-time delivery, safety record, and service quality: platinum (top tier), gold, silver, bronze, or unrated (new/insufficient data).']
  regulatory_licence_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued transport operator licence or permit number authorizing the haulier to conduct commercial freight operations.']
  service_corridors varchar [note: 'Geographic service corridors, routes, or regions covered by the haulier\'s transport network (e.g., port-to-ICD, regional shuttle, cross-border).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the haulier\'s registration or service agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for termination of the haulier\'s registration or service agreement (e.g., contract expiry, performance issues, regulatory non-compliance, business closure).']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transport mode capability of the haulier: road (truck/lorry), rail (freight train), barge (inland waterway), or multimodal (multiple modes).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the haulier record was last modified or updated.']
  vehicle_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vehicle, locomotive, or vessel types operated by the haulier (e.g., flatbed truck, chassis, container wagon, push barge).']

  Note: 'Master record for all intermodal transport carriers and operators including road haulage companies, owner-operators, rail freight operators, and barge service providers engaged in container drayage, last-mile delivery, rail shuttle services, and inland waterway transport. Captures carrier code, company name, registration number, transport mode capability (road/rail/barge/multimodal), fleet size, vehicle/locomotive types, network access agreement references, service corridors, regulatory licence number and authority, accreditation status, ISPS compliance certification, insurance coverage and expiry, performance tier rating, EDI partner ID, operational contact details, and commercial terms reference. SSOT for the unified intermodal transport partner, carrier, and operator registry — the single lookup for all carrier identity resolution within the intermodal domain.'
}

Table intermodal.rail_operator {
  rail_operator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rail freight operator. Primary key for the rail operator master record within the intermodal domain.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Rail operator country determines network access agreement jurisdiction, regulatory licensing authority, and sanctions screening requirements. The plain attribute registered_country_code is a denormali']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Rail operators partner with customs brokers for cross-border freight clearance. Intermodal service providers maintain broker relationships to facilitate customs processing at border crossings and inla']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique EDI partner identifier used for electronic messaging integration with port systems, enabling automated exchange of IFTMIN (transport instruction), COPARN (container pre-announcement), and other UN/EDIFACT messages.']
  facility_security_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.facility_security_plan. Business justification: Rail operators interfacing with port terminals must comply with facility security plans for ISPS interface requirements, security procedure alignment, and MARSEC-level coordination - intermodal securi']

Business justification: Rail operators are invoiced through a participant_account for rail terminal handling charges, track usage fees, and wagon handling. Port commercial management and AR processes require a direct billing']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.port_community_participant. Business justification: Rail operators are port community participants requiring accreditation, regulatory compliance (safety/environmental certifications), EDI partner registration, and unified participant registry manageme']
  container_handling_capability varchar [note: 'Type of container units the operator is equipped to handle, including TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), refrigerated containers, and IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) certified cargo.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rail operator record was first created in the system.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned by a recognized rating agency, indicating the financial stability and creditworthiness of the rail operator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|not_rated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  edi_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for EDI message exchange with the rail operator (e.g., UN/EDIFACT, XML, REST API, AS2, SFTP).']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: '24/7 emergency contact telephone number for urgent operational issues, incidents, or safety events.']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of environmental certifications held by the rail operator (e.g., ISO 14001, Green Freight certification).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rail operator record was last updated or modified.']
  licence_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory licence expires and must be renewed for continued operations.']
  locomotive_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of locomotive types operated by the carrier (e.g., diesel, electric, hybrid), indicating traction capability and environmental profile.']
  max_gross_weight_tonnes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum gross train weight in tonnes that the operator is authorized and equipped to haul, based on locomotive capacity and track load limits.']
  max_train_length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum train length in meters that the operator can handle, constrained by locomotive power, siding capacity, and network infrastructure.']
  network_access_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the network access agreement between the rail operator and the rail infrastructure manager, governing track usage rights and charges.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the rail operator was first registered and onboarded into the port\'s intermodal logistics network.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the rail operator within the port\'s intermodal network, indicating whether the operator is authorized to provide services.']
  operator_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rail operator, used in operational systems and EDI messaging (IFTMIN, COPARN). Typically assigned by port authority or national rail registry.']
  operator_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rail operator based on service scope and operational model.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days for invoices issued to the rail operator for port services (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days).']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Composite performance rating score (0.00 to 5.00) based on KPIs such as on-time delivery, cargo handling quality, safety record, and EDI compliance.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the rail operator\'s preferred invoicing and settlement currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the registered business address of the rail operator, typically street number and name.']
  registered_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered business address, typically suite, floor, or building name.']
  registered_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered business address.']
  registered_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered business address.']
  registered_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the registered business address.']
  regulatory_licence_number varchar [note: 'Official licence or permit number issued by the national railway safety authority authorizing the operator to provide rail freight services.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special handling instructions, or operational comments related to the rail operator.']
  service_corridors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of primary rail corridors or routes served by the operator (e.g., Port-City A, Port-City B-ICD C). Defines geographic service coverage.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the rail operator\'s authorization to operate within the port network was terminated or suspended, if applicable.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the rail operator for public information and service inquiries.']

  Note: 'Master record for rail freight operators providing intermodal rail services to and from the port, capturing operator code, company name, regulatory licence number, network access agreement reference, service corridors, locomotive types, EDI partner ID, and operational contact details. SSOT for rail carrier identity within the intermodal domain.'
}

Table intermodal.service {
  service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intermodal transport service. Primary key.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each intermodal service (port-to-ICD shuttle, rail corridor) is operated by designated cost center; linking enables service-level cost accumulation for pricing decisions, service rationalization analy']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services define demurrage terms as a core commercial parameter — the demurrage schedule governs free time and escalating rates for containers on the service. Required for service-level cont']
  detention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services include detention terms as a core commercial parameter — the detention schedule defines free time and rates for containers on the service. Required for service-level contract confi']
  haulier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.haulier. Business justification: Truck services are operated by hauliers. The operator_id field is polymorphic (could be rail_operator or haulier depending on transport_mode). When transport_mode = \'TRUCK\', this FK links to haulier. ']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Intermodal services (rail/truck) originate from a specific terminal. Terminal-level service capacity planning, terminal operator commercial agreements with rail/truck operators, and terminal throughpu']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transport operator or service provider responsible for executing this intermodal service.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Intermodal services operate between defined origin locations. Service planning, capacity allocation, and network design require proper location reference. Replaces denormalized origin_location text fi']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Intermodal services reference published port tariff schedules to calculate combined transport pricing. Essential for multimodal rate calculation and quote generation in port-hinterland transport opera']
  primary_security_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Scheduled intermodal services operate through designated secure corridors and primary operational zones requiring route security planning, zone-based access control, and MARSEC-level service authoriza']
  rail_operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: Rail services are operated by rail operators. The operator_id field is polymorphic (could be rail_operator or haulier depending on transport_mode). When transport_mode = \'RAIL\', this FK links to rail_']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Intermodal services define the transport product offered — the applicable THC schedule is a core commercial parameter of the service. Required for customer quoting, service tariff publication, and THC']
  booking_cutoff_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure that bookings must be submitted. Used for operational planning and customer service commitments.']
  capacity_teu varchar [note: 'Maximum container capacity per service departure, measured in TEU. Critical for load planning and service utilization tracking.']
  corridor_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the origin-destination corridor, typically in format XXX-YYY where XXX is origin and YYY is destination location code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intermodal service record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customs_clearance_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this intermodal service includes customs clearance coordination at origin or destination, critical for international cargo movements.']
  dangerous_goods_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is certified and equipped to handle IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) classified cargo.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'End point of the intermodal service corridor. Typically an Inland Container Depot (ICD), rail terminal, or distribution center.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service supports EDI integration for automated booking, COPARN (Container Pre-Announcement), and IFTMIN (Instruction Message for Transport) messaging with inland logistics partners.']
  edi_message_types varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of supported EDI message types for this service, such as COPARN, IFTMIN, IFTMBC (Booking Confirmation), IFTSTA (Status Report).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this intermodal service becomes operational and available for booking. Used for service lifecycle management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this intermodal service is scheduled to end or be discontinued. Nullable for ongoing services.']
  equipment_type_supported varchar [note: 'Types of container equipment supported by this service. May include standard dry containers, reefers, open-top, flat-rack, tank containers. Pipe-separated list if multiple types.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the intermodal service operates. Daily: every day. Weekly: once per week. Bi-weekly: twice per week. On-demand: as requested. Scheduled: fixed timetable.']
  gate_cutoff_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours before scheduled departure that containers must be delivered to the origin gate or Container Yard (CY). Critical for load planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this intermodal service record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  oversize_cargo_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service can accommodate oversize or out-of-gauge cargo such as open-top or flat-rack containers.']
  reefer_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service can handle refrigerated containers with temperature-controlled transport throughout the corridor.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, restrictions, or special instructions related to this intermodal service. Free-text field for operational teams.']
  route_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the intermodal service route measured in kilometers, used for transit time calculation and tariff determination.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the intermodal service, used in operational systems and customer communications. Typically alphanumeric 6-12 characters.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the intermodal service offering, including route highlights, special features, and operational characteristics. Used for marketing and customer communications.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the intermodal transport service, used for marketing and operational reference.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the intermodal service. Active: currently operating. Suspended: temporarily halted. Discontinued: permanently ended. Planned: future service. Seasonal: operates during specific periods.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intermodal service offering. Rail shuttle: dedicated rail service between port and inland terminal. ICD linkage: connection to Inland Container Depot. Drayage: short-haul truck movement. Inland barge: waterway transport. Road feeder: scheduled truck service. Multimodal corridor: combination of transport modes.']
  sla_on_time_performance_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time delivery performance as defined in customer Service Level Agreements. Measured as percentage of departures/arrivals within committed transit time window.']
  transit_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Benchmark transit time for the service from origin to destination, measured in hours. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments and customer expectations.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this service. Rail: railway transport. Truck: road transport. Barge: inland waterway. Multimodal: combination of modes.']
  weekly_departures varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled departures per week for this service. Used for capacity planning and customer service commitments.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of intermodal transport services and corridors offered by the port, including rail shuttle services, ICD linkage routes, drayage service packages, and scheduled inland connections. Captures service code, name, transport mode, origin-destination corridor, route distance (km), transit time benchmark, frequency, capacity (TEU), active operators, tariff reference, and service status. SSOT for intermodal service offerings and corridor definitions.'
}

Table intermodal.slot_booking {
  slot_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intermodal slot booking record. Primary key.']
  booking_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Slot bookings (capacity reservations) are priced using customer-specific rate cards. Critical for booking confirmation, revenue estimation, and capacity management billing in intermodal services.']
  call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: An intermodal slot booking for container transport must reference the vessel call booking for billing intermodal handling charges and coordinating cargo cut-off times. Finance teams require this link ']
  cargo_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Intermodal slot bookings (rail/truck capacity) coordinate with vessel cargo bookings for transshipment operations, intermodal transfers, and inland container movements. Required for matching container']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Slot bookings for dangerous goods require commodity code for pre-notification to port authorities and capacity planning by cargo type. Regulatory pre-clearance and dangerous goods slot allocation depe']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: Slot bookings on intermodal services are made for specific containers. Intermodal operators need to know which container occupies which slot for equipment planning, gate coordination, and reefer/hazma']
  container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Slot allocation requires container type to match equipment availability, yard block preference, and handling equipment compatibility. The plain attribute equipment_type_code on slot_booking is a denor']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Slot bookings for international cargo require a customs declaration to be lodged before the slot is confirmed. Port community systems verify customs declaration status at slot booking stage — a standa']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings may result in demurrage if containers are not moved within free time. The demurrage schedule at booking level enables proactive customer notification and cost estimation — required for d']
  destination_icd_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.icd_facility. Business justification: A slot booking for intermodal transport specifies a destination location, which in the intermodal context is typically an ICD or CFS facility. slot_booking has destination_location_code as a free-text']
  detention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings for container pickup initiate the detention clock. The detention schedule at booking level defines free time and billing rates — required for detention cost disclosure at booking, custom']
  origin_terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Slot bookings for intermodal services specify origin terminal zone for container pickup, enabling capacity reservation, zone allocation planning, and service scheduling - intermodal booking process.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, or cargo owner) who made this booking. Links to customer master data.']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit if this intermodal booking is for cargo arriving or departing on a specific vessel. Links to vessel operations.']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Slot bookings involve cargo owners and operators who must be screened against sanctions lists before booking confirmation. Linking each slot booking to its sanctions screening record is a mandatory AM']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the intermodal service (rail or truck route) for which this slot is booked. Links to the service schedule and capacity plan.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: A slot booking on an intermodal service is made for cargo belonging to a specific shipment. Port community systems and intermodal operators link slot bookings to shipments for capacity planning, cargo']
  slot_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.slot_reservation. Business justification: An intermodal slot booking must be linked to the corresponding vessel slot reservation to confirm end-to-end space allocation from inland origin to vessel. Shipping lines and terminal planners use thi']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal. Business justification: Slot bookings are made at a specific terminal — terminal capacity planning, slot utilization reporting by terminal, and commercial agreements between shipping lines and terminal operators all require ']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: Slot bookings involve container moves attracting THC. The THC schedule at booking level enables upfront cost disclosure and billing — required for customer invoicing, booking confirmation with cost br']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: Slot bookings reserve intermodal capacity for transport orders. The booking is the capacity reservation; the transport order is the instruction. Linking allows retrieval of AWB and BOL from transport_']
  actual_transport_date date [note: 'Actual date when the intermodal transport service was executed. Used for on-time performance measurement and SLA compliance.']
  booking_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the booking was submitted. Used for channel performance analysis and digital adoption tracking.']
  booking_date date [note: 'Date when the slot booking was created or submitted by the customer. Used for booking lead time analysis and service level tracking.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference number issued to the customer for this intermodal slot reservation. Used for customer communication and tracking.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the slot booking. Tracks progression from initial request through fulfillment or cancellation.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the booking was created in the system. Used for audit trail and first-come-first-served slot allocation.']
  booking_type varchar [note: 'Type of intermodal transport mode for this booking. Determines handling procedures and capacity allocation rules.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for booking cancellation. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the booking was cancelled. Null if booking is active. Used for cancellation analysis and capacity recovery.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of cargo being transported. Determines handling requirements and equipment allocation for the intermodal service.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the booking status changed to confirmed. Used for service level measurement and customer notification tracking.']
  confirmed_slot_date date [note: 'Actual date confirmed for the intermodal transport slot. Null if booking is pending or waitlisted.']
  confirmed_slot_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time window confirmed for the intermodal transport departure or pickup. Used for gate appointment coordination.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Total number of containers included in this slot booking, regardless of size. Used for slot allocation and capacity planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this booking record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s own internal reference or purchase order number for this booking. Used for reconciliation and customer reporting.']
  edi_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the EDI message (IFTMIN, COPARN, or AWB) that initiated or confirmed this booking. Used for EDI reconciliation and audit.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this booking includes hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this booking record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  no_show_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer failed to utilize the confirmed slot. Used for no-show penalty assessment and capacity planning.']
  oversized_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this booking includes oversized or out-of-gauge cargo requiring special equipment or routing.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed for this booking. Determines billing and collection procedures.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Service priority level for this booking. Determines slot allocation preference and service level agreement targets.']
  reefer_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this booking includes refrigerated containers requiring temperature-controlled transport.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free-text remarks or notes about this booking. Used for operational communication and exception handling.']
  requested_transport_date date [note: 'Date requested by the customer for the intermodal transport service. May differ from confirmed slot date based on availability.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this booking record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling requirements, routing instructions, or operational notes for this booking.']
  teu_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total capacity of this booking expressed in TEU. Calculated from container sizes (20ft=1 TEU, 40ft=2 TEU, 45ft=2.25 TEU). Used for capacity utilization reporting.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of cargo in this booking expressed in cubic meters. Used for space utilization and capacity planning.']
  weight_tonnes decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of cargo in this booking expressed in metric tonnes. Used for load planning and compliance with weight restrictions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for intermodal slot bookings made by shipping lines, freight forwarders, or cargo owners for rail or truck services, capturing booking reference, service ID, customer reference, container count, TEU quantity, booking date, requested transport date, confirmed slot, and booking status. Supports capacity management for intermodal services.'
}

Table customer.port_community_participant {
  port_community_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each port community participant record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master entity. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Country of incorporation determines trade compliance requirements, sanctions screening protocols, customs procedures, WCO membership status, and regulatory jurisdiction for all port community particip']
  flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Vessel operators and shipping lines are registered under flag states; essential for PSC targeting, sanctions screening, regulatory compliance, and maritime authority jurisdiction determination in port']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: port_community_participant has vessel_operator_flag and carrier_code (denormalized). When participant_type is a shipping line, this FK links to the canonical shipping_line master for SCAC code validat']
  aeo_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the participant\'s AEO certification. Customs compliance teams must monitor this date to ensure continued eligibility for expedited customs clearance. Null if AEO certification is not held.']
  aeo_certification_number varchar [note: 'Authorised Economic Operator (AEO) certification number issued by the national customs authority. AEO status indicates a trusted trader with streamlined customs clearance privileges. Applicable to importers, exporters, freight forwarders, and customs brokers.']
  annual_teu_volume_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated annual container throughput volume in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for this participant. Used for capacity planning, berth scheduling, equipment allocation, and commercial negotiations. Sourced from CRM account profiling.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address designated for receipt of invoices, Terminal Handling Charges (THC), wharfage (WHR), demurrage (DMG), and detention (DET) billing communications. May differ from the primary operational contact.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and financial transactions with this participant (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Determines the currency basis for invoicing and accounts receivable.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the participant for port services before prepayment or guarantee is required. Expressed in the port\'s base operating currency. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI and enforced by NAVIS N4 billing controls.']
  crm_account_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM Account entity. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the CRM and the port community master data. Enables traceability back to the originating CRM record.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial segmentation tier assigned to the participant based on volume, revenue contribution, and strategic importance to the port. Determines applicable service level agreements (SLA), tariff schedules, and priority berth allocation. Managed in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  customs_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs authority registration or accreditation number assigned to the participant (e.g., Authorised Economic Operator number, customs broker licence number, or importer/exporter code). Required for customs clearance and trade compliance operations.']
  dangerous_goods_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant is approved to handle, declare, or transport International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) classified cargo through the port. Approval requires IMDG certification and compliance with SOLAS Chapter VII.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s account was deactivated or suspended. Null for currently active participants. Used for historical reporting, regulatory audit trails, and reactivation workflows.']
  freight_forwarder_licence_number varchar [note: 'National freight forwarding licence or accreditation number issued by the relevant transport authority. Required for participants classified as freight forwarders to legally arrange cargo transport and issue House Bills of Lading (BOL). Null for non-freight-forwarder participant types.']
  imo_company_number varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO company identification number assigned by IHS Markit on behalf of the IMO. Mandatory for shipping companies and ship operators under IMO Resolution A.1078(28). Provides a permanent, globally unique identifier for maritime entities.']
  isps_accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant\'s ISPS accreditation expires. Port security management must be notified prior to expiry to ensure uninterrupted access to secure port areas. Null if ISPS accreditation is not applicable.']
  isps_accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current ISPS Code accreditation status of the participant. Determines eligibility for access to restricted port security zones and handling of security-sensitive cargo. Mandatory for shipping lines and port agents operating under SOLAS Chapter XI-2.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent operational activity recorded for this participant (e.g., vessel call, container gate-in/out, cargo manifest submission). Used to identify dormant participants and support account review processes.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the port community participant as recorded in the official business registry of the country of incorporation. Used for all formal documentation, contracts, and regulatory filings.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant was formally onboarded and approved to conduct operations within the port community. Marks the start of the participant\'s active relationship with the port for SLA measurement and tenure reporting.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participant\'s account within the port community system. Controls access to port services, gate entry, and TOS operations. \'suspended\' indicates temporary restriction; \'blacklisted\' indicates permanent exclusion pending regulatory review.']
  p_and_i_club_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Protection and Indemnity (P&I) insurance club providing liability coverage to the participant, particularly relevant for shipping lines and port agents. Used in claims management and incident response coordination.']
  p_and_i_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the participant\'s P&I insurance coverage. Required for port entry of vessels and for processing liability claims arising from cargo damage, pollution incidents, or port infrastructure damage.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the participant\'s primary role within the port community ecosystem. Determines applicable workflows, tariff schedules, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|cargo_owner|consignee|importer|exporter — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to this participant for port service invoices (e.g., NET30, NET60, PREPAID, COD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI customer master and governs invoice due date calculation.']
  pcs_participant_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the participant within the Port Community System (PCS) for EDI messaging, cargo manifest submissions, and stakeholder communications. Used as the primary operational identifier in PCS integrations.']
  preferred_edi_format varchar [note: 'Preferred EDI message format used by the participant for electronic communications with the port, including cargo manifests, COPARN pre-announcements, BAPLIE stowage plans, and IFTMIN transport instructions. Drives PCS integration configuration.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary operational contact at the participant organisation. Used for EDI notifications, vessel arrival alerts, cargo status updates, and billing communications from the Port Community System.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary operational contact person at the participant organisation. Used for day-to-day port operations communications, notifications, and service coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'International format telephone number of the primary operational contact at the participant organisation. Used for urgent operational communications including vessel scheduling, berth changes, and security incidents.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the participant record was first created in the port community master data system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and GDPR data retention policy enforcement.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the participant record in the port community master data system. Used for change data capture (CDC), data quality monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']
  registered_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the participant\'s official registered business address. Used in conjunction with registered_address_line1 and country_of_incorporation for complete address resolution.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the participant\'s official registered business address as recorded in the company registry. Used for formal correspondence, legal notices, and regulatory filings.']
  registered_address_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the participant\'s official registered business address. Required for formal correspondence and regulatory submissions.']
  sanctions_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date on which the participant was most recently screened against international sanctions lists. Compliance policy typically requires periodic re-screening (e.g., quarterly or upon each vessel call). Supports audit trail for trade compliance reporting.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the participant\'s screening against international sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, UN Security Council, EU Consolidated List). Flagged participants are blocked from port services pending compliance review. Critical for trade compliance and anti-money laundering obligations.']
  sap_customer_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer account number assigned in SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) module. Used for invoicing, accounts receivable management, and financial reconciliation of port charges including THC, wharfage, and pilotage fees.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the applicable Service Level Agreement (SLA) tier governing turnaround times, berth priority, equipment availability, and dispute resolution timelines for this participant. Links to the SLA master reference for KPI monitoring.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (TIN) or VAT registration number of the participant in their country of incorporation. Required for customs declarations, invoicing, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  tos_account_code varchar [note: 'Account code assigned to the participant in the Terminal Operating System (NAVIS N4 or TOPS Expert). Used for container tracking, equipment dispatch authorisation, yard management, and billing within the TOS. Links participant master data to TOS operational records.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or operating name used by the participant in day-to-day port operations, which may differ from the registered legal name. Used in operational communications and TOS displays.']
  trucking_licence_number varchar [note: 'Road transport operator licence number issued by the national transport authority. Required for trucking companies accessing the port gate for container pickup and delivery. Used in gate management within NAVIS N4 for vehicle pre-registration.']
  vessel_operator_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the participant operates vessels calling at the port. When true, the participant is subject to vessel scheduling, berth allocation, and VTMS coordination requirements. Drives integration with the Vessel Traffic Management System (VTMS).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing all port community participants — shipping lines, freight forwarders, cargo owners, consignees, importers, exporters, customs brokers, trucking companies, and port agents. This is the SSOT for all legal entities and organisations that interact with the port. Captures entity type, legal registration, IMO company number, ISPS accreditation status, tax identifiers, country of incorporation, and operational status. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Account entity) and Port Community System (PCS) stakeholder registry.'
}

  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated and issued to this participant account. \'per_vessel_call\' is common for shipping lines where charges are consolidated per port call.']
