Used by NAVIS N4 billing module to enforce credit controls on Terminal Handling Charge (THC) and other port dues.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit rating assigned to this participant account reflecting financial risk. Influences credit limit setting, payment terms, and deposit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|unrated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or last-completed date for periodic credit limit review of this account. Ensures credit limits remain aligned with participant trading volumes and financial standing.']
  credit_utilisation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit currently consumed by the outstanding balance (outstanding_balance / credit_limit × 100). Used for credit risk monitoring and automated suspension triggers in NAVIS N4 billing.']
  crm_account_reference varchar [note: 'Source system account identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM corresponding to this participant account. Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and CRM integration.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this account is denominated for billing, invoicing, and credit limit management (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Supports multi-currency port operations and Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) and Currency Adjustment Factor (CAF) calculations.']
  customs_broker_licence varchar [note: 'Regulatory licence number issued by the national customs authority to the participant where the account type is \'customs_broker\'. Required for Port Community System (PCS) customs integration and trade compliance validation.']
  days_sales_outstanding varchar [note: 'Average number of days taken by this account to settle invoices, calculated over a rolling period. Key accounts receivable Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for port commercial and finance teams.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash security deposit (in account currency) held by the port against this account to cover potential non-payment of port dues, Demurrage (DMG), Detention (DET), or damage liabilities.']
  discount_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated percentage discount applied to standard tariff charges for this account. Reflects volume commitments, strategic partnerships, or long-term service agreements. Commercially sensitive.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is configured for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) message exchange via the Port Community System (PCS) for automated cargo manifests, container pre-announcements (COPARN), and delivery orders (D/O).']
  edi_participant_code varchar [note: 'Unique Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) identifier assigned to this account for automated message exchange via the Port Community System (PCS). Used in COPARN, IFTMIN, and BAPLIE message sets for container pre-announcement and vessel planning.']
  isps_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number issued under the International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code confirming the participant\'s security compliance for port access. Required for shipping lines and port agents operating under SOLAS Chapter XI-2.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this participant account record was most recently updated. Used for change data capture, audit trails, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value (in account currency) of the most recent payment received against this account. Supports cash flow monitoring and reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent payment was received and applied to this account. Used for overdue monitoring, Demurrage (DMG) escalation, and credit risk assessment.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial transaction (invoice, payment, or credit note) posted against this account. Used for dormancy detection and account review scheduling.']
  navis_account_code varchar [note: 'Account code as configured in the NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System billing module. Used for charge posting, invoice generation, and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) allocation in terminal operations.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current unpaid balance (in account currency) owed by the participant to the port across all invoices. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI accounts receivable sub-ledger and reconciled with NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount (in account currency) of invoices past their due date on this account. Triggers automated dunning, service suspension, or escalation workflows in SAP S/4HANA and NAVIS N4.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the oldest unpaid invoice on this account has been overdue. Used for dunning level determination and credit suspension escalation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or contracted payment method for settling port dues, Terminal Handling Charges (THC), Wharfage (WHR), and other tariff charges on this account.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to this account (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Governs invoice due dates, Demurrage (DMG) and Detention (DET) billing cycles, and late payment penalty calculations. Aligned with SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'Port service line under which this account operates, determining applicable operational procedures, equipment allocation, and tariff structures. Roll-on Roll-off (RoRo) and container accounts have distinct billing and operational profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container|bulk|roro|general_cargo|liquid_bulk|cruise|offshore — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the Service Level Agreement (SLA) tier applicable to this account, governing guaranteed berth turnaround times, crane productivity rates, and gate processing commitments.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax registration or VAT number for the participant account entity. Required for invoice compliance, customs documentation, and World Customs Organization (WCO) trade reporting.']
  ytd_teu_volume varchar [note: 'Actual Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) throughput processed under this account in the current calendar or fiscal year. Used to track progress against annual volume commitments and trigger tier reclassification.']

  Note: 'Commercial account record for each port community participant, representing the billing and trading relationship between the participant and the port. Tracks account number, credit limit, payment terms, account status, currency, outstanding balance, and account tier. Distinct from the participant master — one participant may hold multiple accounts (e.g., separate accounts per terminal or service line). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and NAVIS N4 billing module.'
}

Table customer.contact_person {
  contact_person_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record in the port community participant registry. Primary key for the contact_person data product.']
  parent_contact_contact_person_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier supporting contact hierarchy structures within a participant organisation. Points to the supervising or parent contact person (e.g., an operations manager who supervises multiple shipping agents). Enables hierarchical escalation routing for SLA breaches and operational exceptions.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: A contact person may be specifically associated with a participant account (e.g., the billing contact for a specific port account, the EDI contact for a particular trading account). A port community p']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) that this contact person is associated with. Foreign key candidate to the customer/participant entity.']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact person\'s port access accreditation or security clearance expires. Triggers renewal workflow notifications to the participant and port security team. Null if accreditation is not required for this contact type.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the contact person for port facility access and operational authorisations. Accredited contacts have passed ISPS security screening and hold valid port access credentials. Expired or revoked contacts must be re-screened before port access is reinstated.']
  contact_person_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact person record. Active contacts are eligible to receive port notifications and access PCS services. Inactive contacts have been deactivated (e.g., staff departure). Suspended contacts are temporarily blocked pending re-verification. Pending_verification contacts are awaiting identity or accreditation confirmation.']
  contact_role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the contact person within the port community context. Drives role-based notification routing: operations contacts receive ETA alerts and vessel call confirmations; finance contacts receive invoices and THC statements; customs contacts receive cargo manifest and trade compliance notifications; emergency contacts receive ISPS and safety alerts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operations|finance|customs|emergency|commercial|technical|legal|environmental|security|management — promote to reference product]']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact person\'s standing within the participant account hierarchy. Primary contacts are the main account representatives; billing contacts receive financial documents (THC invoices, demurrage statements); emergency contacts are notified for ISPS security incidents and safety events; technical contacts handle operational system integrations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact person record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the audit trail creation event sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (createdon field) or the ingestion timestamp for manually entered records.']
  crm_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this contact person in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Contact entity). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM source of record.']
  customs_broker_licence_number varchar [note: 'Official customs broker licence or registration number issued by the national customs authority for contact persons acting as licensed customs brokers. Required for trade compliance validation and customs clearance authorisation within the Port Community System.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional unit within the participant organisation to which this contact person belongs (e.g., Operations, Finance, Customs, Logistics). Supports departmental routing of port notifications and invoices.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of all non-essential communications. When True, the contact is excluded from marketing, commercial outreach, and non-operational notifications. Mandatory for GDPR and privacy regulation compliance.']
  do_not_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of email communications specifically. When True, the contact is excluded from email-based notifications and marketing campaigns. Supports granular GDPR consent management at the channel level.']
  do_not_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person has opted out of SMS/text message communications. When True, the contact is excluded from SMS-based vessel arrival alerts and operational notifications. Supports granular GDPR consent management.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact person record became operationally effective and eligible to receive port notifications and access port community services. Supports temporal validity tracking for contact hierarchy management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact person record ceases to be operationally effective (e.g., upon staff departure or role change). Null for currently active contacts. Used for temporal filtering in reporting and notification suppression after departure.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact person. Used as the principal digital channel for ETA/ETD alerts, vessel call confirmations, invoice delivery, EDI notifications, and Port Community System (PCS) messaging.']
  email_secondary varchar [note: 'Secondary or alternate business email address for the contact person. Used as a failover channel when the primary email is undeliverable, or for routing specific notification categories (e.g., invoice copies to a shared finance mailbox).']
  fax varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for the contact person. Retained for legacy trade documentation workflows where certain customs authorities and port state control bodies still require fax-based submission of manifests and declarations.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person as recorded in the CRM system. Used for personalised communications including ETA alerts, vessel call confirmations, and invoice delivery.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the contact person as it appears in official port communications, vessel call notifications, and trade documentation. May include salutation or professional title prefix.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact person provided explicit consent for the port operator to process their personal data for specified purposes. Required for GDPR Article 7 compliance documentation and data subject rights management.']
  is_edi_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the EDI technical contact for the participant organisation. EDI contacts receive BAPLIE, COPARN, IFTMIN, and other UN/EDIFACT message acknowledgements and error notifications from the Port Community System.']
  is_isps_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the ISPS security contact for the participant organisation. ISPS contacts receive security level change notifications, port facility security alerts, and are included in emergency communication trees as required by the ISPS Code.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated as the primary representative for the associated port community participant. Only one contact per participant should hold this flag as True. Used to determine the default recipient for vessel call confirmations and account-level communications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or designation of the contact person within their organisation (e.g., Operations Manager, Shipping Agent, Customs Broker, Finance Controller, Port Agent). Used for role-based notification routing and SLA escalation.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person as recorded in the CRM system. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence and notification routing.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the contact person. Used by the commercial team for relationship management, stakeholder engagement tracking, and business development activities within Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  mobile varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number of the contact person in E.164 format. Primary channel for urgent SMS/push notifications including vessel arrival alerts, berth allocation changes, and emergency ISPS security notifications.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact person record was last updated in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (modifiedon field). Used for change data capture (CDC) and incremental lakehouse ingestion.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the contact person. Relevant for port access control, ISPS security screening, and compliance with national maritime security regulations governing port facility access.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about the contact person recorded by port staff or account managers. May include special communication instructions, availability constraints, or contextual information relevant to port operations coordination.']
  notification_eta_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is subscribed to receive automated ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) alert notifications when a vessel associated with their participant organisation is approaching the port. Sourced from CRM notification preference settings.']
  notification_invoice boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is designated to receive invoice delivery notifications including Terminal Handling Charges (THC), wharfage (WHR), demurrage (DMG), and detention (DET) billing statements generated by the NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  notification_vessel_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact person is subscribed to receive vessel call confirmation notifications including berth allocation, ETB (Estimated Time of Berthing), and vessel call number assignments from the Port Management Information System (PMIS).']
  pcs_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned to this contact person within the Port Community System (PCS) for stakeholder messaging, cargo manifest communications, and trade documentation routing.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number of the contact person including country and area code in E.164 format. Used for voice communications regarding vessel operations, berth scheduling, and cargo handling coordination.']
  port_access_badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical or RFID-enabled port access badge number issued to the contact person for entry into controlled port facility zones. Links to the port access control system for audit trail and ISPS compliance reporting.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'The contact person\'s preferred channel for receiving port notifications and operational communications. Drives the notification dispatch logic in the Port Management Information System (PMIS) and PCS messaging engine. Valid values: email (electronic mail), sms (short message service), phone (voice call), fax (facsimile), pcs_portal (Port Community System web portal), edi (Electronic Data Interchange automated message).']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or BCP-47 language code representing the contact person\'s preferred language for receiving communications, documents, and notifications (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'zh-CN\' for Simplified Chinese, \'ar\' for Arabic). Supports multilingual port community operations.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact person (e.g., Mr, Ms, Dr, Capt). Used in formal port correspondence, bill of lading communications, and official maritime documentation where professional titles are required.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact person record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during master data management. Primary source is Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM; secondary sources include the Port Community System (PCS) and manual entry.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact person\'s operational location (e.g., \'Asia/Singapore\', \'Europe/Rotterdam\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used to schedule notifications and communications at appropriate local times, particularly for ETA/ETD alerts across international shipping routes.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with port community participants — operations managers, shipping agents, customs brokers, finance contacts, and emergency contacts. Captures full name, job title, department, email, phone, mobile, preferred communication channel, language, and active status. Supports contact hierarchy and role-based routing for notifications (ETA alerts, vessel call confirmations, invoice delivery). Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM (Contact entity).'
}

Table customer.accreditation {
  accreditation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each accreditation, certification, permit, or official document record held by a port community participant or their personnel/vehicle. Primary key for the accreditation data product.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Accreditations (AEO, ISPS, dangerous goods) are verified and renewed through compliance audits. Port compliance teams conduct audits specifically to validate accreditation conditions. Linking the accr']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Dangerous goods handling accreditations (IMDG permits) must specify authorized HS code scope. Port operators require this link to validate that a participant\'s DG accreditation covers the specific com']
  contact_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact_person. Business justification: Accreditations are frequently issued to specific individuals — ISPS clearances, dangerous goods handler certifications, customs broker licences, and port access accreditations are all person-level cre']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Accreditation processing costs (ISPS verification, dangerous goods certification, port access permits) are allocated to security/compliance cost centres for regulatory compliance cost tracking and fee']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Dangerous goods handling accreditations specify which IMDG classes the holder is certified for; mandatory for ISPS compliance, cargo acceptance gate checks, and segregation enforcement in port operati']
  import_export_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.import_export_permit. Business justification: Certain participant accreditations (dangerous goods handler, controlled goods dealer) are prerequisites for or directly tied to import/export permits. Port compliance officers cross-reference accredit']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Accreditations (dangerous goods handling, ISPS, customs broker) are scoped to specific terminals in port operations. The accreditation has port_location_id but not terminal_id. Terminal-scoped accredi']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant entity (shipping line, freight forwarder, customs broker, stevedoring company, trucking company, port agent, cargo owner, etc.) that holds this accreditation at the entity level. Null when the accreditation is held at individual or vehicle level only.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Port access permits and facility-specific accreditations are tied to specific terminals, berths, or ISPS security zones; required for physical access control enforcement and security zone management.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_zone. Business justification: Accreditations grant access to specific security zones under ISPS Code. Links permit scope to physical zone definitions for access control enforcement. Existing security_zone_access_level is denormali']
  accreditation_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the accreditation. Security covers ISPS and access control documents. Trade Compliance covers customs, freight forwarder, and broker licences. Operational covers stevedoring, dangerous goods, and vessel operator certifications. Insurance covers P&I and liability certificates. Environmental covers ISO 14001 and MARPOL compliance certificates. Health and Safety covers OHS and ISO 45001 certifications.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accreditation or permit. Active indicates the document is valid and in force. Expired indicates the document has passed its expiry date without renewal. Suspended indicates temporary withdrawal of privileges pending investigation or compliance action. Revoked indicates permanent cancellation by the issuing authority. Pending indicates the application has been submitted and is awaiting approval. Cancelled indicates the holder voluntarily withdrew the application.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation or document type. Covers entity-level accreditations (e.g., ISPS facility security certificate, customs broker licence, freight forwarder registration, dangerous goods handling certification, stevedoring company accreditation), official documents (trade licence, P&I insurance certificate, vessel operator certificate), and individual/vehicle-level permits (port access permit, ISPS security zone authorisation, biometric access credential, contractor access pass). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: isps_facility_certificate|customs_broker_licence|freight_forwarder_registration|dangerous_goods_certification|stevedoring_accreditation|trade_licence|pi_insurance_certificate|vessel_operator_certificate|port_access_permit|security_zone_authorisation|biometric_credential|contractor_access_pass — promote to reference product]']
  application_reference_number varchar [note: 'The internal or external application reference number assigned when the accreditation application was lodged with the issuing authority or the port. Used to track the application through the approval workflow and to correspond with the issuing authority during the renewal or initial application process.']
  biometric_reference varchar [note: 'Tokenised reference identifier linking this access credential to the holder\'s biometric record stored in the port\'s biometric access control system. This is a non-reversible token, not raw biometric data. Populated only for biometric-linked access credentials. Classified as restricted PII biometric data.']
  coverage_scope_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific operational scope, geographic coverage, or service categories covered by this accreditation (e.g., \'Authorised for FCL and LCL customs clearance at Port of Melbourne\', \'Stevedoring operations limited to container terminal berths 1-6\'). Provides context beyond the accreditation type classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this accreditation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation point for data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  crm_record_reference varchar [note: 'The record identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM corresponding to this accreditation. Enables bidirectional synchronisation between the accreditation data product and the CRM system for customer-facing compliance status visibility and service request management.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The official externally-assigned certificate, licence, permit, or document number as issued by the relevant authority (e.g., customs broker licence number, ISPS facility security certificate number, port access permit number). This is the primary business identifier used in compliance gating and audit trails.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation, certificate, or permit ceases to be valid. Null for open-ended or permanent accreditations. Used as the primary trigger for renewal workflows, compliance gating in PCS and VTMS, and automated expiry alerts. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  file_reference varchar [note: 'Storage path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the original accreditation document or certificate. Enables direct retrieval of source documents for audit, compliance verification, and Port State Control inspections.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum indemnity coverage amount specified in the insurance certificate, expressed in the currency indicated by insurance_currency_code. Populated only for insurance certificate accreditation types. Used to verify that minimum coverage thresholds required by port bylaws are met. Classified as confidential financial data.']
  insurance_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the insurance coverage amount (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD). Populated only when insurance_coverage_amount is present.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this accreditation is a mandatory regulatory or port bylaw requirement for the participant to conduct operations. True indicates the accreditation is legally or contractually required. False indicates it is voluntary or supplementary. Used in compliance gating logic to determine whether a missing or expired accreditation blocks operations.']
  is_revoked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this accreditation has been formally revoked by the issuing authority or the port. True indicates the accreditation is revoked and must not be used for compliance gating. Provides a fast-path check for access control systems independent of the full status field.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation, certificate, or permit was officially issued by the issuing authority. Establishes the effective start of the document\'s validity period and is used in compliance age calculations.']
  issuing_authority_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction under which the accreditation was issued. Critical for cross-border trade compliance, mutual recognition agreements, and Port State Control verification.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the government body, regulatory authority, classification society, or port authority that issued the accreditation, certificate, or permit (e.g., National Maritime Safety Authority, Customs Authority, Port State Control, IMO-recognised organisation). Used for verification and audit purposes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this accreditation record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail completeness in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the accreditation was verified against the issuing authority\'s records or through a formal audit process. Used to determine whether re-verification is required and to support Port State Control inspection readiness.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, conditions, restrictions, or remarks associated with this accreditation record. May include special conditions imposed by the issuing authority, port-specific operational restrictions, or compliance officer observations.']
  pcs_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier assigned by the Port Community System (PCS) to this accreditation record. Enables direct cross-reference between the accreditation data product and the PCS for customs integration, cargo manifest compliance gating, and stakeholder messaging workflows.']
  permit_holder_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued identity document number (passport, national ID, seafarer\'s identity document) of the individual permit holder. Populated only when scope_level is \'individual\'. Used for identity verification at port access control points and ISPS security zone entry. Classified as restricted PII.']
  permit_holder_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual person (personnel, contractor, driver) who holds this permit or access credential when scope_level is \'individual\'. Null for entity-level or vehicle-level accreditations. Classified as restricted PII as it directly identifies a natural person.']
  pi_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The Protection and Indemnity (P&I) Club insurance policy number associated with this insurance certificate accreditation. Populated only when accreditation_type is \'pi_insurance_certificate\'. P&I cover is a mandatory requirement for vessel operators and stevedoring companies operating within the port. Classified as confidential business data.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority to avoid a lapse in accreditation. Typically calculated as a fixed lead-time before the expiry_date (e.g., 30 or 60 days prior). Drives automated renewal reminder workflows and compliance monitoring dashboards.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the current state of the renewal process for this accreditation. Not Due indicates the document is current and renewal is not yet required. Renewal Initiated indicates internal renewal preparation has begun. Renewal Submitted indicates the renewal application has been lodged with the issuing authority. Renewal Approved indicates the renewed document has been received. Renewal Overdue indicates the document has expired and renewal has not been completed.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation was formally revoked by the issuing authority or the port authority. Null when the accreditation has not been revoked. Used in audit trails and compliance investigations.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for revocation of the accreditation, as recorded by the revoking authority or port compliance officer. Null when not revoked. Critical for audit readiness and ISPS Code enforcement documentation.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'The Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tag number embedded in the physical access card or vehicle transponder associated with this accreditation. Used for automated gate access control and security zone entry tracking in NAVIS N4 gate operations.']
  scope_level varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this accreditation applies at the entity (organisation/company) level, individual (person/personnel) level, or vehicle level. Drives compliance gating logic in PCS and VTMS workflows and determines which permit holder reference fields are populated.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the accreditation was suspended by the issuing authority or the port. Null when the accreditation has not been suspended. Used in compliance investigations and audit trails to track the duration of suspension periods.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for suspension of the accreditation, as recorded by the suspending authority or port compliance officer. Null when not suspended. Required for ISPS Code enforcement documentation and Port State Control audit readiness.']
  vehicle_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official vehicle registration plate number for vehicle-level access permits. Populated only when scope_level is \'vehicle\'. Used for automated gate access control, RFID matching, and port security zone vehicle authorisation in NAVIS N4 gate operations.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle for which the access permit is issued. Populated only when scope_level is \'vehicle\'. Determines applicable port zones, weight restrictions, and operational areas within the terminal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truck|trailer|forklift|reach_stacker|agv|private_vehicle|motorcycle|other — promote to reference product]']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the accreditation or document has been independently verified against the issuing authority\'s records. Unverified indicates the document has been received but not yet checked. Verified indicates successful confirmation with the issuing authority. Verification Failed indicates the document could not be confirmed as authentic. Pending Verification indicates verification has been initiated and is in progress.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all accreditations, certifications, permits, official documents, and access credentials held by port community participants and their personnel/vehicles. Covers entity-level accreditations (ISPS facility security, customs broker licensing, freight forwarder registration, dangerous goods handling certification, stevedoring company accreditation), official documents (trade licences, insurance certificates including P&I cover, vessel operator certificates), and individual-level permits (port access permits for personnel and vehicles, ISPS security zone access authorisations, biometric-linked access credentials, contractor access passes). Captures document/certificate type, scope level (entity, individual, vehicle), permit holder reference, issuing authority, certificate/document number, issue date, expiry date, renewal status, verification status, file reference, security zone access level, biometric reference, revocation status, and linked compliance requirements. This product subsumes what were previously separate participant_document and port_access_permit concepts — all official documents and access permits are managed here as a single SSOT. Critical for compliance gating in PCS and VTMS workflows, ISPS Code enforcement, port zone access control, and audit readiness.'
}

Table customer.sla_profile {
  sla_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SLA profile record. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: SLA profiles in port operations are frequently cargo-category-specific: reefer cargo has different dwell time SLAs than dry bulk or dangerous goods. Linking sla_profile to cargo_category enables categ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: SLA Penalty Cost Allocation: when penalty_amount_applied is recorded on an SLA profile, the cost must be allocated to a responsible cost centre for management accounting. Port finance requires cost ce']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: SLA profiles are scoped to specific terminals in port operator contracts (e.g., crane productivity SLA at Terminal A). The sla_profile has port_location_id but not terminal_id. Terminal-level SLA prof']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) to whom this SLA profile is assigned. Links to the customer master in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and Port Community System (PCS).']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: SLAs are often berth-specific or terminal-specific (container terminal vs bulk terminal); performance measurement, crane availability commitments, and turnaround time targets require precise location ']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: SLA profiles are defined against specific port service codes (crane moves, gate processing, reefer monitoring). Linking sla_profile to service_code enables SLA target-setting per billable service, dri']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: sla_profile has imo_number as plain text — a clear denormalization of vessel_master. Vessel-specific SLA profiles (e.g., named vessel turnaround guarantees negotiated by shipping lines) require a FK t']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: SLA targets vary fundamentally by vessel category (container, bulk, tanker); turnaround time commitments, pilotage requirements, and berth allocation priorities differ by vessel type in port service a']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The measured actual performance value for the metric_type during the measurement period. For example, actual vessel turnaround time of 26.5 hours, actual container dwell time of 4.2 days, or actual gate processing time of 12 minutes. Expressed in unit_of_measure. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS, VTMS, or PCS operational data.']
  bol_reference varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading reference number associated with the cargo or container for which this SLA measurement applies. Relevant for cargo handling, documentation processing, and customs clearance SLA types. Links to PCS cargo manifest and trade documentation records.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of SLA breach events recorded within the current measurement_window period. Used to determine if escalating penalty tiers apply (e.g., first breach = warning, third breach = critical escalation) and to track chronic underperformance patterns.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual_value exceeded the breach_threshold for this measurement period, constituting a formal SLA breach. When True, triggers penalty/credit calculation workflows in SAP S/4HANA FI and escalation notifications in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity level when a breach of the SLA threshold occurs. Critical breaches trigger immediate escalation and maximum penalty/credit terms; warning-level breaches initiate remediation planning without financial penalty. Used in SLA breach alerting and escalation workflows.']
  breach_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The value at which an SLA breach is formally triggered. May differ from target_value to allow a tolerance band (e.g., target vessel turnaround = 24 hours, breach threshold = 28 hours). Expressed in the same unit_of_measure as target_value. Used for automated breach alerting in NAVIS N4 and PCS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this SLA profile record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance, lineage tracking, and compliance reporting in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  credit_cap_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum cumulative credit or penalty amount that can be applied within a single measurement_window period, expressed in penalty_currency_code. Protects the port operator from unlimited financial exposure under a single SLA agreement. Confidential commercial contract term.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this SLA profile definition ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended profiles. When populated, triggers profile_status transition to \'expired\'. Aligns with contract expiry management in SAP S/4HANA and Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this SLA profile definition becomes binding and applicable to assigned service agreements. Aligns with contract commencement dates in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and SAP S/4HANA contract management.']
  escalation_contact varchar [note: 'Name or role designation of the primary escalation contact responsible for managing SLA breach resolution on behalf of the port operator. Typically a Port Operations Manager or Commercial Manager. Confidential internal business contact information.']
  force_majeure_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether force majeure clauses apply to this SLA profile, suspending breach penalties during declared force majeure events (e.g., severe weather, port closures, ISPS security incidents). When True, breach measurements are paused during force majeure periods.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this SLA profile record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer, change data capture (CDC) processing, and audit trail requirements.']
  measurement_period_end timestamp [note: 'The precise end timestamp of the performance measurement period for this SLA record. For vessel-call SLAs, this is the Actual Time of Departure (ATD); for gate processing SLAs, this is the gate-out timestamp. Captured from NAVIS N4 TOS or VTMS event feeds.']
  measurement_period_start timestamp [note: 'The precise start timestamp of the performance measurement period for this SLA record. For vessel-call SLAs, this is the Actual Time of Arrival (ATA); for gate processing SLAs, this is the gate-in timestamp. Captured from NAVIS N4 TOS or VTMS event feeds.']
  measurement_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which the actual_value measurement was sourced. Ensures data lineage traceability for SLA disputes and audit purposes. NAVIS N4 is the primary source for container terminal metrics; VTMS for vessel operations; PCS for documentation processing times. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAVIS_N4|VTMS|PCS|TOPS|SAP|DYNAMICS_CRM|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp at which the SLA performance measurement was recorded or calculated. Provides the exact point-in-time reference for the actual_value observation. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS event timestamps, VTMS vessel event feeds, or PCS document processing logs.']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'The time window over which SLA performance is measured and evaluated. \'per_call\' applies to individual vessel calls or gate transactions; \'daily\', \'weekly\', \'monthly\' apply to aggregated operational periods. Determines the frequency of SLA reporting cycles.']
  metric_type varchar [note: 'The specific performance metric being governed by this SLA profile (e.g., \'vessel_turnaround_time\', \'container_dwell_time\', \'gate_in_processing_time\', \'documentation_processing_window\', \'billing_dispute_resolution_time\', \'crane_productivity_moves_per_hour\', \'customs_clearance_time\'). Aligns with IAPH KPI framework and NAVIS N4 performance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_turnaround_time|container_dwell_time|gate_processing_time|documentation_processing_window|billing_dispute_resolution_time|crane_productivity|customs_clearance_time|berth_occupancy_time — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational context, commercial commentary, or exception notes related to this SLA profile or measurement record. Used by port commercial managers and operations teams to document special circumstances, dispute context, or improvement initiatives.']
  penalty_amount_applied decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual financial penalty or credit amount applied for this specific breach event, expressed in penalty_currency_code. May differ from the standard penalty_value if partial waivers, caps, or commercial adjustments were applied. Feeds into SAP S/4HANA FI credit memo or debit note processing.']
  penalty_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which the penalty_value is calculated when an SLA breach occurs. \'per_event\' applies a flat fee per breach; \'per_hour\' or \'per_day\' applies a rate for each unit of time in breach; \'per_teu\' applies to container volume; \'percentage_of_invoice\' applies a percentage of the relevant THC or service charge.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the penalty_value (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD, AUD). Ensures correct financial processing in SAP S/4HANA FI for multi-currency port operations.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'The type of financial remedy applied when an SLA breach occurs. \'credit\' reduces future invoices; \'rebate\' is a cash payment back to the participant; \'liquidated_damages\' are contractually pre-agreed fixed amounts per breach event; \'none\' indicates a non-financial SLA with reporting obligations only.']
  penalty_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The financial penalty or credit amount applied per SLA breach event, expressed in the currency defined by penalty_currency_code. For percentage-based penalties, this represents the percentage of the applicable invoice or THC charge. Confidential as it reflects commercial contract terms.']
  penalty_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification for waiving or reducing the standard penalty_value for a breach event. Common reasons include force majeure, port congestion beyond operator control, mutual agreement, or first-time breach goodwill waiver. Required for audit trail in SAP S/4HANA and compliance reporting.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the SLA profile, used in Port Community System (PCS) messaging, EDI exchanges, and customer-facing documentation. Follows the format SLA-[ALPHANUMERIC].']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA profile (e.g., \'Premium Shipping Line Tier 1\', \'Standard Freight Forwarder SLA\'). Used in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service agreement records and customer-facing communications.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA profile definition. \'draft\' indicates the profile is under review; \'active\' means it is currently governing service agreements; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused (e.g., during force majeure); \'expired\' means the profile has passed its effective_end_date.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA profile by operational domain. Determines which port business process the SLA governs: vessel operations (turnaround, berthing), container terminal (dwell time, crane productivity), gate processing, documentation (customs, manifests), billing dispute resolution, or cargo handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_operations|container_terminal|gate_processing|documentation|billing_dispute|cargo_handling — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective or remediation action taken in response to an SLA breach or near-breach event. Examples include \'Additional quay crane deployed\', \'Gate lane capacity increased\', \'Vessel scheduling adjusted\'. Captured in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service request records.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which the remediation_action must be completed and verified. Drives service request SLA tracking in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM and ensures timely resolution of performance issues.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation action for an SLA breach event. \'open\' indicates no action taken yet; \'in_progress\' means corrective measures are underway; \'completed\' means the issue is resolved; \'waived\' means the breach penalty has been waived by commercial agreement; \'escalated\' means the issue has been raised to senior management.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Identifies which party bears primary responsibility for meeting the SLA target. In maritime logistics, responsibility may rest with the port operator (crane productivity, berth allocation), the shipping line (vessel arrival punctuality), or be shared between parties. Determines penalty applicability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port_operator|shipping_line|freight_forwarder|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|shared — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The scheduled frequency at which this SLA profile is formally reviewed with the port community participant for performance assessment and potential renegotiation. Drives SLA governance calendar in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification of the SLA profile reflecting the service commitment level offered to the port community participant. Platinum and Gold tiers typically apply to major shipping lines with high TEU volumes; Bronze and Standard apply to smaller freight forwarders or occasional users.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The committed performance target value for the metric defined in metric_type. For example, a vessel turnaround time target of 24 (hours), a container dwell time limit of 5 (days), or a gate processing time of 15 (minutes). Interpreted in conjunction with unit_of_measure.']
  teu_volume decimal(10,2) [note: 'The TEU volume of containers associated with this SLA measurement event. Used for per-TEU penalty calculations, throughput-based SLA assessments, and volume-weighted performance reporting. Sourced from NAVIS N4 TOS container movement records.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure applicable to target_value, breach_threshold, and actual_value fields. Standard maritime logistics units include hours (vessel turnaround), minutes (gate processing), days (container dwell time), moves_per_hour (crane productivity), TEU per day (terminal throughput), percentage (utilisation), or count (number of incidents). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|minutes|days|moves_per_hour|teu_per_day|percentage|count — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_from_target decimal(18,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between actual_value and target_value (actual_value minus target_value). A positive variance indicates underperformance (actual exceeded target); a negative variance indicates overperformance. Expressed in unit_of_measure. Used for SLA performance trending and continuous improvement analysis.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for Service Level Agreement definitions, targets, and measured performance outcomes for port community participants. Defines performance commitments including vessel turnaround time targets, container dwell time limits, gate processing time SLAs, documentation processing windows, and billing dispute resolution timeframes. Captures SLA tier name, metric type, target value, unit of measure, measurement window, breach threshold, and penalty/credit terms. Also stores all operational performance measurement records: measurement period, actual value, variance from target, breach flag, breach severity, remediation action taken, responsible party, and measurement timestamp. This product subsumes what was previously a separate sla_performance concept — both SLA definitions and their measured outcomes are managed here as a single SSOT. Assigned to participants via service agreements. Enables SLA reporting, penalty/credit calculation, performance trending, breach alerting, and continuous improvement tracking.'
}

Table customer.sla_performance {
  sla_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SLA performance measurement record. Primary key for the sla_performance data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: SLA performance measurement and penalty calculation require cost centre attribution for management reporting, operational efficiency analysis, and cost recovery. Essential for terminal-level P&L and s']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: SLA metrics for customs clearance time (submission to release) must reference the specific declaration. Port operators measure and report customs processing performance as a key service level indicato']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Business justification: Customs holds directly cause SLA performance breaches (dwell time, vessel turnaround KPIs). Port operations attribute SLA breaches to specific holds for penalty calculations, dispute resolution, and f']
  handling_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.handling_order. Business justification: SLA performance for terminal operations (crane productivity, vessel turnaround, move completion rates) is measured against specific handling orders. SLA breach reports and penalty calculations in port']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: SLA performance measurements are terminal-specific in port operator contracts (crane productivity, berth utilization, gate throughput per terminal). The sla_performance has port_location_id but not te']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: SLA measurement, penalty calculation, and credit application are tracked at account level for participants with multiple service accounts. Enables account-specific performance reporting and penalty in']
  penalty_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: SLA Penalty Revenue/Cost Posting: sla_performance records penalty_amount and penalty_applicable_flag; the GL account for posting SLA penalty income or credit notes must be traceable for audit and reve']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) whose SLA performance is being tracked.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: SLA performance is measured at specific terminals or berths; root cause analysis, remediation planning, and capacity constraint identification require location-level granularity for operational improv']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: SLA breach penalty calculation requires knowing which rate card\'s penalty terms apply to a measured performance event. Rate cards contain penalty_clause_description and sla_tier; linking sla_performan']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: SLA performance records measure actual vs target for a specific port service. Linking to service_code replaces the denormalized metric_name/service_type plain-text fields with a canonical reference, e']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_profile. Business justification: sla_performance records track actual measured outcomes AGAINST a defined sla_profile definition. The sla_profile is the authoritative SLA contract (targets, thresholds, penalty rules, metric type), wh']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vessel port call associated with this SLA measurement, where applicable (e.g., for vessel turnaround time, berth waiting time, or crane productivity metrics). Null for non-vessel-related SLA metrics such as documentation or billing.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Performance measurement and breach analysis must segment by vessel category; operational benchmarks, turnaround time standards, and penalty calculations differ significantly across container, bulk, an']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured performance value achieved during the measurement period for this SLA metric. Sourced from operational systems such as NAVIS N4 TOS, VTMS, or Port Community System. Compared against target_value to determine compliance.']
  breach_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the actual value exceeded or fell below the SLA target. Critical for metrics where both over and under performance may be contractually significant (e.g., crane productivity above target is positive, but vessel turnaround time above target is a breach).']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA measurement event constitutes a breach of the committed target. True when actual performance fails to meet the contractual SLA threshold. Drives penalty/credit calculation and escalation workflows in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the SLA breach when breach_flag is True. Drives escalation priority, penalty tier, and remediation urgency. \'none\' applies when no breach occurred. Severity tiers are defined in the service agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA performance measurement record was first created in the system. Represents the audit creation timestamp, distinct from the measurement_event_timestamp which captures the real-world business event time.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the port community participant was formally notified of this SLA breach or performance event. Populated only when customer_notified_flag is True. Supports contractual notification timeline compliance.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port community participant has been formally notified of this SLA breach event. Notification may be via Port Community System (PCS) messaging, EDI, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM email. Required for contractual transparency obligations.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the port community participant type for this SLA performance record. Enables segment-level SLA reporting and benchmarking. Denormalized from the customer profile for analytical efficiency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|consignee|importer|exporter|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent — promote to reference product]']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the actual_value for this SLA measurement was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and auditability. \'MANUAL\' indicates the value was entered manually by port operations staff. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAVIS_N4|VTMS|PCS|TOPS|SAP_S4HANA|DYNAMICS_365|MAXIMO|MANUAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has formally disputed this SLA measurement or breach determination. When True, the measurement_status should be \'disputed\' and the record is under review. Drives dispute resolution workflow in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the formal dispute case raised by the customer against this SLA measurement. Links to the dispute case in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM. Populated only when dispute_flag is True.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA breach has been escalated to senior management, the commercial team, or the customer relationship manager. Triggered by critical breach severity or repeated breaches within a measurement period.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA breach was formally escalated. Populated only when escalation_flag is True. Used for tracking escalation response times and SLA governance compliance.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA performance measurement record was most recently modified. Tracks updates such as status changes, dispute flags, waiver grants, or remediation action updates. Supports Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  measurement_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world timestamp at which this SLA performance measurement was captured or triggered. Represents the principal business event time distinct from audit timestamps. For example, the moment a vessel berth operation completed and the metric was evaluated.']
  measurement_period_end timestamp [note: 'The end date and time of the measurement period over which SLA performance is evaluated. Defines the closing boundary of the observation window.']
  measurement_period_start timestamp [note: 'The start date and time of the measurement period over which SLA performance is evaluated. Defines the beginning boundary of the observation window (e.g., start of a vessel call, start of a billing cycle, start of a gate operation window).']
  measurement_period_type varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity and type of the measurement period. \'vessel_call\' — measured per individual vessel port call; \'per_transaction\' — measured per individual cargo or gate transaction; \'daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly\' — calendar-based aggregation periods as defined in the service agreement.']
  measurement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this SLA performance measurement event, used for cross-referencing with CRM service records, billing disputes, and customer communications. Sourced from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM SLA KPI Instance ID.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this SLA performance measurement record. \'pending\' — awaiting data capture; \'measured\' — raw value recorded; \'validated\' — confirmed by operations; \'disputed\' — customer has raised a dispute; \'waived\' — breach waived per agreement terms; \'cancelled\' — measurement voided.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the SLA metric target and actual values. Examples include \'hours\', \'minutes\', \'moves_per_hour\', \'TEU_per_day\', \'percentage\', \'days\', \'count\'. Ensures consistent interpretation of target_value and actual_value fields.']
  metric_category varchar [note: 'Operational category classifying the SLA metric into a port business domain. Enables domain-level SLA reporting and benchmarking across vessel operations, terminal operations, cargo handling, documentation, billing, and marine services.']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA metric being measured, such as \'Vessel Turnaround Time\', \'Container Gate-In Processing Time\', \'Berth Waiting Time\', \'Crane Productivity (moves/hour)\', \'Documentation Processing Time\', or \'Cargo Availability Notification Lead Time\'.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial penalty or service credit amount applicable for this SLA breach, expressed in the contract currency. Calculated per the penalty schedule in the service agreement. Zero when no penalty applies. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of contractual penalty terms.']
  penalty_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial penalty or service credit is contractually applicable for this SLA breach event. Determined by the service agreement penalty clauses. Drives the penalty/credit calculation workflow in SAP S/4HANA FI and billing in NAVIS N4.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD, AUD). Ensures correct financial processing in SAP S/4HANA FI when multi-currency contracts are in place.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective or remediation action taken in response to the SLA breach or performance deviation. Examples include \'Additional crane deployed\', \'Priority berth allocation granted\', \'Expedited customs clearance arranged\', \'Service credit issued\'. Supports continuous improvement tracking.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation action for this SLA breach event. \'not_required\' — no breach or waived; \'pending\' — action identified but not started; \'in_progress\' — action underway; \'completed\' — action resolved; \'escalated\' — escalated to senior management or commercial team.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause of an SLA breach or near-miss. Enables systemic analysis and continuous improvement tracking. \'port_operations\' — internal operational failure; \'equipment_failure\' — crane, RTG, AGV, or STS breakdown; \'weather\' — adverse weather or sea conditions; \'third_party\' — customs, pilotage, or towage delay; \'customer_delay\' — cargo or documentation delay by the customer; \'regulatory\' — PSC inspection or customs hold; \'it_system\' — TOS, VTMS, or PCS system outage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port_operations|equipment_failure|weather|third_party|customer_delay|regulatory|it_system|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific root cause of the SLA breach or performance deviation. Provides operational context beyond the categorical root_cause_category for incident investigation and continuous improvement reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The specific port service type to which this SLA measurement applies, such as \'Berth Allocation\', \'Container Discharge\', \'Container Loading\', \'Gate Processing\', \'Pilotage\', \'Towage\', \'Documentation\', \'Customs Clearance\', \'Intermodal Transfer\'. Aligns with the service catalogue in the service agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: berth_allocation|container_discharge|container_loading|gate_processing|pilotage|towage|documentation|customs_clearance|intermodal_transfer|yard_management — promote to reference product]']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The transaction or record identifier from the source operational system (e.g., NAVIS N4 vessel visit ID, VTMS port call number, PCS cargo manifest reference) that was used to derive the actual_value for this SLA measurement. Supports data lineage and audit trail.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The committed SLA target value as defined in the service agreement for this metric and measurement period. Represents the performance threshold the port has contractually committed to deliver (e.g., vessel turnaround ≤ 24 hours, crane productivity ≥ 25 moves/hour).']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the port operations staff member or system process that validated this SLA measurement record. Populated when measurement_status transitions to \'validated\'. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this SLA measurement record was formally validated by port operations staff or an automated validation process. Populated when measurement_status transitions to \'validated\'.']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of the target value ((actual - target) / target * 100). Enables proportional comparison of SLA deviations across metrics with different units and scales. Stored as a raw operational measurement field captured at the time of evaluation.']
  variance_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The arithmetic difference between actual_value and target_value (actual minus target). A positive variance may indicate overperformance or underperformance depending on the metric direction (e.g., for time-based metrics, positive variance means slower than target). Stored as a raw operational field, not a derived KPI.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA breach for this measurement event has been formally waived by the port authority. Waivers may be granted for force majeure events, weather disruptions, or commercial goodwill. When True, penalty_applicable_flag should be False.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of the reason a breach waiver was granted. Common reasons include force majeure (weather, natural disaster), equipment breakdown, third-party delay (customs, pilotage), or commercial agreement. Populated only when waiver_flag is True.']

  Note: 'Operational records tracking actual SLA performance against committed targets for each port community participant and service agreement. Captures measurement period, SLA metric, target value, actual value, variance, breach flag, breach severity, and remediation action taken. Enables SLA reporting, penalty/credit calculation, and continuous improvement tracking. Distinct from analytics — this is the raw operational performance record per measurement event.'
}

Port compliance teams track audit-driven service requests to close ']

Direct FK enables lookup of shipment details, consignee verification, freight terms, and cargo manifest for resolving documentation disputes, delivery or']

Linking service_request to contact_perso']
  container_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.container. Business justification: Service requests reference container_number as text. Direct FK enables operational lookup of container status, location, customs holds, and damage records when resolving customer inquiries. Critical f']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Service requests (disputes, operational complaints, special handling) consume port resources tracked by cost centres for internal cost allocation, management reporting, and operational efficiency anal']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Service requests for customs clearance delays, declaration amendments, or duty disputes must reference the specific declaration. Customer service teams need this link to investigate and resolve custom']
  customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold. Port customer service requires this link to coordinate hold resolution with customs author']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.delivery_order. DO-related service requests require direct linkage to the delivery_order for case ']

  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal.   marpol_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.marpol_record. Business justification: Service requests and disputes often relate to specific billing accounts rather than participant entity. Multi-account participants require account-level service tracking for billing disputes, SLA brea']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) who lodged the service request.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Service requests are location-specific (crane breakdown at Terminal 3, gate congestion at North Gate); dispatch, resource allocation, and resolution tracking require precise location context for opera']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Security incidents (cargo theft, unauthorized access, vessel security breach) routinely trigger customer service requests for affected cargo owners, vessel operators, or freight forwarders. This FK li']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: Port service requests (reefer monitoring, dangerous goods handling, vessel berthing) are raised against specific service codes. This FK drives billing trigger identification (tos_billing_trigger on se']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: Customer service cases (cargo condition disputes, delivery issues, release queries) are raised at the shipment level. Port customer service operations require linking service requests directly to ship']
  vessel_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.call. Business justification: Service requests for vessel-related issues (special cargo handling, dispute resolution, operational complaints) must be traceable to the specific port call. Port customer service teams use this link f']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: service_request has imo_number as plain text — strong denormalization signal. Port service requests (vessel inspections, dangerous goods manifests, ISPS queries) are raised in context of a specific ve']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Service requests often relate to vessel-type-specific issues (reefer power for container vessels, ballast water for bulk carriers); request categorization, routing, and resolution depend on vessel typ']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the port formally acknowledged receipt of the service request to the customer. Used to measure SLA first-response time compliance against contracted service level agreements.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the service request was originally lodged by the customer. Includes the Port Community System (PCS) portal, email, telephone, in-person at the port office, EDI message, or fax. Used for channel analytics and digital transformation reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: portal|email|phone|in_person|pcs|edi|fax — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally closed after resolution confirmation or customer acceptance. Distinct from resolved_timestamp — a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer confirmation before closure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this service request record was first created in the data platform. Distinct from lodgement_timestamp which captures the business event time. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.']
  crm_case_reference varchar [note: 'The native case identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, the system of record for service requests. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the dispute_amount field. Required for ports operating in multiple currencies or handling international billing disputes involving BAF, CAF, or foreign-currency THC charges.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer, typically on a scale of 1 to 5. Captured via automated survey sent after case closure. Used for port service quality KPI reporting and continuous improvement programmes.']
  dispute_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value under dispute for billing disputes, demurrage (DMG) claims, detention (DET) charges, Terminal Handling Charge (THC) disputes, or wharfage (WHR) fee challenges. Null for non-financial service requests. Expressed in the port\'s operating currency.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request has been formally escalated to a higher authority or management tier due to complexity, SLA breach, customer dissatisfaction, or regulatory implications (e.g., PSC complaint, ISPS security concern).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for escalation of the service request. Supports root cause analysis of escalation patterns and identification of systemic operational issues.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally escalated. Null if escalation_flag is false. Used to track escalation response times and management intervention patterns.']
