  imdg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request involves dangerous goods classified under the IMDG Code, including misdeclaration complaints, handling incidents, documentation disputes, or storage compliance issues for hazardous cargo.']
  isps_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request relates to an ISPS Code security matter, including access control breaches, security level changes, security incidents, or ISPS compliance complaints. Triggers mandatory security officer review and potential regulatory notification.']
  lodgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the service request was formally lodged by the customer or captured in the system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor point for all SLA response time calculations.']
  pcs_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the originating Port Community System (PCS) message (e.g., COPARN, IFTMIN, or stakeholder message) that triggered or is associated with this service request. Enables traceability back to the PCS transaction for EDI reconciliation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the service request, determining SLA response and resolution targets. Critical priority applies to vessel safety, ISPS security incidents, or cargo at imminent risk. High applies to operational disruptions affecting vessel or terminal schedules.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the service request at lodgement. Used in all customer-facing communications, Port Community System (PCS) messaging, and EDI exchanges. Format: SR-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority to which this service request must be or has been notified (e.g., IMO, National Maritime Safety Authority, Port State Control, WCO, ILO). Populated when regulatory_notification_required is true.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service request requires formal notification to a regulatory authority such as the National Maritime Safety Authority, Port State Control (PSC), IMO, or customs authority. Triggered for ISPS security incidents, MARPOL environmental complaints, SOLAS safety violations, or customs fraud allegations.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this service request has been reopened after a previous resolution or closure. A high reopen count indicates unresolved root causes or customer dissatisfaction with the initial resolution. Used in quality and recurrence analysis.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service request from lodgement through investigation to resolution. Drives workflow routing and SLA monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|in_progress|pending_customer|resolved|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the customer interaction. Distinguishes between general enquiries, formal complaints, billing disputes, operational service requests, incident reports, permit applications, and documentation requests. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enquiry|complaint|dispute|service_request|incident_report|permit_application|documentation_request — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardised outcome code applied at case closure indicating how the service request was resolved (e.g., resolved_in_favour_of_customer, resolved_in_favour_of_port, partial_credit_issued, no_action_required, referred_to_regulator, withdrawn_by_customer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resolved_customer_favour|resolved_port_favour|partial_credit|no_action|referred_regulator|withdrawn|information_provided — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the resolution actions taken, findings of investigation, decisions made, and any remedial actions or credits applied. Captured by the assigned case handler at resolution. Forms part of the audit trail for regulatory and compliance purposes.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally resolved and a resolution communicated to the customer. Used to calculate total resolution time and SLA compliance for resolution targets.']
  service_request_category varchar [note: 'Primary business domain category of the service request (e.g., Vessel Operations, Container Handling, Billing and Tariffs, Customs Clearance, Gate Operations, Yard Management, Berth Allocation, Marine Services). Aligns with port operational domains for routing and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_operations|container_handling|billing_tariffs|customs_clearance|gate_operations|yard_management|berth_allocation|marine_services|infrastructure|safety — promote to reference product]']
  service_request_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the service request, complaint, dispute, or enquiry as provided by the customer. Captures the complete context of the issue including relevant operational details such as vessel name, container numbers, BOL references, or terminal locations.']
  sla_resolution_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA resolution target was breached for this service request. True if the resolved_timestamp exceeded sla_resolution_target or if the case remains unresolved past the target. Used for SLA compliance reporting and penalty calculations.']
  sla_resolution_target timestamp [note: 'The contractual deadline by which the port must fully resolve this service request, calculated from lodgement_timestamp based on the applicable SLA tier, request type, and priority level.']
  sla_response_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA first-response target was breached for this service request. True if the acknowledged_timestamp exceeded sla_response_target or if no acknowledgement was sent before the target. Used for SLA compliance reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  sla_response_target timestamp [note: 'The contractual deadline by which the port must send an initial response to the customer for this service request, calculated from lodgement_timestamp based on the applicable SLA tier and priority level.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular context within the primary category. Examples include: THC dispute, demurrage claim, container damage, vessel delay, ISPS access, dangerous goods documentation, BOL amendment. Supports detailed analytics and specialist routing.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, descriptive title summarising the nature of the service request as provided by the customer or captured by the port agent at lodgement. Used in case lists, dashboards, and customer communications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this service request record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all customer-initiated service requests, enquiries, formal complaints, disputes, and case management records raised by port community participants. Covers the full case lifecycle from lodgement through investigation to resolution.'
}

Table customer.credit_assessment {
  credit_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credit assessment record within the port community participant credit management system.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Credit Limit Management by Legal Entity: credit limits are approved and governed per company code in multi-entity port operators (e.g., container terminal vs. bulk terminal subsidiary). Finance and ri']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Credit assessments for importers/exporters reference specific customs declaration history — duty payment compliance, declaration volumes, rejection rates — as part of creditworthiness methodology. Por']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Credit assessments evaluate specific accounts, not just participants. Large shipping lines or freight forwarders maintain multiple accounts with different credit profiles, payment terms, and risk rati']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, or port agent) for whom this credit assessment is conducted.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Credit Risk by Business Unit reporting: port operators segment credit exposure by profit centre (e.g., container terminal vs. ro-ro terminal). Finance risk reports require credit_assessment linked to ']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Credit assessments explicitly evaluate compliance violation history — penalty amounts, recurrence flags, payment status — when determining risk category and credit limits. Port credit teams reference ']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'The port community participant\'s most recently reported annual revenue, sourced from submitted financial statements and used as a key input to the credit assessment and limit determination.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which the port community participant formally submitted the credit application or the credit assessment was initiated by the commercial team.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit assessment was formally approved by the authorised approver, marking the point from which the credit limit becomes effective.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'The organisational approval authority level at which the credit assessment was approved, reflecting the port\'s delegated authority framework based on credit limit thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_officer|credit_manager|finance_director|cfo|board — promote to reference product]']
  approved_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final credit limit formally approved by the authorised approver, which may differ from the assessed limit following management review or escalation. This is the enforceable credit ceiling for the participant.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorised approver who approved or rejected the credit assessment, captured at the time of decision for audit trail and governance reporting.']
  assessed_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit amount determined by the assessor following evaluation of the participant\'s financial standing, payment history, and risk profile. This is the operative limit used by the billing domain for credit control.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit assessor formally conducted and completed the credit evaluation, distinct from the application submission date.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'The methodology or combination of methods used to conduct the credit assessment, such as financial statement analysis, external credit bureau reports, trade references, or the port\'s internal scoring model. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_statement_analysis|credit_bureau_report|trade_reference|internal_scoring_model|combined|external_agency — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes recorded by the assessor documenting key findings, risk factors, qualitative observations, and rationale for the credit decision. Supports audit trail and future review context.']
  assessment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference number assigned to this credit assessment, used in correspondence with the port community participant and referenced in billing and commercial onboarding workflows.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit assessment record, tracking its progression from initial submission through review, approval or rejection, and eventual expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|expired|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit assessment indicating whether it is an initial assessment for new account onboarding, a scheduled periodic review, a triggered review due to payment behaviour changes, or a credit limit adjustment request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial|periodic_review|triggered_review|limit_increase|limit_decrease|onboarding — promote to reference product]']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the credit assessor who conducted the evaluation, captured at the time of assessment for audit and reporting purposes. Complements the assessor_employee_id for human-readable audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this credit assessment record was first created in the system, used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting purposes.']
  credit_bureau_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or report identifier from an external credit bureau or trade credit agency report obtained as part of the assessment process. Enables traceability back to the source credit report.']
  credit_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of any trade credit insurance arrangement covering the port community participant\'s credit exposure, where the port has obtained insurance to mitigate default risk. Null if no insurance is in place.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal credit rating assigned to the port community participant following assessment, using a standardised rating scale aligned with international credit rating conventions. Drives review frequency and credit terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  credit_score decimal(6,2) [note: 'Numeric credit score derived from the assessment methodology, representing the port community participant\'s creditworthiness on the port\'s internal scoring scale. Used to determine credit rating tier and approved limit.']
  crm_opportunity_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM opportunity or account onboarding record that initiated this credit assessment, enabling traceability between commercial pipeline activities and credit decisions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limits and financial assessments are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD). Aligns with the port\'s billing currency configuration in SAP S/4HANA.']
  days_past_due_count varchar [note: 'The number of days the port community participant\'s outstanding invoices were past due at the time of assessment, sourced from SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable. A key input to the credit scoring model.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which the assessed credit limit and terms become binding and active for the port community participant, used by the billing domain for credit control enforcement.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The date on which the current credit assessment expires and a review must be conducted. Nullable for open-ended credit arrangements. Triggers automated review workflow in the commercial team.']
  financial_statement_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year of the financial statements submitted by the port community participant as supporting documentation for the credit assessment (e.g., 2023). Used to assess recency and relevance of financial data.']
  is_watch_list boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the port community participant has been placed on the credit watch list due to deteriorating payment behaviour, adverse financial news, or elevated risk signals, triggering enhanced monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this credit assessment record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail, change tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic credit review of the port community participant, set based on credit rating tier and risk policy. Used to trigger review workflows in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total outstanding accounts receivable balance owed by the port community participant at the time of the credit assessment, sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI. Used as a key input to limit utilisation and risk evaluation.']
  payment_history_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the port community participant\'s historical payment behaviour based on analysis of invoice settlement patterns in SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable. A primary input to the credit scoring model.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms code assigned to the port community participant based on the credit assessment outcome, defining the number of days allowed for invoice settlement (e.g., NET30, NET60, IMMEDIATE). Feeds directly into NAVIS N4 billing configuration.']
  previous_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit that was in effect prior to this assessment, enabling tracking of credit limit changes over time and trend analysis of the participant\'s credit standing.']
  previous_credit_rating varchar [note: 'The credit rating assigned in the immediately preceding assessment, enabling tracking of rating migrations and trend analysis. Null for initial assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Structured reason code or narrative explanation for the rejection of a credit application or reduction of a requested credit limit. Populated only when assessment_status is \'rejected\'. Used for participant communication and regulatory reporting.']
  requested_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The credit limit amount requested by the port community participant in their credit application, expressed in the port\'s operating currency. Used as the baseline for the assessor\'s evaluation.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Broad risk classification assigned to the port community participant based on the credit assessment outcome, used to determine credit control measures, payment terms, and security deposit requirements.']
  sap_credit_account_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA customer credit account number associated with this assessment, enabling direct linkage between the credit assessment record and the operative credit master data in the ERP system.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the security deposit required from the port community participant as a condition of credit approval, denominated in the currency specified by currency_code. Null if no deposit is required.']
  security_deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit assessment outcome requires the port community participant to lodge a security deposit (cash, bank guarantee, or letter of credit) before credit facilities are activated.']
  security_deposit_type varchar [note: 'The form of security instrument lodged or required from the port community participant as collateral for the credit facility, such as cash deposit, bank guarantee, or letter of credit.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document management system path for the supporting financial documents submitted with the credit application (e.g., audited financial statements, bank references, trade references). Enables document retrieval for audit.']
  watch_list_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason the port community participant was placed on the credit watch list. Populated only when is_watch_list is True. Used for enhanced monitoring and escalation decisions.']

  Note: 'Credit assessment records for port community participants, capturing credit application details, assessed credit limit, credit rating, assessment methodology, supporting financial documents, assessor identity, assessment date, approval status, and next review date. Tracks credit history and limit changes over time. Used by the billing domain for credit control and by commercial teams for account onboarding decisions.'
}

Table customer.relationship_manager {
  relationship_manager_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each relationship manager assignment record linking a port community participant to a designated port organisation staff member.']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Relationship managers are assigned to manage specific terminal operator relationships. The relationship_manager product carries terminal_code as plain text — a clear denormalization of the terminal en']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant (shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, customs broker, trucking company, port agent, etc.) assigned to this relationship manager.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: relationship_manager has port_code as plain text — denormalization of port_location. In multi-port operators, relationship managers are assigned to specific port locations. FK to port_location replace']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Key Account Revenue Reporting: relationship managers carry annual_revenue_target and annual_teu_volume_target that must be attributed to a profit centre for P&L performance tracking. Port finance team']
  annual_revenue_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual revenue target in the port\'s base currency assigned to this relationship manager for the customer account. Supports commercial performance management and EBITDA contribution tracking.']
  annual_teu_volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'The agreed annual container volume target in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for this customer under the current relationship manager assignment. Used for commercial planning, KPI tracking, and revenue forecasting.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment is scheduled to end or was terminated. Null for open-ended active assignments. Used for succession planning and historical reporting.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for creating or modifying this relationship manager assignment. Supports audit trail, workforce planning, and CRM change management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_customer|reassignment|staff_departure|restructure|volume_growth|escalation|rotation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assignment_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business reference code for this assignment record, used in CRM workflow routing, correspondence, and escalation management. Format: RM-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment became effective. Used for tenure tracking, SLA accountability, and CRM reporting.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the relationship manager assignment. Active indicates the assignment is current and operational; pending indicates awaiting formal activation; suspended indicates temporarily paused; terminated indicates the assignment has ended.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the relationship manager assignment. Permanent indicates a long-term designated assignment; interim indicates a temporary cover arrangement; acting indicates a stand-in during absence; backup indicates a secondary escalation contact; co_managed indicates shared responsibility.']
  churn_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been flagged as at risk of reducing or ceasing port service usage. Set by the relationship manager or CRM system based on engagement signals, volume trends, or competitor activity. Triggers escalation workflows.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the relationship manager assignment covers all port services or is restricted to specific service lines such as container terminal operations, vessel operations, cargo handling, intermodal logistics, marine services, or trade compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_services|container_terminal|vessel_operations|cargo_handling|intermodal|marine_services|trade_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship manager assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  crm_owner_team varchar [note: 'The name of the CRM team or business unit within the port organisation responsible for this relationship assignment. Used for CRM workflow routing, queue management, and team-level performance reporting.']
  crm_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM for this relationship assignment. Used for system integration, data lineage, and bi-directional synchronisation between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM source system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the revenue target and commercial targets for this assignment (e.g., USD, EUR, AUD, SGD).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The commercial segment classification of the port community participant for this assignment. Used for portfolio management, revenue reporting, and targeted service delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1_shipping_line|tier_2_shipping_line|freight_forwarder|cargo_owner|customs_broker|trucking_company|port_agent|nvocc|government_entity — promote to reference product]']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided consent for their relationship data to be shared with third-party port community system participants or regulatory bodies. Supports privacy compliance and data governance obligations.']
  escalation_tier varchar [note: 'Numeric tier indicating the escalation level of this assignment within the CRM workflow routing hierarchy. Tier 1 is the primary point of contact; higher tiers represent escalation paths to senior management. Used for automated CRM escalation routing.']
  handover_date date [note: 'The date on which the relationship manager assignment was formally handed over from the previous staff member to the current assignee. Null if this is an original assignment with no prior holder.']
  handover_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting key customer context, open issues, relationship history, and action items transferred during the assignment handover. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive customer relationship intelligence.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary relationship manager assignment for the customer. A customer may have multiple assigned managers across different service lines, but only one should be flagged as primary for default CRM routing and escalation.']
  isps_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned relationship manager requires International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code clearance to access restricted port areas when meeting with this customer. Supports security compliance and access management.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent formal account review meeting conducted by the relationship manager with the customer. Used to track engagement cadence and identify overdue reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date of the next scheduled formal account review meeting between the relationship manager and the customer. Drives CRM calendar scheduling and proactive engagement workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the relationship manager to record contextual notes about the customer relationship, special instructions, or account-specific considerations not captured in structured fields. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive content.']
  pcs_participant_code varchar [note: 'The participant code assigned to the customer within the Port Community System (PCS). Used to correlate the relationship manager assignment with PCS messaging, cargo manifests, and trade documentation workflows.']
  preferred_contact_channel varchar [note: 'The customer\'s preferred communication channel for engagement with their assigned relationship manager. Supports CRM workflow configuration and communication preference management. EDI and PCS messaging are standard channels in maritime logistics.']
  preferred_contact_language varchar [note: 'The ISO 639-1 language code representing the customer\'s preferred language for communication with their relationship manager (e.g., en, zh-CN, ar, fr). Supports multilingual port community engagement.']
  relationship_health_score varchar [note: 'A manually assessed or CRM-system-generated integer score (typically 1-100) reflecting the overall health and strength of the customer relationship at the time of last review. Used for portfolio risk management and proactive engagement prioritisation.']
  response_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually or operationally agreed maximum response time in hours for the assigned relationship manager to respond to customer enquiries or service requests under this assignment. Supports SLA compliance monitoring.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'The agreed frequency at which the relationship manager conducts formal account review meetings with the customer. Supports scheduling of Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reviews and service performance discussions.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assigned staff member\'s role in managing the customer relationship. Defines the nature of engagement and authority level within the port organisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: key_account_manager|commercial_manager|port_agent_liaison|customer_success_manager|trade_development_manager|operations_liaison — promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA tier applicable to this customer relationship assignment, determining response time commitments, service priority, and escalation thresholds. Platinum and gold tiers typically apply to major shipping lines and key accounts.']
  strategic_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is classified as a strategic account requiring executive-level engagement and enhanced service delivery. Strategic accounts typically represent major shipping lines or high-volume cargo owners critical to port revenue.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for terminating this relationship manager assignment. Populated when assignment_status transitions to terminated. Supports workforce analytics and customer retention reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this relationship manager assignment record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Assignment records linking port community participants to their designated account managers, relationship managers, and port agents within the port organisation. Captures assigned staff member, role type (key account manager, commercial manager, port agent liaison), assignment start date, end date, primary flag, and coverage scope (all services or specific service lines). Supports CRM workflow routing and escalation management.'
}

Table customer.port_access_permit {
  port_access_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each port access permit record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  access_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_credential. Business justification: ISPS compliance requires traceability from the formal permit authorization document to the physical/digital access credential issued against it. Port security officers use this link for revocation wor']
  authorized_access_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.access_point. Business justification: Permits specify which access points holder may use for entry/exit. Essential for access control system integration and permit validation at gates. Role prefix clarifies this is authorized entry point,']
  contact_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact_person. Business justification: Port access permits are issued to specific individuals who must be registered in the port community system. The contact_person table already carries port_access_badge_number, confirming the cross-refe']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Under the ISPS Code, port access permits specify which terminal(s) the holder is authorized to access. The port_access_permit has port_location_id but not terminal_id. Terminal-scoped access control i']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: Port access permits must validate against ISPS facility security requirements and current security level. Port security officers require this link to enforce zone access restrictions based on facility']
  pcs_permit_id varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this permit record in the Port Community System (PCS). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration between the PCS and the lakehouse silver layer.']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant entity (shipping line, freight forwarder, trucking company, port agent, etc.) under whose sponsorship this permit was issued. Links to the customer master.']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Access permits grant entry to specific terminals, berths, or ISPS security zones; physical access control enforcement, gate validation, and security zone management require precise location-level auth']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Port access permits require sanctions screening of the permit holder prior to issuance, per ISPS and national port security regulations. Port security operations must record which sanctions screening ']
  sponsoring_vessel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.vessel. Business justification: ISPS Code requires port access permits for crew and visitors to be sponsored by the vessel they are boarding. Port security teams must link access permits to the specific vessel for security zone comp']
  sponsoring_vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: port_access_permit has sponsoring_vessel_imo_number as plain text — strong denormalization of vessel_master. Under ISPS Code requirements, crew and vessel agent port access permits are sponsored by a ']
  access_time_restriction varchar [note: 'Time-of-day restriction applied to this permit. Unrestricted permits allow 24/7 access; other values limit access to defined operational windows. Enforced at gate entry systems and ISPS checkpoints.']
  application_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the original permit application submitted through the Port Community System (PCS) or port authority portal. Links the issued permit back to the application workflow for audit and traceability.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which the permit application was formally approved by the issuing authority. Distinct from issue_date which records the calendar date of issuance. Supports ISPS audit trail with precise approval timing.']
  background_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the security background check was completed for this permit holder. Used to determine whether a re-check is required at renewal based on the port\'s ISPS security policy interval.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the security background check conducted on the permit holder prior to issuance. Passed status is a prerequisite for active permit issuance under ISPS requirements. Waived may apply to certain visitor categories under escort.']
  badge_number varchar [note: 'Physical badge or access card number printed on the permit holder\'s port ID badge. Used by gate access control systems, RFID readers, and security checkpoints to validate physical access. May differ from permit_number.']
  biometric_reference_code varchar [note: 'Unique reference token linking this permit to the holder\'s biometric record stored in the port\'s secure biometric identity management system (fingerprint, iris, or facial recognition template). The actual biometric template is NOT stored here — only the reference token. Null for vehicle permits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which this permit record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and lineage tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  crm_contact_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding contact record in Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM for the permit holder or their sponsoring organisation. Enables cross-system traceability between the access permit and the CRM customer engagement record.']
  escort_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit holder must be accompanied by an authorised port escort at all times while within restricted ISPS zones. True for visitor and certain contractor permit types; False for credentialed personnel with full unescorted access.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the port access permit ceases to be valid. After this date the permit_status transitions to expired and physical access is denied. Renewal must be initiated before this date to maintain uninterrupted access.']
  holder_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the individual permit holder in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for identity verification, background screening, and ISPS compliance records. Null for vehicle permits.']
  holder_employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organisation that employs or sponsors the permit holder (e.g., shipping line, stevedoring company, customs broker, trucking company). Used to link the individual to their sponsoring port community participant for accountability and audit.']
  holder_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual permit holder (person) as it appears on their government-issued identity document. Populated for personnel, contractor, visitor, crew, and emergency permit types. Null for vehicle-only permits.']
  holder_id_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique number of the government-issued identity document (passport number, national ID number, seafarer book number, etc.) used to verify the permit holder\'s identity during the application process.']
  holder_id_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identity document presented by the permit holder at the time of application and verification. Used for ISPS compliance audit and background check records.']
  holder_nationality varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the nationality of the individual permit holder. Required for ISPS background screening and customs/immigration compliance reporting.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code security level at which this permit was issued. Level 1 is normal operations; Level 2 is heightened threat; Level 3 is exceptional threat. Permits issued at higher security levels may carry additional access restrictions.']
  isps_zone_access varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISPS-designated restricted port zones to which this permit grants access (e.g., berth_side|container_yard|gate_complex|bonded_warehouse|control_room). Defined per the port\'s ISPS Port Facility Security Plan (PFSP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: berth_side|container_yard|gate_complex|bonded_warehouse|control_room|vessel_deck|hazmat_area — promote to reference product for zone management]']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the port access permit was formally issued and became effective. Marks the start of the permit\'s validity period.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the port authority department or designated security officer responsible for issuing this permit (e.g., Port Security Office, ISPS Compliance Unit). Required for audit trail and regulatory accountability.']
  max_entry_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of gate entries permitted under this access permit within its validity period. Null indicates unlimited entries (applicable to permanent and multi-entry permits). Enforced by the gate access control system.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'Operational category of the permit defining its usage scope. Permanent permits are issued for long-term employees; temporary permits cover short-term contractors or visitors; single-entry permits are for one-time access events; multi-entry permits allow repeated access within the validity period.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique permit reference number assigned by the port authority at issuance. Used on physical badges, gate scanners, and ISPS audit trails. Format: PAP-<PORT_CODE>-<SEQUENCE>.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the port access permit. Active permits grant physical access; suspended permits are temporarily blocked pending investigation; revoked permits are permanently cancelled; expired permits have passed their expiry date without renewal.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the access permit indicating whether it covers a person (personnel, contractor, visitor, crew, emergency responder) or a vehicle. Drives the applicable ISPS security zone rules and badge colour coding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: personnel|vehicle|contractor|visitor|crew|emergency — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  previous_permit_number varchar [note: 'Permit number of the immediately preceding permit that this record renews or replaces. Enables lineage tracing across permit renewal cycles for ISPS audit and compliance history.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or special conditions associated with this permit (e.g., restricted to specific berth, requires safety induction completion, hazardous cargo handling certification required). Captured by the issuing security officer.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this permit has been renewed since its original issuance. Tracks the renewal history for compliance reporting and to identify long-standing permit holders who may require periodic re-vetting under ISPS policy.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was formally revoked prior to its natural expiry. Populated only when permit_status is revoked. Null for active, suspended, or expired permits.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the permit was revoked. Required when permit_status is revoked to support ISPS audit trails and incident reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: security_breach|employment_terminated|document_expired|disciplinary_action|voluntary_surrender|lost_stolen|other — promote to reference product if additional reasons are required]']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) chip embedded in the permit holder\'s access badge or vehicle transponder. Enables automated gate entry/exit tracking and real-time zone monitoring within the port.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was placed into suspended status pending investigation or administrative review. Populated only when permit_status is suspended. Null for all other statuses.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for suspending the permit. Populated when permit_status is suspended. Supports security incident investigation and ISPS compliance audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time (with timezone offset) at which this permit record was last modified. Supports change detection, incremental loading in the lakehouse, and audit trail requirements.']
  vehicle_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued registration plate number of the vehicle for vehicle-type permits. Null for personnel permits. Used at gate entry/exit for automated plate recognition and ISPS vehicle access control.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle covered by a vehicle-type permit. Determines applicable port zone access rules, weight restrictions, and escort requirements. Null for personnel permits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: truck|trailer|forklift|crane|passenger_vehicle|motorcycle|heavy_equipment — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']

  Note: 'Port access permits issued to port community participant personnel and vehicles, granting authorised access to restricted port zones in compliance with ISPS Code requirements. Captures permit holder (person or vehicle), permit type (personnel, vehicle, contractor), ISPS security zone access level, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, biometric reference, and revocation status. Distinct from accreditation (which is entity-level) — this is individual-level access control.'
}

Table tariff.port_tariff {
  port_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the port tariff schedule record. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Port tariffs are issued by specific legal entities in multi-entity port operators. Regulatory filing, inter-company billing, and statutory reporting require linking each tariff schedule to the issuing']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Port tariffs are administered by specific operational cost centres (e.g., marine services, terminal operations). Budget tracking and cost allocation for tariff administration and approval processes re']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Port tariffs require national regulatory approval (approval_authority, regulatory_filing_required_flag). Country context determines jurisdiction, regulatory framework, currency regulations, and intern']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Port tariff schedules must map to GL revenue accounts for revenue recognition and financial reporting. Maritime finance controllers require this mapping to post tariff-generated revenue to the correct']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Port tariff schedules are published per operational port by the port authority. Tariff management, regulatory filing, and public tariff publication all require direct reference to the operational port']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Port tariffs generate revenue attributed to specific profit centres (container terminal, bulk terminal, marine services). Segment P&L reporting and management accounting require this attribution at th']
  superseded_by_tariff_port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port_tariff_id of the newer tariff schedule that replaces this one. Null if this is the current active schedule.']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Port tariffs must reference trade restrictions to exclude embargoed goods, sanctioned parties from standard tariff application. Regulatory compliance requirement for port authorities.']
  un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: Port tariffs are published for specific UN LOCODE locations. Tariff schedules must reference the standardized port location code for regulatory filing, publication, and enforcement. Essential for tari']
  applicable_cargo_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cargo types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'FCL\', \'LCL\', \'Breakbulk\', \'Dangerous Goods\', \'Reefer\'). Null if applicable to all cargo types.']
  applicable_container_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of container types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'20GP\', \'40GP\', \'40HC\', \'45HC\', \'Reefer\', \'Tank\', \'Flat Rack\'). Null if not container-specific.']
  applicable_movement_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cargo movement types to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Import\', \'Export\', \'Transshipment\', \'Empty Repositioning\', \'Restow\'). Null if applicable to all movements.']
  applicable_terminal_zones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of terminal zones, berths, or container yards to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Zone A\', \'Berth 1-5\', \'CY North\'). Null if applicable port-wide.']
  applicable_trade_lanes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trade lanes or routes to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Asia-Europe\', \'Transpacific\', \'Intra-Asia\'). Null if applicable to all lanes.']
  applicable_vessel_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vessel types or categories to which this tariff applies (e.g., \'Container\', \'Bulk Carrier\', \'Tanker\', \'RoRo\'). Null if applicable to all vessel types.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or internal authority that approved this tariff schedule (e.g., \'Port Authority Board\', \'National Maritime Safety Authority\', \'Finance Committee\').']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the tariff schedule received regulatory or port authority board approval.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference or filing number assigned by the approval authority for audit and compliance tracking.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base rate amount for this tariff schedule. Interpretation depends on charge_type: per TEU for THC, per tonne for WHR, per movement for pilotage, per day for storage/DMG/DET. Null if tariff uses complex multi-tier pricing defined in tariff_item.']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the category of port charge. THC=Terminal Handling Charge, WHR=Wharfage, PILOTAGE=Pilotage fees, TOWAGE=Towage services, MOORING=Mooring services, PORT_DUES=Port dues (light/conservancy/anchorage), STORAGE=Container storage, DMG=Demurrage, DET=Detention, BAF=Bunker Adjustment Factor, CAF=Currency Adjustment Factor, PIL=Port Infrastructure Levy. New charge types added as new rows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: THC|WHR|PILOTAGE|TOWAGE|MOORING|PORT_DUES|STORAGE|DMG|DET|BAF|CAF|PIL — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this tariff schedule record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tariff schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which tariff rates are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, SGD).']
  discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff schedule is eligible for volume discounts, loyalty discounts, or promotional pricing. True=Eligible, False=Fixed pricing only.']
  dwt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Deadweight Tonnage for towage or mooring tariffs that are banded by cargo capacity. Null if not DWT-banded or open-ended.']
  dwt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Deadweight Tonnage for towage or mooring tariffs that are banded by cargo capacity. Null if not DWT-banded.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this tariff schedule becomes binding and applicable to port operations and billing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this tariff schedule ceases to be applicable. Nullable for open-ended schedules.']
  escalation_structure varchar [note: 'Pricing escalation model for storage, demurrage, or detention charges. FLAT=Single rate throughout, TIERED=Rate increases at defined day thresholds, PROGRESSIVE=Rate increases daily or weekly.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before demurrage or storage charges begin to accrue. Applicable to DMG, DET, and STORAGE charge types. Null if no free time applies.']
  grt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Gross Registered Tonnage for port dues, pilotage, or towage tariffs that are banded by vessel tonnage. Null if not tonnage-banded or open-ended.']
  grt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Gross Registered Tonnage for port dues, pilotage, or towage tariffs that are banded by vessel tonnage. Null if not tonnage-banded.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tariff schedule record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tariff schedule record was last updated in the system.']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Length Overall in meters for pilotage, towage, or mooring tariffs that are banded by vessel size. Null if not size-banded or open-ended.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Length Overall in meters for pilotage, towage, or mooring tariffs that are banded by vessel size. Null if not size-banded.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum charge amount cap that applies regardless of calculated charge. Used for regulatory compliance or customer protection. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum charge amount that applies regardless of calculated charge. Used to ensure minimum revenue per transaction or vessel call. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to the application of this tariff schedule. May include references to related schedules or regulatory requirements.']
  public_tariff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff schedule is publicly published and available to all port users, or is a confidential negotiated rate. True=Public tariff, False=Confidential/negotiated rate.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which the tariff schedule was officially published to customers, shipping lines, and stakeholders.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the tariff rate. PER_TEU=Per Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, PER_FEU=Per Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, PER_CONTAINER=Per container regardless of size, PER_TONNE=Per metric tonne, PER_CBM=Per cubic meter, PER_MOVEMENT=Per handling movement, PER_HOUR=Per hour, PER_DAY=Per day, PER_CALL=Per vessel call, LUMP_SUM=Fixed amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PER_TEU|PER_FEU|PER_CONTAINER|PER_TONNE|PER_CBM|PER_MOVEMENT|PER_HOUR|PER_DAY|PER_CALL|LUMP_SUM — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff schedule requires formal filing with or approval from a regulatory authority before implementation. True=Regulatory filing required, False=Internal approval sufficient.']
  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff is linked to specific Service Level Agreement performance commitments. True=SLA-linked with performance guarantees, False=Standard tariff without SLA.']
  tariff_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tariff schedule including scope, applicability conditions, calculation methodology, and any special terms or exclusions.']
  tariff_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Externally published unique code identifying this tariff schedule (e.g., THC-2024-Q1, PIL-ZONE-A-2024). Used in billing systems, customer communications, and regulatory filings.']
  tariff_schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tariff schedule (e.g., \'Terminal Handling Charges - Container Import 2024\', \'Pilotage Fees - LOA Band 200-300m\').']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tariff schedule. DRAFT=Under development, PENDING_APPROVAL=Submitted for regulatory approval, APPROVED=Approved but not yet effective, PUBLISHED=Published to stakeholders, ACTIVE=Currently in force, SUSPENDED=Temporarily inactive, SUPERSEDED=Replaced by newer schedule, ARCHIVED=Historical record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|PENDING_APPROVAL|APPROVED|PUBLISHED|ACTIVE|SUSPENDED|SUPERSEDED|ARCHIVED — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all port tariff schedules published by the port authority, organized by charge type discriminator. Supported charge types include THC (Terminal Handling Charges by container type/movement/zone), wharfage (WHR by cargo type/HS code/tonnage), pilotage (by LOA/GRT band/zone/time), towage (by GRT/DWT band/tug count/operation type), mooring (by LOA band/gang count/time), port dues (light dues, conservancy, anchorage by vessel GRT/flag), storage (by container status/type/zone with escalating bands), demurrage (DMG by container type/day with free time and escalation), and detention (DET for off-terminal equipment retention). Each record defines a named tariff schedule with its charge type classification, effective period, applicable trade lanes, vessel categories, cargo types, terminal zones, regulatory approval status, and filing reference. New charge types are added as new rows with a charge_type discriminator — no schema changes required. Supports multiple concurrent schedules per charge type. This is the SSOT for all tariff schedule definitions — individual charge amounts are held in tariff_item.'
}

Table tariff.item {
  item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tariff item. Primary key for the tariff item entity.']
  commodity_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.commodity_code. Business justification: Tariff line items apply to specific cargo commodities (cargo_type_applicability). HS code classification determines applicable charges, handling requirements, and regulatory compliance. Critical for t']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Tariff items apply commodity-specific charges based on HS codes (dangerous goods surcharges, refrigerated cargo handling fees). Core charge calculation process distinct from wharfage.']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Tariff line items are associated with specific operational cost centres for cost allocation and budget variance analysis. Item-level cost centre attribution enables granular charge-type budget trackin']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is demurrage (DMG), this FK links to the demurrage schedule defining tiered daily rates after free time expires. Resolves demurrage_schedule silo.']
  detention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.detention_schedule. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is detention (DET), this FK links to the detention schedule defining equipment detention charges. Resolves detention_schedule silo.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Every tariff item posts revenue to a specific GL account when invoiced. Revenue recognition and financial statement preparation require mapping each charge type (wharfage, pilotage, storage) to its co']
  mooring_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.mooring_tariff. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is mooring, this FK links to the mooring tariff defining line-handling service charges. Resolves mooring_tariff silo.']
  pilotage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.pilotage_tariff. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is pilotage, this FK links to the pilotage tariff defining vessel-based pilotage fees. Resolves pilotage_tariff silo.']
  port_dues_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_dues_schedule. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is port dues, this FK links to the port dues schedule defining statutory vessel call charges. Resolves port_dues_schedule silo.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent tariff schedule that contains this tariff item. Links the item to its governing rate card or pricing schedule.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Tariff items generate revenue attributed to specific profit centres. Item-level profit centre attribution enables granular P&L reporting by charge type and service category, complementing the existing']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: Tariff items price specific port services. Currently tariff_item has service_type as a string. Adding FK to masterdata.service_code provides standardized service definitions, billing_basis, tax_code, ']
  storage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.storage_tariff. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is storage, this FK links to the storage tariff defining progressive daily storage rates by container type and zone. Resolves storage_tariff silo.']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: tariff_item defines a billable charge within port_tariff. When the charge_category is THC (Terminal Handling Charge), this FK links to the detailed THC schedule that defines the rate structure by cont']
  towage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is towage, this FK links to the towage tariff defining tug assistance charges by vessel size and operation type. Resolves towage_tariff silo.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Tariff items in port billing are scoped to vessel types (e.g., pilotage item applies only to certain vessel categories). Linking to vessel_type master enables consistent applicability filtering in bil']
  wharfage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: When tariff_item charge_category is wharfage (WHR), this FK links to the wharfage schedule defining cargo-based port dues. Resolves wharfage_schedule silo and enables navigation from charge definition']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this tariff item. Pending items await management approval, approved items are authorized for use, rejected items require revision.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this tariff item for publication and use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tariff item was approved for use.']
  charge_basis varchar [note: 'The calculation method for the charge (e.g., per unit, per day, per move, flat rate, percentage of cargo value). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_unit|per_day|per_move|per_gang|per_hour|per_shift|per_vessel_call|flat_rate|percentage — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the charge type for grouping and reporting purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: terminal_handling|wharfage|pilotage|towage|storage|demurrage|detention|infrastructure|bunker_adjustment|currency_adjustment|service_fee — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the type of charge (e.g., THC, WHR, DMG, DET, PIL, BAF, CAF). Used as the billing system reference for this tariff item.']
  charge_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tariff item, including applicability conditions, service scope, and any special terms or exclusions.']
  charge_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the charge (e.g., Terminal Handling Charge, Wharfage, Pilotage Fee, Demurrage, Detention, Port Infrastructure Levy).']
  container_size_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies which container sizes this tariff item applies to (20ft for TEU, 40ft for FEU, 45ft for high-cube, all for any size).']
  container_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of container types to which this tariff item applies (e.g., standard, refrigerated, open_top, flat_rack, tank). Empty means applies to all container types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|refrigerated|open_top|flat_rack|tank|platform|ventilated — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tariff item record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff item applies specifically to dangerous goods (IMDG cargo). True means this rate is for dangerous goods only; False means it applies to general cargo.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this tariff item becomes active and applicable for billing. Part of the tariff items temporal validity window.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this tariff item remains active and applicable for billing. Null indicates the tariff item is open-ended and remains active until superseded.']
  escalation_tier_1_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after free time expires when the first escalation tier rate applies (used for storage, demurrage, detention with escalating daily rates).']
  escalation_tier_1_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount for the first escalation tier (days beyond free time up to escalation_tier_1_days).']
  escalation_tier_2_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after tier 1 expires when the second escalation tier rate applies.']
  escalation_tier_2_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount for the second escalation tier (days beyond tier 1 up to escalation_tier_2_days).']
  escalation_tier_3_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after tier 2 expires when the third escalation tier rate applies.']
  escalation_tier_3_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount for the third escalation tier (days beyond tier 2 up to escalation_tier_3_days).']
  fcl_lcl_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff item applies to Full Container Load (FCL) shipments, Less than Container Load (LCL) shipments, or both.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before charges begin to accrue (applicable to storage, demurrage, and detention charges). Zero means charges start immediately.']
  import_export_direction varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this tariff item applies to import cargo, export cargo, transshipment cargo, or all directions.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tariff item. Draft items are under review, active items are in use, suspended items are temporarily inactive, expired items are past their effective date, superseded items have been replaced by newer versions.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum charge that will be applied regardless of calculated amount. Caps the price for the service.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum charge that will be applied regardless of calculated amount. Ensures a floor price for the service.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tariff item record was last modified or updated.']
  rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The base rate or price per unit of measure for this tariff item. Expressed in the currency specified in the tariff schedule.']
  rate_band_amount_1 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount applied to units within the first rate band (up to rate_band_threshold_1).']
  rate_band_amount_2 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount applied to units within the second rate band (between threshold_1 and threshold_2).']
  rate_band_amount_3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate amount applied to units within the third rate band (between threshold_2 and threshold_3).']
  rate_band_threshold_1 decimal(18,4) [note: 'First threshold value for tiered pricing. Units below or equal to this threshold are charged at rate_band_amount_1.']
  rate_band_threshold_2 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Second threshold value for tiered pricing. Units above threshold_1 and below or equal to this threshold are charged at rate_band_amount_2.']
  rate_band_threshold_3 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Third threshold value for tiered pricing. Units above threshold_2 and below or equal to this threshold are charged at rate_band_amount_3.']
  rounding_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places to which the charge should be rounded (e.g., 0 for whole numbers, 2 for cents).']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Specifies how calculated charges should be rounded (e.g., round up to nearest whole unit, round down, round to nearest, no rounding).']
  sla_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level tier associated with this tariff item (e.g., standard, express, premium). Used for SLA-linked rate cards where pricing varies by committed service level.']
  tiered_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff item uses tiered or banded pricing (True) or a flat rate (False). When True, rate bands and thresholds apply.']
  trade_lane_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trade lanes or routes to which this tariff item applies (e.g., asia_europe, transatlantic, intra_asia). Empty means applies to all trade lanes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit by which the charge is calculated and billed (e.g., TEU for Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU for Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, GRT for Gross Registered Tonnage, LOA for Length Overall, CBM for Cubic Meter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|GRT|NRT|LOA|CBM|tonne|day|move|gang|hour|shift|container|vessel_call — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Individual line-item charge definition within a port tariff schedule. Represents a single billable service rate (e.g., THC per TEU by container type, wharfage per GRT by cargo category, pilotage per LOA band by zone, storage per day per TEU by escalation tier, towage per GRT band by operation type). Captures the charge code, unit of measure (TEU, FEU, GRT, LOA, CBM, tonne, day, move, gang), charge basis, rate amount, minimum charge, maximum charge, rounding rules, vessel/cargo/trade applicability conditions, and rate band thresholds for tiered pricing. Forms the atomic pricing unit referenced by rate cards, pricing rules, and the billing engine.'
}

Table tariff.rate_card {
  rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rate card. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Rate cards are issued by specific legal entities in multi-entity port operators. Inter-company billing, regulatory compliance, and financial consolidation require linking each rate card to the issuing']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Rate cards are assigned to cost centres for revenue tracking and profitability analysis. Monthly P&L reporting by cost centre requires linking negotiated customer rates to the delivering cost centre f']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Negotiated rate cards for customs brokers who are high-volume port service users. Real commercial relationship in maritime logistics for freight forwarding services.']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Rate cards are commercial agreements between terminal operators and shipping lines, fundamentally scoped to a specific terminal. Direct FK to infrastructure.terminal enables terminal operator contract']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Rate cards for major customers or port development projects are linked to internal orders for project-based cost and revenue tracking. Port operators use internal orders to monitor committed volumes a']
  masterdata_shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Negotiated rate cards are customer-specific contracts with shipping lines. Shipping lines are major port customers with volume commitments, SLA requirements, and preferential pricing. Essential for co']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: A rate card is negotiated against a base port tariff schedule. This FK anchors the rate card to the master tariff it overrides or supplements, enabling traceability from negotiated pricing back to the']
  primary_rate_port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer or shipping line to which this rate card applies. Null if rate card is segment-based rather than customer-specific.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Rate cards drive revenue streams tracked by profit centre for segment reporting. Executive dashboards showing revenue by business unit/terminal require linking rate cards to profit centres for accurat']
  superseded_by_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer rate card that replaces this one. Null if this rate card is still current or has not been superseded.']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Rate cards negotiated for specific trade lanes must be validated against active trade restrictions before activation. A rate card for an embargoed trade lane cannot be offered. Named process: rate car']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the rate card in the approval and lifecycle process. Draft = under construction, pending_approval = submitted for review, approved = authorized but not yet active, active = currently in use, expired = past expiry date, superseded = replaced by newer version. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|active|expired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the commercial manager or authority who approved this rate card for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rate card was formally approved.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate card automatically renews upon expiry (True) or requires explicit renewal negotiation (False).']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which charges under this rate card are invoiced to the customer.']
  committed_volume_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed container volume in TEU that the customer agrees to handle under this rate card during the contract period. Applicable to volume-based rate cards.']
  crane_productivity_target_moves_per_hour decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target crane productivity in container moves per hour committed under SLA-linked rate cards. Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rate card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all rates on this rate card are denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'SGD\').']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer tier or segment to which this rate card applies (e.g., tier_1 for top-tier shipping lines, sme for small-medium enterprises).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount percentage applied to base tariff rates in this rate card (e.g., 15.00 represents a 15% discount). Null if no blanket discount applies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this rate card becomes active and applicable for billing and pricing calculations.']
  escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of rate escalation terms, such as annual CPI adjustments, fuel surcharge indexing, or fixed percentage increases (e.g., \'3% annual escalation on anniversary date\').']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this rate card ceases to be valid. Null for open-ended rate cards that remain active until explicitly superseded.']
  gate_processing_time_target_minutes decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target gate processing time in minutes for truck transactions committed under SLA-linked rate cards. Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which SLA performance is measured and assessed (e.g., monthly average, per vessel call). Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  minimum_commitment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum revenue or volume commitment required from the customer to qualify for this rate card pricing. Null if no minimum commitment applies.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system that last modified this rate card record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rate card record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commercial notes, special conditions, or clarifications related to this rate card.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate or amend this rate card.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms applicable to invoices generated under this rate card (e.g., \'Net 30 days\', \'Net 15 days\', \'Prepayment required\').']
  penalty_clause_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of penalty terms applicable if SLA performance targets are not met (e.g., \'5% rate reduction for each hour beyond target turnaround time\'). Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  premium_clause_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of premium or bonus terms applicable if SLA performance targets are exceeded (e.g., \'3% rate premium for turnaround time 20% faster than target\'). Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  premium_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall premium percentage applied to base tariff rates in this rate card for expedited or premium services (e.g., 10.00 represents a 10% premium). Null if no premium applies.']
  rate_card_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the rate card, typically describing the customer segment, trade lane, or service tier (e.g., \'Asia-Europe Premium SLA Gold Tier\', \'Standard Container Handling - Tier 1 Customers\').']
  rate_card_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the rate card, used in commercial documentation and billing references.']
  rate_card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate card: standard (published rates), sla_linked (performance-based pricing with SLA tiers), promotional (temporary discount rates), spot (one-time negotiated rates), contract (long-term agreement rates).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of port service covered by this rate card (e.g., \'Container Handling\', \'Vessel Berthing\', \'Pilotage\', \'Storage\', \'THC\'). May reference multiple service types if rate card is comprehensive.
