[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: container_handling|vessel_berthing|pilotage|towage|storage|thc|wharfage|demurrage|detention|pil|baf|caf — promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service performance tier applicable to SLA-linked rate cards, defining the level of service commitment and associated pricing (gold = highest service level, bronze = basic service level). Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Geographic trade route or lane to which this rate card applies (e.g., \'Asia-Europe\', \'Transpacific\', \'Intra-Asia\'). Null if rate card is not trade-lane specific.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the rate card, enabling tracking of amendments and revisions over time (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'2.1\').']
  vessel_turnaround_time_target_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target vessel turnaround time in hours committed under SLA-linked rate cards. Null for non-SLA rate cards.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system that created this rate card record.']

  Note: 'Negotiated or published rate card defining specific pricing applicable to a customer segment, shipping line, cargo owner, or trade lane. A rate card references one or more tariff items and overrides or discounts the base tariff rates for a defined period. Supports standard commercial rate cards and SLA-linked rate cards with service performance tiers (Gold/Silver/Bronze), committed KPIs (vessel turnaround time, crane productivity moves/hour, gate processing time), penalty/premium clauses, measurement periods, and escalation clauses. Captures rate card name, version, effective and expiry dates, applicable customer tier, trade lane, service type, SLA tier (if applicable), currency, approval workflow status, and link to originating tariff_negotiation. An SLA tier discriminator distinguishes standard rate cards from performance-linked rate cards — no separate entity required. Serves as the commercial pricing instrument linking the tariff catalog to customer agreements and SLA-based billing adjustments.'
}

Linking to cargo_category master ensures consistent classification in c']
  discount_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.discount_scheme. Business justification: rate_card_line has discount_percentage which is driven by a discount scheme. Linking to discount_scheme identifies the commercial discount definition governing the line-level discount, enabling discou']
  free_time_allowance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.free_time_allowance. Business justification: rate_card_line has free_time_days which represents a free time period granted on this pricing line. Linking to free_time_allowance references the master free time definition governing this allowance, ']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Rate card lines are terminal-scoped charge definitions. The existing terminal_code plain-text column is a denormalized reference to infrastructure.terminal. Direct FK enables terminal-level rate card ']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_item. Business justification: rate_card_line currently has tariff_item_code (STRING) for business reference, but needs a proper FK to tariff_item to link negotiated rates to the master tariff item definition. This enables joining ']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent rate card that contains this line item. Links this pricing line to the overall commercial rate card agreement.']
  surcharge_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.surcharge_rule. Business justification: rate_card_line has surcharge_applicable_flag indicating a surcharge applies to this pricing line. The specific surcharge rule governing the calculation should be referenced via FK to surcharge_rule, e']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Commercial rate card lines in port agreements are scoped to vessel size/type categories. Linking to vessel_type master enables consistent vessel classification in commercial rate structures. Replaces ']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rate card line was approved for use. Critical for audit trail and compliance with pricing governance policies.']
  baf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) surcharge is applicable to this rate card line. BAF compensates for fuel cost fluctuations. True if BAF applies, False otherwise.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which charges based on this rate card line are billed. Values include per_event (billed per occurrence), daily (daily billing), weekly (weekly billing), monthly (monthly billing), quarterly (quarterly billing), annual (annual billing).']
  caf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Currency Adjustment Factor (CAF) surcharge is applicable to this rate card line. CAF compensates for currency exchange rate fluctuations. True if CAF applies, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rate card line record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the unit rate is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base unit rate for this line. Used for volume discounts, promotional pricing, or negotiated rate reductions. Null if no discount applies.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this rate card line becomes effective and can be applied to billing. Part of the temporal validity window for this pricing line.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this rate card line remains effective. Null indicates an open-ended validity period. Part of the temporal validity window for this pricing line.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before charges such as storage, demurrage, or detention begin to accrue. Commonly used for container storage and cargo dwell time pricing. Null if not applicable.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rate card line record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering number of this line within the parent rate card. Used for display and processing order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this rate card line. Values: draft (under negotiation), active (in effect and billable), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (validity period ended), superseded (replaced by newer version), cancelled (terminated before expiry).']
  maximum_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum quantity threshold for this rate to apply. Used for tiered pricing structures where different rates apply to different volume bands. Null if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum quantity threshold for this rate to apply. Used for minimum charge enforcement or tiered pricing structures. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this rate card line. Used for business context that does not fit structured fields.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any manual override or exception to standard pricing rules for this line. Examples include VOLUME_DISCOUNT, STRATEGIC_CUSTOMER, PROMOTIONAL, SERVICE_RECOVERY, COMPETITIVE_MATCH. Null if no override applies.']
  override_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the reason for any pricing override or exception applied to this rate card line. Provides audit trail and business justification.']
  penalty_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The penalty rate per unit applied when service levels are not met or when charges such as demurrage or detention are incurred. Null if no penalty applies to this line.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the port service covered by this rate card line. Values include handling (cargo/container handling), storage (yard/warehouse storage), pilotage (marine pilotage), towage (tug services), mooring (mooring services), security (ISPS security services), documentation (customs/trade documentation), inspection (cargo inspection), other (miscellaneous services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: handling|storage|pilotage|towage|mooring|security|documentation|inspection|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the service or charge covered by this rate card line. Provides business context for the tariff item.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The target service delivery time in hours associated with this rate card line, if the pricing is linked to a Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used for SLA-linked service rate cards. Null if no SLA applies.']
  surcharge_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional surcharges (such as BAF, CAF, or other adjustment factors) are applicable to this rate card line. True if surcharges apply, False otherwise.']
  tax_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes (such as VAT, GST, or other local taxes) are applicable to this rate card line. True if taxes apply, False otherwise.']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The tax rate percentage applicable to this rate card line, if taxes apply. Null if no tax applies or tax is calculated externally.']
  tier_threshold_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the quantity tier for tiered pricing. When quantity falls within this tier, the specified unit rate applies. Null for flat-rate pricing.']
  tier_threshold_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the quantity tier for tiered pricing. When quantity falls within this tier, the specified unit rate applies. Null for flat-rate pricing or open-ended top tier.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for which the unit rate applies. Common values include TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), TON (metric ton or deadweight tonnage), CBM (Cubic Meter), MOVE (container move), HOUR (hourly rate), DAY (daily rate), CALL (per vessel call), UNIT (per equipment unit), CONTAINER (per container), SHIPMENT (per shipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|TON|CBM|MOVE|HOUR|DAY|CALL|UNIT|CONTAINER|SHIPMENT — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated rate per unit of measure for this tariff item. Represents the base price before any quantity-based discounts or surcharges.']

  Note: 'Individual pricing line within a rate card, specifying the negotiated rate for a specific tariff item, service, or charge code. Captures the unit rate, unit of measure, minimum quantity, maximum quantity, tiered pricing thresholds, surcharge applicability (BAF, CAF), and override reason. Enables granular rate management at the charge-code level within a commercial rate card.'
}

Linking to cargo_category master ensures standardized classification']

Business justification: THC schedules are differentiated by container type (20ft standard, 40ft HC, reefer, OOG have different handling charges). Port billing systems require container_type master reference for THC rate look']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Terminal Handling Charges are a distinct container revenue stream posted to dedicated GL accounts. Finance teams require this mapping for THC revenue recognition, separate from port dues and wharfage,']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: THC schedules are published per terminal by terminal operators and are the primary commercial document in terminal operator–shipping line negotiations. Direct FK to infrastructure.terminal enables ter']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: A THC schedule is a specific charge schedule published under a master port tariff. Linking thc_schedule to port_tariff establishes the parent tariff context for the THC rates, enabling tariff versioni']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: THC revenue is attributed to specific terminal profit centres for container terminal P&L reporting. Port operators segment revenue by terminal and service type; this link enables that segmentation at ']
  supersedes_schedule_thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous THC schedule ID that this schedule replaces or supersedes. Null if this is the initial version.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: THC (Terminal Handling Charges) vary by terminal zone. Currently thc_schedule has terminal_zone as a string code. Adding FK to infrastructure.terminal_zone provides referential integrity and enables j']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the THC schedule in the tariff management workflow. Only approved schedules are active for billing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the tariff manager or authorized person who approved this schedule.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this THC schedule was approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base THC rate amount per container or TEU before any adjustments, surcharges, or discounts. Expressed in the schedule currency.']
  container_size_teu decimal(5,2) [note: 'Container size expressed in TEU for standardized capacity and billing calculations. 20ft = 1.0 TEU, 40ft = 2.0 TEU, 45ft = 2.25 TEU.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific customer contract or service agreement if this THC schedule is contract-specific. Null for published general tariff rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this THC schedule record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the THC rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment for this THC rate (e.g., shipping lines, freight forwarders, direct shippers, government). May be null for general applicability.']
  dangerous_goods_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge amount applied for handling dangerous goods (IMDG classified cargo). Zero if not applicable.']
  discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this THC rate is eligible for volume discounts or promotional pricing. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this THC schedule becomes effective and applicable to billing operations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this THC schedule remains effective. Null indicates open-ended validity. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date when this THC schedule was filed with the regulatory authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum THC amount cap for this schedule. Null indicates no cap. Used for rate protection agreements.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum THC amount that will be charged regardless of calculated rate. Ensures cost recovery for low-volume transactions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this THC schedule record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this THC schedule record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Type of container movement through the terminal. Import (vessel to land), export (land to vessel), transshipment (vessel to vessel), coastal (domestic), or empty repositioning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special conditions associated with this THC schedule. May include handling instructions, exclusions, or clarifications.']
  oversize_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge for handling out-of-gauge (OOG) or oversize containers that require special handling equipment or procedures. Zero if not applicable.']
  peak_season_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge applied during peak shipping seasons or high-volume periods. Zero if not applicable.']
  published_date date [note: 'Date when this THC schedule was published to customers. Null if not yet published. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this THC schedule has been published to customers and external systems. True if published, False if internal-only.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the THC rate. Typically per container, per TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), per FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), per move, or per metric ton.']
  reefer_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge amount for handling refrigerated (reefer) containers requiring power and temperature monitoring. Zero if not applicable.']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory tariff filing with port authority or maritime regulatory body. Required for compliance and audit purposes.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the THC schedule, used in billing systems and tariff publications. Format: THC-<alphanumeric>.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the THC schedule for business users and reporting purposes.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level tier associated with this THC rate. Different service levels may have different handling priorities and rates.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade lane or shipping route to which this THC rate applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific, Transatlantic). May be null for general applicability.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this THC schedule. Incremented with each amendment or revision to maintain change history.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this THC schedule record.']

  Note: 'Terminal Handling Charge (THC) schedule defining the applicable THC rates by container type (20ft, 40ft, HC, RF, OOG), cargo category (FCL, LCL, RoRo, LoLo), movement type (import, export, transshipment, coastal), and terminal zone. Captures base THC rate per TEU/FEU, effective period, trade lane applicability, and regulatory filing reference. The SSOT for THC pricing used by the billing engine and TOS (NAVIS N4) charge calculation modules.'
}
