Port finance controllers require this GL mapping for revenue recognition by cargo type ']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Wharfage charges vary by commodity (HS code). Currently wharfage_schedule has hs_code_category as a string. Adding FK to compliance.hs_code enables proper commodity-based pricing and joins to HS code ']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Wharfage surcharges for dangerous goods cargo reference IMDG class for rate differentiation and regulatory compliance. Port DG handling billing requires imdg_class master reference. Replaces denormali']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: A wharfage schedule is a component of the master port tariff, defining cargo dues levied on goods passing over the wharf. Linking to port_tariff provides the governing tariff context and supports tari']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Wharfage revenue is attributed to specific berth or terminal profit centres. Segment P&L reporting by terminal zone and cargo type requires this profit centre attribution at the wharfage schedule leve']
  superseded_by_schedule_wharfage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wharfage_schedule_id that supersedes this tariff entry. Populated when a new version replaces this schedule.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Wharfage charges apply to cargo handled through specific terminal zones. Complements existing berth FK by adding cargo handling location. Required for zone-specific wharfage billing in cargo operation']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Wharfage rate calculation in port billing is differentiated by vessel type (container, bulk, tanker). Maritime billing experts expect wharfage_schedule to reference vessel_type master for consistent c']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body, port authority, or internal governance entity that approved this wharfage tariff schedule.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this wharfage tariff schedule was officially approved by the relevant authority.']
  approval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or document identifier from the approval authority for this wharfage tariff schedule. Used for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  baf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Bunker Adjustment Factor surcharge is applicable to this wharfage rate to account for fuel cost fluctuations.']
  caf_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Currency Adjustment Factor surcharge is applicable to this wharfage rate to account for exchange rate fluctuations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wharfage tariff schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the wharfage rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wharfage rate applies to dangerous goods cargo classified under IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods) Code. May attract surcharges.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base wharfage rate for qualifying cargo or customers (e.g., volume discounts, loyalty discounts, promotional rates).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this wharfage tariff schedule entry becomes active and applicable for billing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this wharfage tariff schedule entry remains active. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  exemption_condition varchar [note: 'Description of conditions under which wharfage charges are exempted (e.g., transshipment cargo not leaving port, government cargo, humanitarian aid, military cargo). Populated only when exemption_flag is True.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff entry represents an exemption condition (True) or a standard chargeable rate (False).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wharfage tariff schedule record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum wharfage charge threshold per transaction or shipment. Applied when calculated charge falls below this amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, conditions, or clarifications regarding the application of this wharfage tariff schedule. May include special instructions for billing or exceptions.']
  oversized_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wharfage rate applies to oversized or out-of-gauge cargo exceeding standard dimensions.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this wharfage tariff schedule was published and made available to port users and stakeholders.']
  rate_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Wharfage charge rate per unit of measure. Base rate before any adjustments, surcharges, or discounts.']
  refrigerated_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wharfage rate applies to refrigerated (reefer) cargo requiring temperature-controlled handling.']
  sla_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level tier associated with this wharfage rate. Different service levels may have different rates based on guaranteed turnaround times and priority handling.']
  surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to the base wharfage rate for special handling requirements (e.g., dangerous goods, oversized cargo, peak season).']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the wharfage tariff schedule entry. Used for external reference and billing system integration.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the wharfage tariff schedule entry describing the cargo category and applicable conditions.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wharfage tariff schedule entry. Only active tariffs are used for billing calculations.']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the wharfage tariff schedule. Incremented with each revision to maintain historical tracking and audit trail.']
  trade_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of cargo movement to which this wharfage rate applies. Import (inbound international), export (outbound international), coastal (domestic), or transshipment (in-transit).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for wharfage charge calculation. TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), tonne (metric ton), CBM (Cubic Meter), unit (per item), or revenue tonne (whichever is greater between weight and volume).']
  volume_break_lower_limit decimal(15,3) [note: 'Lower volume threshold (in CBM - Cubic Meters) for tiered wharfage pricing. Cargo volume at or above this limit qualifies for this rate tier.']
  volume_break_upper_limit decimal(15,3) [note: 'Upper volume threshold (in CBM - Cubic Meters) for tiered wharfage pricing. Cargo volume below this limit qualifies for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  weight_break_lower_limit decimal(15,3) [note: 'Lower weight threshold (in tonnes) for tiered wharfage pricing. Cargo weight at or above this limit qualifies for this rate tier.']
  weight_break_upper_limit decimal(15,3) [note: 'Upper weight threshold (in tonnes) for tiered wharfage pricing. Cargo weight below this limit qualifies for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit.']

  Note: 'Wharfage (WHR) tariff schedule defining port dues levied on cargo passing over the wharf. Captures wharfage rates by cargo type, HS Code category, unit of measure (tonne, CBM, TEU), vessel type, trade direction (import/export/coastal), and berth zone. Includes minimum wharfage thresholds, exemption conditions (e.g., transshipment cargo), and regulatory approval references. Feeds directly into the billing domain for WHR charge generation.'
}

Table tariff.pilotage_tariff {
  pilotage_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pilotage tariff record. Primary key.']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Pilotage charges depend on destination berth characteristics (draft, LOA capacity, tidal constraints). Commercial pilotage tariffs reference specific berths for rate determination. Required for berth-']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.channel. Business justification: Pilotage tariffs vary by channel depth, width, and navigational complexity. Port tariff schedules routinely specify channel-specific pilotage rates for billing and regulatory compliance. Essential for']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Pilotage fees are a regulated marine services revenue stream posted to dedicated GL accounts. Regulatory reporting to maritime authorities and revenue recognition for pilotage services require this GL']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Pilotage tariffs are published by the port pilotage authority and are port-scoped. Regulatory approval, tariff publication, and vessel call cost estimation require direct reference to infrastructure.p']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Pilotage services are location-specific (port_zone attribute). Different zones within port have different pilotage requirements, distances, and rates. Links to port location master for zone-based tari']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Pilotage fees are a statutory component of the port tariff schedule. Linking pilotage_tariff to port_tariff establishes the master tariff under which pilotage charges are published, supporting regulat']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Pilotage is a distinct marine services profit centre in port operations. Marine services P&L reporting and performance measurement require attributing pilotage revenue to the correct profit centre.']
  superseded_by_tariff_pilotage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pilotage_tariff_id that replaces this tariff when status is \'superseded\'. Creates a chain of tariff versions for historical tracking. Nullable if tariff is current or not superseded.']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Pilotage services are denied or modified for vessels from sanctioned flag states or embargoed trade lanes. Port authorities must check trade restrictions before applying pilotage tariffs. Maritime dom']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Pilotage tariffs vary by vessel type (container, tanker, bulk, etc.). Currently pilotage_tariff has vessel_type_restriction as a string. Adding FK to masterdata.vessel_type enables proper type-based p']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the port authority official or commercial manager who approved this tariff for publication. Audit field for governance and accountability. Nullable if approval workflow not required.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tariff was officially approved for use. Distinct from created_timestamp as tariffs may be drafted before approval. Nullable if approval workflow not required.']
  base_pilotage_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base charge amount for the pilotage service before any surcharges or adjustments. Represents the standard fee for the defined vessel size band, zone, and time category. Currency is defined at port level (typically USD or local currency).']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which pilotage charges under this tariff are invoiced. Per_service means immediate billing after each pilotage, monthly and quarterly indicate consolidated billing periods for high-volume customers.']
  cancellation_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed charge applied when a confirmed pilotage service is cancelled by the vessel within the cancellation notice period. Compensates for pilot mobilization costs. Nullable if no cancellation charges apply.']
  cancellation_notice_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice required to cancel a pilotage booking without incurring cancellation fees. Cancellations within this window trigger the cancellation_fee.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pilotage tariff record was first created in the system. Audit field for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this tariff (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All fees and charges are denominated in this currency.']
  dangerous_goods_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional charge applied when piloting vessels carrying dangerous goods or hazardous materials as classified under IMDG Code. Reflects additional risk and certification requirements. Nullable if no dangerous goods surcharges apply.']
  discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff is eligible for volume discounts, loyalty discounts, or contractual rate reductions. True if discounts can be applied, false if this is a fixed non-discountable rate.']
  distance_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff includes distance-based pricing components. True if charges vary by nautical miles traveled, false if charges are fixed per service regardless of distance.']
  dwt_band_max_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) in tons for which this tariff applies. Nullable for open-ended upper bands. Defines the upper boundary of the deadweight segment.']
  dwt_band_min_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Deadweight Tonnage (DWT) in tons for which this tariff applies. DWT is the measure of how much weight a vessel can safely carry (cargo, fuel, crew, provisions). Used for cargo vessel tariff segmentation.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this pilotage tariff becomes valid and applicable for billing. Tariff cannot be applied to services before this date.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this pilotage tariff remains valid. Nullable for open-ended tariffs. After this date, the tariff is no longer applicable for new services.']
  extraordinary_conditions_surcharge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional charge applied for pilotage under extraordinary conditions such as severe weather, restricted visibility, emergency situations, or vessels with special handling requirements. Nullable if no extraordinary surcharges apply.']
  grt_band_max_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) in tons for which this tariff applies. Nullable for open-ended upper bands. Defines the upper boundary of the tonnage segment.']
  grt_band_min_tons decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Registered Tonnage (GRT) in tons for which this tariff applies. GRT is a measure of the overall internal volume of a vessel. Used as an alternative or complementary vessel size metric for tariff segmentation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pilotage tariff record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data lineage.']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Length Overall (LOA) in meters for which this tariff applies. Nullable for open-ended upper bands (e.g., \'over 300m\'). Defines the upper boundary of the vessel size segment.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Length Overall (LOA) in meters for which this tariff applies. LOA is the maximum length of a vessel\'s hull measured parallel to the waterline. Used to segment tariffs by vessel size.']
  maximum_charge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total charge for the pilotage service regardless of calculated fees. Ensures a ceiling price for the service. Nullable if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_charge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum total charge for the pilotage service regardless of calculated fees. Ensures a floor price for the service. Nullable if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional tariff conditions, special instructions, exemptions, or clarifications that do not fit structured fields. Used for communicating tariff nuances to billing and operations teams.']
  pilotage_tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pilotage tariff. Active tariffs are in use, inactive are retired, suspended are temporarily not applicable, pending_approval awaits regulatory approval, superseded have been replaced by newer versions.']
  pilotage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of pilotage service type. Harbour pilotage covers port entry/exit, coastal pilotage covers coastal navigation, river pilotage covers inland waterways, shifting covers vessel movements between berths within the port.']
  rate_per_nautical_mile decimal(10,4) [note: 'Charge per nautical mile for distance-based pilotage tariffs. Applied in addition to or instead of base_pilotage_fee depending on tariff structure. Nullable if distance_based_flag is false.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory approval or port authority authorization for this tariff. Links to official tariff approval documentation. Nullable if no formal approval reference exists.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pilotage service is compulsory (mandated by port authority), voluntary (optional for vessel), or extraordinary (special circumstances requiring additional pilot expertise).']
  sla_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Committed maximum response time in minutes from pilotage request to pilot boarding, as defined in the service level agreement. Used for performance monitoring and SLA compliance tracking. Nullable if no SLA applies.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the pilotage tariff, used in billing systems and rate cards. Externally visible code for referencing this tariff in commercial documents.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pilotage tariff, describing the service and applicable conditions (e.g., \'Harbour Pilotage - Day Rate - LOA 150-200m\').']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this tariff definition, following semantic versioning (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented when tariff rates or conditions are updated. Enables tariff change tracking and audit trail.']
  tax_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes (VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes) should be applied to charges under this tariff. True if taxable, false if tax-exempt. Tax rates are defined separately in tax configuration.']
  time_of_day_category varchar [note: 'Time period classification for differential pricing. Day rates apply during standard business hours, night rates apply outside business hours, weekend and public holiday rates apply on non-working days. Reflects premium charges for off-hours pilotage services.']
  waiting_time_surcharge_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional charge per hour when pilot is required to wait beyond the scheduled boarding time due to vessel delays. Applied when vessel is not ready for pilotage at the agreed time. Nullable if no waiting time charges apply.']

  Note: 'Pilotage fee tariff defining charges for compulsory and voluntary pilotage services. Rates are structured by vessel LOA band, GRT band, DWT band, port zone (inbound, outbound, shifting), time of day (day/night/weekend), and pilotage type (harbour, coastal, river). Captures base pilotage fee, waiting time surcharge, cancellation fee, and extraordinary pilotage conditions. Aligned with marine pilotage scheduling in the VTMS and marine domain.'
}

Table tariff.storage_tariff {
  storage_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the storage tariff schedule record. Primary key.']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Storage rates in port terminals differ significantly by cargo category (reefer, hazmat, general cargo, bulk). Port TOS billing systems require cargo_category master reference for storage tariff lookup']
  compliance_hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hs_code. Business justification: Storage rates vary by commodity classification per HS code. Dangerous goods (IMDG class) and refrigerated cargo require special storage zones with premium rates. Real operational pricing.']
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.demurrage_schedule. Business justification: storage_tariff has demurrage_linkage_flag (BOOLEAN) and demurrage_conversion_day (INT) — these fields explicitly indicate that after a certain storage day, charges convert to demurrage. The FK to demu']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Storage charges are posted to specific GL revenue accounts, separate from handling and dues. Finance teams track storage revenue by cargo type and container status for revenue recognition and demurrag']
  imdg_class_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.imdg_class. Business justification: Storage tariffs for dangerous goods in port terminals reference IMDG class for rate differentiation (Class 1 explosives vs. Class 3 flammables have different storage rates). Replaces denormalized \'imd']
  masterdata_container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Storage rates differentiate by container size/type (container_size_type attribute). Links to ISO container specifications for accurate TEU calculation, yard space allocation, and standardized billing.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Storage tariffs are published as part of the master port tariff schedule. This FK links the storage tariff to its governing port tariff, enabling tariff lifecycle management and ensuring storage rates']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Storage revenue is attributed to yard or warehouse profit centres. Port operators report storage P&L separately from marine and handling services; this link enables that segmentation at the tariff sch']
  superseded_by_tariff_storage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage tariff ID that replaces this tariff schedule, enabling version lineage tracking. Null if current version.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Container storage charges are zone-specific based on zone type, ground slot capacity, reefer availability, and hazmat approval. Direct operational link for storage billing. Replaces denormalized stora']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Warehouse storage tariffs depend on facility type, temperature control, bonded status, and floor load capacity. Natural link for cargo storage billing in CFS and bonded warehouse operations. Essential']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this storage tariff schedule was officially approved by authorized commercial management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized person who approved this storage tariff schedule.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which storage charges are calculated and invoiced. On_exit = charges billed when container leaves storage.']
  container_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the container determining applicable storage rates. Import = inbound cargo awaiting pickup, Export = outbound cargo awaiting vessel loading, Transshipment = cargo in transit between vessels, Empty = empty container, Laden = loaded container.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage tariff record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which storage rates are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Customer service tier classification determining preferential rates and free storage periods. Premium = top-tier customers with volume commitments, Standard = regular contract customers, Basic = occasional customers, Spot = one-time transactions.']
  demurrage_conversion_day varchar [note: 'Day number at which storage charges convert to demurrage charges, if demurrage linkage is enabled. Null if no conversion applies.']
  demurrage_linkage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage tariff is linked to demurrage charges. True = storage charges convert to or trigger demurrage after specified period, False = standalone storage charges only.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this storage tariff schedule becomes applicable for billing purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this storage tariff schedule remains applicable. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  free_storage_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of free storage allowed before storage charges commence. Varies by container status, customer tier, and cargo type.']
  grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours grace period allowed before the first storage day is counted, accommodating operational delays in cargo pickup.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage tariff record was last updated in the system.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum storage charge cap amount that can be billed, protecting customers from excessive charges during extended storage periods.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum storage charge amount that will be billed regardless of calculated daily rate, ensuring cost recovery for administrative overhead.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special conditions, or exceptions applicable to this storage tariff schedule.']
  public_holiday_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether storage charges apply on public holidays. True = holidays are chargeable days, False = holidays excluded from storage day count.']
  rate_band_1_daily_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily storage charge amount for rate band 1, expressed in the tariff currency.']
  rate_band_1_end_day varchar [note: 'Ending day number (inclusive) for the first storage rate band.']
  rate_band_1_start_day varchar [note: 'Starting day number (inclusive) for the first storage rate band, typically immediately after free storage period expires.']
  rate_band_2_daily_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily storage charge amount for rate band 2, typically higher than band 1 to incentivize cargo movement.']
  rate_band_2_end_day varchar [note: 'Ending day number (inclusive) for the second storage rate band. Null indicates open-ended band.']
  rate_band_2_start_day varchar [note: 'Starting day number (inclusive) for the second storage rate band, typically with escalated rates.']
  rate_band_3_daily_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily storage charge amount for rate band 3, typically the highest rate for long-term storage.']
  rate_band_3_end_day varchar [note: 'Ending day number (inclusive) for the third storage rate band. Null indicates open-ended band.']
  rate_band_3_start_day varchar [note: 'Starting day number (inclusive) for the third storage rate band, for extended storage periods.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for storage rate calculation. TEU = Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU = Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, CBM = Cubic Meter.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the storage tariff schedule, used in billing and commercial documentation.']
  tariff_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the storage tariff terms, conditions, and applicability scope.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the storage tariff schedule for business reference and reporting.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the storage tariff schedule. Active = currently in use, Inactive = temporarily disabled, Pending = approved but not yet effective, Superseded = replaced by newer version, Archived = historical record only.']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the storage tariff schedule for change tracking and audit purposes (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']
  tax_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax (VAT, GST, or other applicable sales tax) should be applied to storage charges. True = taxable, False = tax-exempt.']
  weekend_charge_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether storage charges apply on weekends. True = weekends are chargeable days, False = weekends excluded from storage day count.']

  Note: 'Container and cargo storage tariff schedule defining free storage periods and progressive daily storage rates. Captures free days by container status (import, export, transshipment, empty), container type (20ft, 40ft, HC, RF, OOG, IMO/IMDG), storage zone (CY, CFS, ICD), and customer tier. Defines escalating rate bands (e.g., days 1-5 free, days 6-10 rate band 1, days 11+ rate band 2), currency, and applicable demurrage linkage. Critical for yard revenue management.'
}

Table tariff.demurrage_schedule {
  demurrage_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demurrage schedule record. Primary key.']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Demurrage schedules in ports are differentiated by cargo category (dry bulk, liquid bulk, containers, breakbulk have different free-time and rate structures). Replaces denormalized \'cargo_type\' text c']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Demurrage is a significant and distinct revenue line in port finance, posted to dedicated GL accounts. Revenue recognition, dispute resolution reporting, and shipping line reconciliation all require t']
  masterdata_container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Demurrage charges apply to specific container equipment types (container_size_type attribute). Standardized container specifications required for accurate free-time calculation, rate tier application,']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Demurrage schedules are published under a master port tariff. This FK links the demurrage schedule to its governing port tariff, supporting tariff publication reference (tariff_publication_reference f']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Demurrage revenue is attributed to specific commercial or terminal profit centres. P&L reporting for demurrage as a distinct revenue stream requires profit centre attribution at the schedule level.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Demurrage free time and rates vary by terminal zone operational characteristics, congestion levels, and handling capacity. Zone-specific demurrage policies are standard practice for container terminal']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this demurrage schedule was formally approved by the authorized commercial or executive authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this demurrage schedule for activation.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate demurrage days: calendar days (all days including weekends and holidays), working days (excludes weekends), or business days (excludes weekends and public holidays).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demurrage schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which demurrage rates are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Customer service tier or segment to which this demurrage schedule applies, enabling differentiated free time and rates by customer relationship level.']
  demurrage_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demurrage schedule. Active schedules are applied to billing; draft schedules are under review; suspended schedules are temporarily inactive; expired schedules are past their validity period; archived schedules are retained for historical reference only.']
  dispute_resolution_terms varchar [note: 'Terms and conditions governing dispute resolution for demurrage charges under this schedule, including escalation procedures and arbitration clauses.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this demurrage schedule becomes applicable and enforceable for billing purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this demurrage schedule remains valid. Nullable for open-ended schedules.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days allowed before demurrage charges begin to accrue. This is the grace period during which no demurrage is charged.']
  grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Additional grace period in hours beyond the free time days before demurrage calculation begins. Allows for intra-day flexibility.']
  holiday_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether public holidays are excluded from demurrage day calculations. True = holidays excluded, False = holidays included.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this demurrage schedule record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_demurrage_cap decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total demurrage amount that can be charged per unit under this schedule, regardless of overstay duration. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or exceptions applicable to this demurrage schedule.']
  prorated_calculation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partial days are prorated in demurrage calculations. True = partial days are prorated hourly, False = partial days are rounded up to full days.']
  rate_tier_1_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily demurrage charge amount for the first rate tier, applied per unit (container, vessel, or cargo unit) per day.']
  rate_tier_1_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the first demurrage rate tier after free time expires. Demurrage escalates through tiers to incentivize prompt cargo movement.']
  rate_tier_2_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily demurrage charge amount for the second rate tier, typically higher than tier 1 to escalate pressure for cargo removal. Nullable if only one tier is defined.']
  rate_tier_2_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the second demurrage rate tier. Nullable if only one tier is defined.']
  rate_tier_3_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily demurrage charge amount for the third rate tier, typically the highest escalation level. Nullable if fewer than three tiers are defined.']
  rate_tier_3_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the third demurrage rate tier. Nullable if fewer than three tiers are defined.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the demurrage schedule, used in billing and tariff documentation. Externally visible code.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the demurrage schedule for business reference and reporting.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of demurrage schedule by asset type: container demurrage for TEU/FEU overstay, vessel demurrage for berth overstay, cargo demurrage for cargo dwell time, or equipment demurrage for terminal equipment.']
  sla_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this demurrage schedule to a specific Service Level Agreement (SLA) with a customer or customer segment. Nullable if not SLA-linked.']
  tariff_publication_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the official tariff publication document or section where this demurrage schedule is published for regulatory and customer transparency.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade route or geographic lane to which this demurrage schedule applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific). Nullable if schedule applies globally.']
  waiver_authority_level varchar [note: 'Organizational authority level required to approve waivers or reductions of demurrage charges under this schedule.']
  weekend_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether weekends are excluded from demurrage day calculations. True = weekends excluded, False = weekends included.']

  Note: 'Demurrage (DMG) charge schedule defining the daily rates applied when containers or vessels exceed the agreed free time or laytime at the terminal or berth. Captures demurrage rate per container type per day, free time allowance by trade lane and customer tier, escalation tiers, maximum demurrage cap, currency, and applicable dispute resolution rules. Distinct from detention (DET) which applies to equipment off-terminal. Feeds into billing domain for DMG invoice generation.'
}

Table tariff.detention_schedule {
  detention_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the detention charge schedule record. Primary key.']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Detention schedules vary by cargo category in port operations. Consistent cargo classification via cargo_category master is required for detention billing and dispute resolution. Replaces denormalized']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Detention charges are posted to GL accounts distinct from demurrage. Finance teams require this mapping for revenue recognition and shipping line billing reconciliation for equipment detention outside']
  masterdata_container_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.container_type. Business justification: Detention schedules specify rates by container type and equipment category. ISO container master provides standardized specifications for detention calculation, equipment tracking, and billing. Essent']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Detention schedules are published as part of the port tariff framework. Linking detention_schedule to port_tariff establishes the governing tariff context for detention charges, supporting tariff vers']
  superseded_by_schedule_detention_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the detention schedule that replaces this one when status is superseded. Maintains lineage of schedule evolution.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the detention schedule. Schedules must be approved before activation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the authorized person who approved this detention schedule for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the detention schedule was approved for activation.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which detention charges are invoiced to customers. Daily accrual but may be billed in batches.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate detention days. Calendar days include weekends and holidays; working days exclude them.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial contract or SLA (Service Level Agreement) under which this detention schedule is defined.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this detention schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which detention rates are denominated. Typically USD for international maritime operations.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Customer service tier or segment to which this detention schedule applies. Premium customers may receive preferential rates or extended free time.']
  detention_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the detention schedule. Only active schedules are used in billing calculations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this detention schedule becomes active and applicable to new equipment releases.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this detention schedule ceases to be applicable. Nullable for open-ended schedules.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the equipment may be retained outside the terminal gate without incurring detention charges. Free time begins when equipment exits the terminal.']
  grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Additional grace period in hours before detention charges begin after free time expires. Provides operational flexibility.']
  holiday_charge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detention charges apply on public holidays. Some schedules waive charges on recognized port holidays.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this detention schedule record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_detention_cap decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total detention charge that can be assessed per container regardless of duration. Protects customers from unlimited liability in exceptional circumstances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, special conditions, or business rules applicable to this detention schedule.']
  proration_rule varchar [note: 'Rule for prorating detention charges for partial days. Determines whether partial days are charged at full rate, prorated hourly, or rounded.']
  published_date date [note: 'Date when this detention schedule was officially published and communicated to customers and stakeholders.']
  rate_tier_1_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily detention charge amount for the first rate tier. Applied per container per day during tier 1 period.']
  rate_tier_1_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the first detention rate tier after free time expires. Typically days 1-5 of chargeable detention.']
  rate_tier_2_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily detention charge amount for the second rate tier. Typically higher than tier 1 to incentivize prompt equipment return.']
  rate_tier_2_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the second detention rate tier. Typically days 6-10 of chargeable detention with escalated rates.']
  rate_tier_3_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Daily detention charge amount for the third and subsequent rate tiers. Highest rate to strongly incentivize equipment return.']
  rate_tier_3_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the third detention rate tier. Typically days 11+ of chargeable detention with maximum escalation.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the detention schedule, used in billing and tariff documentation. Externally-known unique code.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the detention schedule for business reference and reporting.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'Shipping line or service route to which this detention schedule applies. Different carriers may have negotiated different rates.']
  trade_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of cargo movement to which this detention schedule applies. Rates may differ between import and export operations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this detention schedule. Incremented when schedule is revised to maintain audit trail of rate changes.']
  waiver_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detention charges under this schedule are eligible for waiver or dispute resolution processes.']
  weekend_charge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detention charges apply on weekends. Some schedules waive charges on Saturdays and Sundays.']

  Note: 'Detention (DET) charge schedule defining daily rates applied when port equipment (containers, chassis) is retained by the customer beyond the agreed free period outside the terminal gate. Captures detention rate per container type per day, free time allowance, escalation tiers, maximum detention cap, applicable trade direction (import/export), and currency. Distinct from demurrage (DMG) which applies within the terminal. Managed in NAVIS N4 billing module.'
}

Table tariff.surcharge_rule {
  surcharge_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the surcharge rule record. Primary key.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Surcharges (BAF, CAF, security surcharges) post to specific GL accounts separate from base tariff revenue. Finance teams track surcharge revenue independently for regulatory reporting and index-linked']
  marsec_level_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.marsec_level_change. Business justification: Security surcharges (ISPS surcharge, MARSEC surcharge) are activated by specific MARSEC level changes. A surcharge_rule for security-related charges must reference the MARSEC level change that trigger']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Port-specific surcharge rules (congestion surcharges, environmental levies, security levies) are published by port authorities and are port-scoped. Direct FK to infrastructure.port enables port-level ']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Surcharge rules are layered on top of base tariff rates and are published as part of the port tariff framework. Linking surcharge_rule to port_tariff establishes which master tariff the surcharge appl']
  superseded_by_rule_surcharge_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the surcharge_rule_id that supersedes this rule. Null if this is the current active version. Enables navigation through rule version history.']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: War risk, sanctions, and embargo surcharges are directly derived from specific trade restrictions. surcharge_rule already has sanctions_screening_id but lacks the trade_restriction reference that mand']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Surcharge rules (BAF, CAF, peak season) in port billing are scoped to vessel types. Linking to vessel_type master ensures consistent applicability classification. Replaces denormalized \'vessel_type_ap']
  applicability_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of specific conditions under which this surcharge applies (e.g., applies only during peak season months, applies when fuel price exceeds threshold, applies to vessels over 50,000 DWT). Captures business rules not covered by structured fields.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval and lifecycle status of the surcharge rule: DRAFT (under development), PENDING_APPROVAL (submitted for review), APPROVED (approved but not yet effective), ACTIVE (currently in effect), SUSPENDED (temporarily inactive), EXPIRED (past effective_to_date), CANCELLED (permanently withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|PENDING_APPROVAL|APPROVED|ACTIVE|SUSPENDED|EXPIRED|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user or authority who approved this surcharge rule for publication. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge rule was approved. Null if not yet approved. Supports audit trail and compliance verification.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this surcharge is billed: PER_TRANSACTION (charged on each applicable transaction), MONTHLY (aggregated and billed monthly), QUARTERLY, ANNUALLY. Defines billing cycle for the surcharge.']
  calculation_base varchar [note: 'The base amount upon which percentage-based surcharges are calculated: FREIGHT (ocean freight charges), THC (Terminal Handling Charges), BASE_TARIFF (base port tariff), TOTAL_CHARGES (sum of all charges before surcharge), CARGO_VALUE (declared cargo value). Applicable when calculation_method is PERCENTAGE_OF_BASE.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the surcharge amount: FLAT_FEE (fixed amount per transaction), PERCENTAGE_OF_BASE (percentage of base tariff or freight), PER_UNIT (amount per TEU/FEU/ton), TIERED (rate varies by volume bands), INDEX_LINKED (calculated from external index reference).']
  calculation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority order for applying this surcharge when multiple surcharges are applicable (lower numbers calculated first). Ensures consistent calculation sequence when compounding is allowed.']
  cargo_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Types of cargo to which this surcharge applies: FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), BREAKBULK, RORO (Roll-on Roll-off), DANGEROUS (IMDG), REEFER (Refrigerated), OOG (Out-of-Gauge), ALL. Defines cargo classification scope for the surcharge. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCL|LCL|BREAKBULK|RORO|DANGEROUS|REEFER|OOG|ALL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compounding_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this surcharge can be compounded with other surcharges (true) or must be calculated independently on the base amount only (false). Affects multi-surcharge calculation order and logic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge rule record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the surcharge rate (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Applicable when rate_amount represents a monetary value.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'Currency pair for CAF (Currency Adjustment Factor) surcharges in format BASE/QUOTE (e.g., USD/EUR, EUR/SGD). Indicates the exchange rate relationship used for currency adjustment calculations. Applicable only for CAF surcharge types.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this surcharge rule becomes active and applicable to transactions. Part of the time-series versioning for surcharge rules.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'End date until which this surcharge rule remains active. Null indicates the rule is currently active with no defined end date. Part of the time-series versioning for surcharge rules.']
  exemption_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions under which this surcharge may be waived or exempted (e.g., government vessels exempt, long-term contract customers exempt, vessels with cold ironing capability exempt from environmental surcharge).']
  index_reference_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external index or benchmark used for index-linked surcharges (e.g., Platts Singapore Fuel Oil 380, MOPS Singapore, Bloomberg Commodity Index). Used primarily for BAF calculations. Null if not index-linked.']
  index_reference_url varchar [note: 'URL or reference location for the external index source used in index-linked surcharge calculations. Enables audit trail and verification of index values.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this surcharge rule record was last modified. Updated on any change to the record. Part of audit trail.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum surcharge amount that can be applied regardless of calculated value. Ensures a ceiling charge for the surcharge. Null if no maximum applies.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum surcharge amount that must be applied regardless of calculated value. Ensures a floor charge for the surcharge. Null if no minimum applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments about this surcharge rule for operational reference. Not published to customers. May include rationale for rate changes, special handling instructions, or historical context.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before this surcharge becomes effective. Ensures compliance with contractual or regulatory notice requirements.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method for prorating the surcharge when applicable period is partial: NONE (no proration, full charge applies), DAILY (prorated by days), MONTHLY (prorated by months), PROPORTIONAL (prorated by usage or volume). Applicable for time-based or usage-based surcharges.']
  publication_authority varchar [note: 'Entity or regulatory body that published or mandated this surcharge (e.g., Port Authority, Shipping Line, Government Agency, Industry Association). Establishes the source of authority for the surcharge.']
  published_date date [note: 'Date when this surcharge rule was officially published and communicated to customers and stakeholders. May differ from effective_from_date to allow advance notice period.']
  rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric rate value for the surcharge. Interpretation depends on calculation_method: flat fee amount, percentage value (e.g., 5.5 for 5.5%), or per-unit rate. Null if index-linked calculation is used.']
  rate_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage rate for surcharges calculated as percentage of base amount (e.g., 3.5000 represents 3.5%). Used when calculation_method is PERCENTAGE_OF_BASE. Null for non-percentage methods.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulation, tariff schedule, or legal instrument that authorizes or mandates this surcharge (e.g., Port Tariff Schedule 2024, ISPS Code Amendment, Government Gazette No. 12345).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the surcharge rule (e.g., BAF_2024_Q1, CAF_USD_EUR, ISPS_SECURITY). Used for external reference and integration with billing systems.']
  service_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Port services to which this surcharge applies (e.g., Wharfage, Pilotage, Towage, Storage, ALL). Pipe-separated list if multiple services apply. Null indicates applicability to all services.']
  surcharge_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the surcharge for display and reporting purposes (e.g., Bunker Adjustment Factor - Asia Pacific, Currency Adjustment Factor USD/EUR).']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the category of surcharge: BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor), CAF (Currency Adjustment Factor), PIL (Port Infrastructure Levy), ISPS (International Ship and Port Facility Security), PEAK_SEASON, IMDG (International Maritime Dangerous Goods), OOG (Out-of-Gauge), COLD_IRONING, THC_ADJUSTMENT (Terminal Handling Charge Adjustment), CONGESTION, REEFER (Refrigerated Container), SECURITY. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAF|CAF|PIL|ISPS|PEAK_SEASON|IMDG|OOG|COLD_IRONING|THC_ADJUSTMENT|CONGESTION|REEFER|SECURITY — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trade_lane_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or trade lane scope to which this surcharge applies (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Trans-Pacific, Europe-Asia, Intra-Asia, ALL). Defines the routing or origin-destination pairs covered by this surcharge rule.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for per-unit surcharges: TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), TON (metric ton), CBM (Cubic Meter), CONTAINER, VESSEL_CALL, TRANSACTION. Null for percentage-based or flat-fee surcharges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|TON|CBM|CONTAINER|VESSEL_CALL|TRANSACTION — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this surcharge rule. Incremented each time the rule is revised. Supports time-series versioning and historical reconstruction for billing disputes and audits.']

  Note: 'Unified definition of all applicable surcharges and adjustment factors layered on top of base tariff rates, maintained as versioned time-series records. Covers BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor) with fuel index references (Platts, MOPS, Singapore), calculation method (flat per TEU or percentage), and trade lane scope; CAF (Currency Adjustment Factor) with currency pair, percentage, and calculation base (percentage of freight or THC); PIL (Port Infrastructure Levy); ISPS security surcharge; peak season surcharge; hazardous cargo (IMDG) surcharge; OOG (out-of-gauge) surcharge; cold ironing surcharge; and any other periodic or conditional surcharge. Each record defines the surcharge type discriminator, calculation method (flat fee, percentage of base, per unit), rate amount or percentage, applicable index reference, effective period, trade lane scope, vessel type applicability, publication authority, and applicability conditions. New surcharge types are added as new rows — no schema changes required. Enables historical surcharge reconstruction for billing disputes and audit.'
}

Table tariff.discount_scheme {
  discount_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the discount scheme. Primary key.']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Port discount schemes are restricted to specific cargo categories (e.g., reefer discount, bulk cargo volume discount). Linking to cargo_category master enables consistent eligibility checking. Replace']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Discounts reduce revenue posted to specific contra-revenue GL accounts. Finance teams require this mapping for net revenue reporting, discount budget tracking, and P&L impact analysis of commercial di']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Discount schemes are negotiated between terminal operators and shipping lines and are terminal-scoped commercial agreements. The existing terminal_restriction plain-text column is a denormalization of']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Discount schemes are commercial discounts applicable to port tariff charges. Linking discount_scheme to port_tariff establishes the governing tariff under which the discount applies, resolving the sil']
  applicable_charge_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tariff charge codes to which this discount applies (e.g., THC, WHR, DMG, DET, PIL, BAF, CAF). Empty means applies to all charges.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the authority who approved this discount scheme (e.g., Commercial Director, CFO, Pricing Committee). Required for governance and audit trail.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this discount scheme was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the approval decision (e.g., board resolution number, approval memo reference).']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount should be automatically applied when eligibility criteria are met (True) or requires manual application by billing staff (False).']
  billing_system_code varchar [note: 'Code used to identify this discount scheme in the billing system (e.g., NAVIS N4, SAP S/4HANA). Enables integration and automated discount application.']
  combinable_with_other_discounts boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discount schemes on the same transaction. True allows stacking, False requires exclusive application.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the master service contract or commercial agreement under which this discount scheme is granted. Links discount to contractual obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount scheme record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_tier_eligibility varchar [note: 'Customer tier classification that is eligible for this discount scheme. Defines which customer segments can access this pricing benefit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|vip|strategic|standard — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  customer_type_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer types eligible for this discount (e.g., shipping_line, freight_forwarder, cargo_owner, nvocc). Empty means all customer types are eligible.']
  discount_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the discount scheme: promotional campaign, contractual agreement, volume incentive, loyalty reward, seasonal offer, or strategic partnership discount.']
  discount_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for flat rate discounts. Null for percentage or free days discount types.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanism: percentage reduction, flat rate deduction, free storage days, tiered pricing, volume-based rebate, or loyalty-based incentive.']
  discount_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount. Interpretation depends on discount_type: percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%), flat rate (monetary amount), or number of free days.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this discount scheme becomes active and can be applied to qualifying transactions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this discount scheme expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended scheme with no expiration.']
  maximum_discount_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value of discount that can be applied per transaction or period, regardless of calculated discount amount. Null means no cap applies.']
  minimum_charge_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum charge amount below which the discount cannot be applied. Ensures discounts are only given on transactions above a certain value.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this discount scheme record. Maintains accountability for changes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount scheme record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update for audit and change management purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or business context related to this discount scheme.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for discount application when multiple schemes are eligible. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = highest priority).']
  promotional_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this discount to a specific marketing or promotional campaign. Used for tracking campaign effectiveness and ROI.']
  requires_customer_request boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must explicitly request this discount (True) or if it can be applied proactively by the port (False).']
  retroactive_application_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be applied retroactively to past transactions within the effective period (True) or only to future transactions (False).']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the discount scheme, used for external reference and integration with billing systems.']
  scheme_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the discount scheme including its purpose, eligibility criteria, and business rationale.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the discount scheme for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discount scheme: draft (being designed), pending approval (awaiting authorization), active (in use), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past effective date), or cancelled (terminated before expiration).']
  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount is tied to Service Level Agreement (SLA) performance metrics. True means discount is conditional on meeting service standards.']
  sla_performance_metric varchar [note: 'Description of the SLA performance metric that must be met for this discount to apply (e.g., vessel turnaround time < 24 hours, container dwell time < 5 days).']
  threshold_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which the threshold is measured and evaluated: per vessel call, monthly, quarterly, annually, or over the entire contract term.']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualifying threshold that must be met to earn the discount: TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit) volume, vessel call frequency, cargo tonnage, revenue value, container count, or none for unconditional discounts.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold value: TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit), FEU (Forty-foot Equivalent Unit), vessel calls, metric tonnes (MT), cubic meters (CBM), container count, or currency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|calls|tonnes|MT|CBM|containers|USD|EUR — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value that must be met or exceeded to qualify for the discount. Interpretation depends on threshold_type (e.g., 1000 TEU, 12 calls, 50000 tonnes).']
  vessel_type_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of vessel types eligible for this discount (e.g., container_ship, bulk_carrier, tanker, roro_vessel). Empty means no vessel type restriction.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this discount scheme record. Required for governance and accountability.']

  Note: 'Commercial discount scheme defining volume-based, loyalty-based, or promotional discounts applicable to port service charges. Captures discount scheme name, discount type (percentage, flat rate, free days), applicable tariff items or charge codes, qualifying thresholds (TEU volume, call frequency, cargo tonnage), customer tier eligibility, effective period, and approval authority. Enables the port to offer competitive pricing to high-volume shipping lines and cargo owners.'
}

Table tariff.pricing_rule {
  pricing_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing rule. Primary key.']
  applicable_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Specific rate card identifier that this pricing rule selects or modifies when trigger conditions are met.']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules based on berth availability, draft restrictions, shore power availability, and crane capacity. Real-time tariff adjustments depend on berth operational status. Required for berth']
  cargo_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.cargo_category. Business justification: Port pricing rules are triggered by cargo type in billing engines (e.g., hazmat surcharge trigger, reefer premium trigger). Linking to cargo_category master ensures consistent trigger classification. ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Pricing rules are often scoped to specific cost centres (e.g., terminal-specific automated pricing). Pricing governance and audit trails require linking automated pricing rules to the responsible cost']
  discount_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.discount_scheme. Business justification: pricing_rule has discount_percentage as a configurable output. Linking to discount_scheme identifies the commercial discount scheme that the pricing rule applies, enabling discount eligibility validat']
  free_time_allowance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.free_time_allowance. Business justification: pricing_rule has free_time_days as a configurable parameter. Linking to free_time_allowance identifies the master free time definition that the pricing rule grants or modifies, ensuring free time gran']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Pricing rules that override standard tariffs require GL account mapping for the override revenue or cost adjustment posting. Finance controllers need this to audit pricing overrides and ensure correct']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.item. Business justification: A pricing rule applies conditional logic for charge calculation on specific tariff items. Linking pricing_rule to item identifies which billable service item the rule governs, enabling item-level pric']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Specific customer identifier to which this pricing rule applies. Null indicates the rule applies to all customers meeting other trigger conditions.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Specific port tariff identifier that this pricing rule selects or modifies when trigger conditions are met.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Pricing rules drive revenue for specific profit centres and must be linked for profitability analysis. Financial planning and revenue forecasting require understanding which automated pricing rules ap']
  superseded_by_rule_pricing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing rule that supersedes this rule, establishing version lineage and rule evolution history.']
  surcharge_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.surcharge_rule. Business justification: pricing_rule has surcharge_percentage as a trigger/output field. Linking to surcharge_rule identifies the specific surcharge rule that the pricing rule applies or references, enabling rule-based surch']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Replaces denormalized trigger_terminal_zone with proper FK f']
  trade_restriction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_restriction. Business justification: Pricing rules must exclude restricted/prohibited goods from standard tariff application. Compliance requirement to prevent handling of embargoed cargo at port facilities.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Linking to vessel_type master ensures consistent vessel classification in rule evaluation. Rep']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing rule requires management approval before it can be activated and used in billing calculations.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing rule was approved for activation.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing rule is automatically applied by the billing engine when trigger conditions are met, or requires manual approval.']
  conflict_resolution_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to resolve conflicts when multiple pricing rules with the same priority could apply to the same billable event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for amounts defined in this pricing rule.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied by this pricing rule when trigger conditions are met.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the pricing rule becomes active and applicable to billing calculations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which the pricing rule remains active. Null indicates open-ended applicability.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before demurrage or detention charges apply, as defined by this pricing rule.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum charge amount cap enforced by this pricing rule, ensuring that no charge exceeds this threshold regardless of calculation.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum charge amount enforced by this pricing rule, ensuring that no charge falls below this threshold regardless of calculation.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing rule record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the pricing rule for operational reference.']
  priority_order varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used for conflict resolution when multiple pricing rules could apply to the same billable event. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the pricing rule, used for external reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the pricing rule purpose, business rationale, and application context for business user reference.']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'Formal expression or decision logic defining the rule conditions and calculation formula, stored in a structured or semi-structured format for execution by the billing engine.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pricing rule describing its purpose and application context.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing rule indicating whether it is in use, pending approval, or retired.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing rule logic type determining how charges are calculated or tariffs are selected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tiered_pricing|conditional_surcharge|minimum_charge_enforcement|free_period_calculation|tariff_selection|applicability_matrix|conflict_resolution — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing rule is linked to SLA performance metrics, affecting rate selection based on service delivery.']
  surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied by this pricing rule when trigger conditions are met (e.g., BAF, CAF, peak season surcharge).']
  trigger_cargo_weight_max_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum cargo weight threshold in kilograms that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for weight-based tiered pricing.']
  trigger_cargo_weight_min_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum cargo weight threshold in kilograms that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for weight-based tiered pricing.']
  trigger_container_type varchar [note: 'Container type condition that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule (e.g., 20ft dry, 40ft dry, 40ft high cube, reefer, tank, flat rack). Pipe-separated list for multiple values.']
  trigger_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment condition that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule (e.g., shipping line, freight forwarder, cargo owner, government). Pipe-separated list for multiple values.']
  trigger_grt_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum gross registered tonnage that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for vessel tonnage-based pricing.']
  trigger_grt_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum gross registered tonnage that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for vessel tonnage-based pricing.']
  trigger_loa_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel length overall in meters that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for vessel size-based pricing.']
  trigger_loa_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel length overall in meters that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for vessel size-based pricing.']
  trigger_service_type varchar [note: 'Service type condition that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule (e.g., THC, wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, detention, storage). Pipe-separated list for multiple values.']
  trigger_trade_direction varchar [note: 'Trade direction condition that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule, determining whether the cargo is inbound, outbound, or in transit.']
  trigger_trade_lane varchar [note: 'Trade lane or route condition that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific, Transatlantic). Pipe-separated list for multiple values.']
  trigger_volume_max_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum volume threshold in TEU that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for volume-based tiered pricing.']
  trigger_volume_min_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum volume threshold in TEU that triggers the applicability of this pricing rule. Used for volume-based tiered pricing or minimum commitment enforcement.']

  Note: 'Configurable business rule defining conditional logic for charge calculation, rate selection, tariff applicability, and conflict resolution. Captures rule name, rule type (tiered pricing, conditional surcharge, minimum charge enforcement, free period calculation, tariff selection, applicability matrix, conflict resolution), trigger conditions (cargo type, vessel class, trade direction, customer segment, terminal zone, service type, trade lane), priority order for conflict resolution when multiple tariffs could apply, effective period, and rule expression or decision logic. Includes the full applicability matrix specifying which tariff applies to which combination of customer segment, vessel type, cargo category, trade lane, terminal zone, and service type — serving as the deterministic tariff selection engine. Used by the billing engine and TOS charge calculation to select the correct tariff and rate for each billable event without ambiguity. New applicability dimensions are added as rule conditions — no separate entity required.'
}

  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this free time allowance rule becomes active and applicable to new transactions.']

  free_time_type varchar [note: 'Category of free time allowance: import free days (container dwell before storage charges), export free days (pre-shipment dwell), transshipment free days (in-transit dwell), equipment free days (chassis/equipment rental grace period), storage free days (yard storage grace), or demurrage free days (vessel delay grace).']
  holiday_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether public holidays are excluded from the free time calculation. True means holidays do not count toward free days.']

  priority_order varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for rule evaluation when multiple free time allowances could apply to the same transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  reefer_cargo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this free time allowance applies to refrigerated (reefer) cargo. True means it applies to reefer containers.']
  start_trigger_event varchar [note: 'The operational event that triggers the start of the free time period: vessel discharge, gate-in, customs release, delivery order (D/O) issue, container available for pickup, or berth departure.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade lane or shipping route to which this free time allowance applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Transpacific, Intra-Asia). Null indicates all trade lanes.']
  vessel_category varchar [note: 'Category of vessel to which this free time allowance applies: feeder, panamax, post-panamax, ultra-large container vessel, or all categories.']
  weekend_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether weekends (Saturday and Sunday) are excluded from the free time calculation. True means weekends do not count toward free days.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this free time allowance record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of free time periods granted to customers before storage, demurrage, or detention charges commence. Captures free time type (import free days, export free days, transshipment free days, equipment free days), applicable container type, trade lane, customer tier, number of free days, calculation basis (calendar days vs. working days), and holiday exclusion rules. The SSOT for free time parameters consumed by the storage, demurrage, and detention charge engines.'
}
