  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum billable amount for a towage operation regardless of calculated rate, ensuring cost recovery for tug mobilization.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this towage tariff record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special conditions, or clarifications regarding the application of this towage tariff.']
  number_of_tugs_required varchar [note: 'Standard number of tug vessels required for the towage operation under this tariff, based on vessel size and port conditions.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Type of towage operation covered by this tariff: berthing (vessel arrival to berth), unberthing (vessel departure from berth), shifting (vessel movement between berths), emergency (unscheduled assistance), escort (harbor transit), or standby (tug on standby).']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this towage tariff schedule was published and made available to port users and stakeholders.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the towage tariff rate calculation: per operation (flat fee), per hour (time-based), per tug (per vessel), per GRT, or per DWT.']
  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this towage tariff is linked to a Service Level Agreement with performance guarantees and potential penalties or rebates.']
  standby_time_rate_per_hour decimal(12,2) [note: 'Hourly rate charged when tugs are on standby awaiting vessel readiness or operational clearance beyond the included free standby time.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the towage tariff schedule, used for external reference and billing system integration.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the towage tariff schedule for business user identification.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the towage tariff schedule in the approval and publication workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|expired|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the towage tariff schedule, incremented with each revision or amendment.']
  time_of_day_category varchar [note: 'Time period classification for towage operations affecting the applicable rate: standard hours (normal business hours), after hours, night shift, weekend, or public holiday.']
  tug_bollard_pull_min_tonnes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum bollard pull capacity in tonnes required for tugs performing this towage operation. Bollard pull is the pulling force a tug can exert.']

  Note: 'Towage service tariff defining charges for tug assistance during vessel berthing, unberthing, and shifting operations. Rates are structured by vessel GRT band, DWT band, number of tugs required, tug bollard pull rating, operation type (berthing, unberthing, shifting, emergency), time of day, and port zone. Captures base towage fee, standby time rate, cancellation fee, and extraordinary towage conditions. Aligned with marine towage operations in the marine domain.'
}

  mooring_tariff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the mooring tariff record. Primary key.']

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Mooring fees are a distinct marine services revenue stream posted to specific GL accounts. Revenue recognition for mooring gang operations and marine services financial reporting require this GL accou']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Mooring tariffs are published by the port authority at port level. Port-level mooring tariff management, regulatory filing, and vessel call invoicing require direct reference to infrastructure.port. E']
  port_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.port_location. Business justification: Mooring services are berth/zone-specific (berth_zone attribute). Location determines mooring gang requirements, equipment availability, and rates. Links to port location master for berth-specific tari']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Mooring tariffs are port service charges published under the master port tariff schedule. Linking mooring_tariff to port_tariff establishes the governing tariff context for line-handling service charg']
  superseded_by_tariff_mooring_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer mooring tariff record that replaces this one, maintaining tariff version history and lineage.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Mooring charges in ports are differentiated by vessel category (gang count, mooring type vary by vessel class). Maritime tariff experts expect mooring_tariff to reference vessel_type master. Replaces ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this mooring tariff was officially approved for implementation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the tariff: pending (awaiting review), approved (authorized for use), or rejected (not authorized).']
  base_mooring_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard charge amount for the mooring or unmooring service under normal conditions, excluding surcharges and additional fees.']
  cancellation_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Charge applied when a scheduled mooring service is cancelled within the notice period, compensating for crew mobilization costs.']
  cancellation_notice_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours advance notice required to cancel a mooring service without incurring the cancellation fee.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mooring tariff record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all tariff amounts are denominated.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Customer segmentation level for differential pricing: standard (public tariff), preferred (volume customer), premium (strategic account), or contract (negotiated rate).']
  discount_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff rate is eligible for additional volume discounts or promotional adjustments.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this mooring tariff becomes valid and applicable for billing purposes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this mooring tariff remains valid. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Description of automatic rate adjustment provisions based on inflation indices, fuel costs, or other economic factors.']
  free_waiting_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Grace period in minutes during which no waiting time surcharge is applied before the mooring gang begins work.']
  grt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum gross registered tonnage for this tariff band. Null indicates no upper limit for the band.']
  grt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum gross registered tonnage for this tariff band. GRT is the total internal volume of a vessel expressed in register tons.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mooring tariff record was most recently updated or modified.']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel length overall in meters for this tariff band. Null indicates no upper limit for the band.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel length overall in meters for this tariff band. LOA is the maximum length of a vessel\'s hull measured parallel to the waterline.']
  maximum_charge_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum billable amount ceiling for the mooring service to protect customers from excessive charges in extended operations. Null indicates no cap.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum billable amount for the mooring service regardless of actual time or resources used, ensuring cost recovery for mobilization.']
  mooring_gang_count varchar [note: 'Number of mooring gangs (line-handling crews) deployed for the service covered by this tariff rate.']
  mooring_type varchar [note: 'Classification of mooring configuration: conventional (alongside berth), buoy mooring, single-point mooring (SPM), multi-buoy, or dolphin mooring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or internal documentation regarding this mooring tariff.']
  public_tariff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff is publicly published and available to all customers, or is a private negotiated rate.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this tariff was published or made available to customers and stakeholders.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit basis for the tariff rate calculation: per operation (single mooring event), per gang (crew unit), per hour (time-based), or per line (individual mooring line).']
  regulatory_filing_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff must be filed with or approved by maritime regulatory authorities before implementation.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of mooring service covered by this tariff: mooring (arrival line-handling), unmooring (departure line-handling), or combined (both services).']
  sla_linked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tariff is tied to specific service level agreement performance metrics, with potential penalties or credits for non-compliance.']
  sla_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Committed maximum time in minutes from service request to mooring gang arrival at vessel, applicable when SLA is linked.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Additional terms, restrictions, or requirements that apply to this mooring tariff, such as weather limitations or equipment prerequisites.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the mooring tariff schedule, used for external reference and billing system integration.']
  tariff_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the mooring tariff schedule for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  tariff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mooring tariff: draft (under development), active (in use), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past validity), or superseded (replaced by newer version).']
  tariff_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this tariff schedule in major.minor format, enabling tracking of tariff evolution and changes over time.']
  time_of_day_category varchar [note: 'Time period classification for differential pricing: day (standard business hours), night (after-hours), weekend, or holiday operations.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade route or shipping lane to which this tariff applies, enabling route-specific pricing strategies.']
  waiting_time_surcharge_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional charge per hour when mooring gang is on standby waiting for vessel readiness or berth availability beyond the free waiting period.']

  Note: 'Mooring and unmooring service tariff defining charges for line-handling services during vessel arrival and departure. Rates are structured by vessel LOA band, GRT band, mooring type (conventional, buoy, single-point), time of day (day/night/weekend/holiday), and number of mooring gangs deployed. Captures base mooring fee, waiting time surcharge, and cancellation fee. Supports marine services billing in the marine and billing domains.'
}

Table tariff.port_dues_schedule {
  port_dues_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the port dues schedule record. Primary key for this entity.']
  anchorage_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.anchorage_area. Business justification: Anchorage dues are standard maritime charges for vessels at anchorage. Dues vary by anchorage area designation, security zone, and holding duration. Essential for anchorage-specific billing in vessel ']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.berth. Business justification: Port dues often calculated based on berth utilization, berth depth, and LOA capacity. Regulatory filings and tariff schedules reference specific berths for dues calculation. Required for berth-specifi']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Port dues are national-level charges subject to country-specific regulations, international maritime conventions (SOLAS, MARPOL), and trade agreements. Country context determines regulatory framework,']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Port dues are a primary statutory revenue stream with dedicated GL accounts mandated by port authority regulations. Statutory financial reporting and regulatory compliance require this GL account mapp']
  isps_facility_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.isps_facility_record. Business justification: Port dues schedules include ISPS security levies tied to facility security level. Real cost recovery mechanism for maritime security compliance mandated by SOLAS.']
  masterdata_flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Port dues often have flag state differentials (flag_state attribute). Bilateral maritime agreements, reciprocity arrangements, and flag state performance affect rates. Links to flag state master for t']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Port dues schedules are levied at port level by the port authority. Dues billing, vessel call invoicing, and regulatory reporting all require direct reference to the operational port. Existing berth a']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Port dues schedules define statutory vessel call charges that are published as part of the master port tariff. Linking port_dues_schedule to port_tariff establishes the governing tariff authority for ']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Port dues revenue is attributed to specific profit centres for statutory and management reporting. Port authority financial statements require dues revenue to be reported by profit centre for regulato']
  superseded_by_schedule_port_dues_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port dues schedule that replaces this schedule. Nullable for current active schedules. Maintains version history and audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Port dues are fundamentally structured around vessel type alongside GRT/LOA bands. Regulatory port dues filings and billing require vessel_type master reference. Replaces denormalized \'vessel_type\' te']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority officially approved this port dues schedule for implementation.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base monetary amount for the port dues charge before any discounts, surcharges, or adjustments. Represents the standard statutory rate for the defined vessel classification and dues type.']
  call_frequency_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to port dues for vessels meeting the call frequency tier criteria. Incentivizes regular service commitments and port loyalty.']
  call_frequency_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier based on vessel call frequency at the port. First call applies to vessels making their inaugural visit, regular applies to standard service schedules, frequent applies to high-frequency callers, and premium applies to vessels with committed service agreements. Higher frequency tiers may attract discounted dues rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port dues schedule record was first created in the system. Part of mandatory audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the port dues are denominated and payable.']
  dangerous_goods_surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional percentage surcharge applied to port dues for vessels carrying International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) classified cargo. Reflects additional risk management and safety oversight costs.']
  dues_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the statutory port dues charge type levied on vessels. Light dues cover navigational aids, conservancy dues cover channel maintenance, port entry fee is the basic vessel call charge, anchorage dues apply to vessels at anchor, navigation dues cover vessel traffic services, and pilotage dues cover mandatory pilotage services.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this port dues schedule becomes legally binding and applicable to vessel calls. Aligns with regulatory publication requirements.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this port dues schedule remains valid. Nullable for open-ended schedules subject to regulatory review.']
  environmental_levy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage levy added to port dues to fund environmental protection initiatives, emissions monitoring, and compliance with MARPOL (Marine Pollution Convention) requirements.']
  exemption_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the criteria under which vessels may be exempt from port dues under this schedule. Typically includes naval vessels, government vessels on official duty, vessels in distress, and humanitarian mission vessels.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dues schedule includes provisions for exemptions. True if certain vessel categories (e.g., naval vessels, emergency response vessels, vessels in distress) may be exempt from dues under this schedule.']
  grt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Gross Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band to which this dues schedule applies. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  grt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band to which this dues schedule applies. GRT is the primary metric for vessel-based port dues calculation.']
  late_payment_penalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage penalty applied to overdue port dues payments. Calculated as a percentage of the outstanding amount per period (typically per month or per day).']
  loa_band_max_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel Length Overall in meters for this dues schedule band. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  loa_band_min_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel Length Overall in meters for this dues schedule band. LOA may be used as a supplementary classification criterion for berth allocation and dues calculation.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum port dues charge amount applicable as a cap. Protects large vessel operators from excessive dues and maintains port competitiveness for mega-vessels.']
  measurement_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which call frequency is measured for discount eligibility. Typically 30, 90, or 365 days depending on service agreement terms.']
  minimum_calls_per_period varchar [note: 'Minimum number of vessel calls required within the measurement period to qualify for the call frequency discount. Used to enforce service commitment thresholds.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum port dues charge amount applicable regardless of calculated rate. Ensures a floor revenue per vessel call for small vessels where tonnage-based calculation would yield negligible amounts.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this port dues schedule record was last modified in the system. Part of mandatory audit trail for regulatory compliance and version control.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or clarifications regarding the application of this port dues schedule. May include references to specific regulatory provisions or commercial agreements.']
  nrt_band_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum Net Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band. Nullable for open-ended upper bands.']
  nrt_band_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Net Registered Tonnage for the vessel size band. NRT represents the earning capacity of the vessel and may be used as an alternative or supplementary metric for dues calculation in some jurisdictions.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from vessel departure or invoice date within which port dues must be paid. Standard terms are typically 7, 14, or 30 days depending on customer credit standing.']
  port_dues_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the port dues schedule. Draft schedules are under development, active schedules are in force, suspended schedules are temporarily inactive, expired schedules have passed their validity period, superseded schedules have been replaced by newer versions, and withdrawn schedules have been permanently removed.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this port dues schedule was published in the official gazette or public tariff book. Statutory port dues must be publicly disclosed prior to enforcement.']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base rate calculation. Per GRT and per NRT are tonnage-based, per call is a flat fee per vessel visit, per meter LOA is length-based, per day applies to anchorage dues, and flat fee is a fixed charge regardless of vessel size.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or gazette notification number issued by the regulatory authority approving this port dues schedule. Required for legal enforceability and audit compliance.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the government or regulatory body that has jurisdiction over this port dues schedule. Port dues are statutory charges requiring regulatory approval and oversight.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the port dues schedule, used for external reference and regulatory reporting.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the port dues schedule for business identification and reporting purposes.']
  security_levy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage levy added to port dues to fund International Ship and Port Facility Security (ISPS) Code compliance measures and port security infrastructure.']
  trade_type varchar [note: 'Classification of trade type for which this dues schedule applies. International trade involves cross-border cargo, coastal trade is between domestic ports, domestic trade is within national waters, cabotage is domestic cargo carriage reserved for national flag vessels, and transshipment involves cargo transfer without customs clearance.']

  Note: 'Port dues (port call charges) schedule defining the statutory charges levied on vessels calling at the port. Captures dues type (light dues, conservancy dues, port entry fee, anchorage dues), vessel classification (GRT band, vessel type, flag state), trade type (international, coastal, domestic), call frequency discounts, and regulatory authority reference. Distinct from cargo-based wharfage; port dues are vessel-based charges payable by the shipowner or agent per vessel call.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|paid|partially_paid|disputed|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this invoice record. Audit trail for change tracking.']

  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date payment was received and cleared in the port authority bank account. Used for cash flow reporting and revenue recognition.']

  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms defining the number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Determines due date calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|prepaid — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pod_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port of discharge for the cargo. Used for trade lane analysis and tariff determination.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or special instructions related to this invoice. Used for billing notes, customer communications, or internal annotations.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this invoice is recognized in financial statements per IFRS 15 criteria. May differ from invoice date.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI accounting document number for this invoice. Links billing record to financial accounting system of record.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Primary category of port service being billed. Includes Terminal Handling Charge (THC), wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, detention, and other maritime services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: thc|wharfage|pilotage|demurrage|detention|towage|mooring|berth_hire|reefer|storage — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of all line items before taxes, adjustments, and discounts. Base amount for revenue calculation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes per jurisdiction.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the tax exemption certificate on file justifying tax exemption status. Required for audit compliance.']
  tax_exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is exempt from taxation due to customer status, service type, or regulatory exemption.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code determining which tax rates and rules apply to this invoice. ISO country code or regional tax authority identifier.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total amount due including subtotal, taxes, discounts, and adjustments. Net amount payable by customer.']

  Note: 'Core billing document issued to port community participants (shipping lines, freight forwarders, cargo owners) for all port services rendered. Generated from NAVIS N4 TOS and integrated with SAP S/4HANA FI. Captures invoice number, invoice date, due date, billing period, total amount, currency, tax amount, invoice status (draft/issued/paid/disputed/cancelled), payment terms, vessel call reference, BOL reference, service type (THC, wharfage, pilotage, demurrage, detention), port of loading/discharge, and SAP document number. SSOT for all revenue billing documents.'
}

  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Cargo storage charges must reference which warehouse facility provided the service for accurate billing and warehouse-level revenue tracking. Essential for bonded vs non-bonded storage differentiation']
  adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a billing adjustment or correction. True for adjustments (credit notes, rebills), False for original charges.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with this line item. Links the charge to the specific cargo shipment documentation.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 container identification number if this line item relates to a specific container. Format: 4 letters (owner code) + 7 digits (serial number + check digit).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (unit rate, line amount, tax amount, net amount).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of discount applied to this line item. Reduces the line amount before tax calculation.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any discount applied to this line item. References promotional discounts, volume discounts, or contractual rate reductions.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is under dispute by the customer. True if disputed, False otherwise. Affects accounts receivable aging and collection processes.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for dispute if dispute_flag is True. Captures customer objections and billing discrepancies.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount for this line item before tax. Calculated as quantity multiplied by unit rate, plus any applicable adjustments.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and display sequence of line items on the invoice document.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data governance.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount for this line item including tax. Sum of line amount and tax amount. Contributes to the invoice total.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this line item. Captures special instructions, billing clarifications, or operational context.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the service rendered. May represent TEUs, FEUs, CBM, hours, moves, or other units depending on the service type.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue for this line item is recognized in the financial statements. May differ from invoice date based on revenue recognition policies.']
  service_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service was completed. Used to calculate duration-based charges and service level agreement compliance.']
  service_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service commenced. Used for time-based charges such as storage, demurrage, and detention calculations.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item. Derived from line amount multiplied by tax rate.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining the tax treatment for this line item. References the tax jurisdiction and rate applicable to the service.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10% tax).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity. TEU=Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit, FEU=Forty-foot Equivalent Unit, CBM=Cubic Meter. Aligns with maritime logistics industry standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|CBM|HOUR|MOVE|TON|UNIT|DAY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Rate per unit of measure. The price charged for one unit of the service (e.g., rate per TEU, rate per hour).']

  Note: 'Individual line items on a port services invoice, each representing a discrete chargeable service or tariff component. Captures line number, service code, service description, tariff item reference, quantity (TEUs, FEUs, CBM, hours, moves), unit of measure, unit rate, line amount, tax code, tax amount, net amount, container number, BOL reference, vessel call ID, charge category (THC, WHR, DMG, DET, BAF, CAF, pilotage, towage, storage), and NAVIS N4 charge code. Enables granular revenue analysis at the service level.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment record. Primary key for the payment entity.']
  call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Advance payments and pre-payments for vessel calls are made against call bookings before invoice generation. Port finance teams track advance payments against specific call bookings for booking confir']
  cargo_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Freight forwarders and shippers pre-pay THC and cargo handling charges against cargo bookings before vessel arrival. Port finance teams must link advance cargo payments to the originating cargo bookin']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payments must be attributed to a legal entity for bank account management, statutory cash reporting, and intercompany reconciliation in multi-entity port operators. Company code on payment is a standa']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Payment cost center attribution enables cash collection performance tracking by operational unit. Port finance teams measure collection efficiency by terminal or service division for working capital m']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Payments are allocated to billing cycles for period accounting and cash flow reporting. This FK enables cycle-based payment analysis. Keeps fiscal_year and fiscal_period on payment as execution-specif']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cash receipts post to bank GL accounts for cash accounting. Daily bank reconciliation process requires linking payments to specific bank account GL codes for treasury management and cash flow reportin']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to billing document.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cash receipt postings create journal entries in the GL; linking payment to its journal entry is required for bank reconciliation, audit trail, and SAP FI cash application reporting. Port treasury team']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account making the payment. Identifies the payer.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Cash receipts attributed to profit centers for cash-basis divisional reporting. Port authorities track cash realization by business segment for liquidity planning and capital allocation decisions.']
  receivable_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.receivable_account. Business justification: A payment is made against a customer\'s receivable account — the receivable_account is the authoritative AR master for each port community participant in the billing domain. Linking payment.receivable_']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been allocated to specific invoices. May be less than amount_paid if payment is partially applied or held on account.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid in the transaction currency. Represents the gross payment received before any adjustments or allocations.']
  bank_reference varchar [note: 'Bank transaction reference or confirmation number provided by the financial institution. Used for payment tracing and dispute resolution.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount converted to the port\'s base reporting currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the payment was submitted (online portal, bank transfer, Port Community System, SAP, manual entry, or Electronic Data Interchange).']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was cleared and funds were confirmed available in the port\'s bank account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, SGD).']
  discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount claimed by the customer and deducted from the invoice total. Reflects payment terms incentives.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for payment dispute case if reconciliation status is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert payment currency to port base currency. Used for multi-currency payment reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the payment was received. Used for monthly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the payment was received. Used for annual financial reporting and revenue recognition.']
  is_advance_payment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is an advance payment received before services are rendered. True if advance, False otherwise.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  navis_billing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System billing transaction that generated the invoice. Links payment back to operational billing source.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment. May include remittance advice, special instructions, or reconciliation remarks.']
  payer_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number from which the payment originated. Used for payment verification and reconciliation. Business-confidential financial information.']
  payer_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution from which the payment was sent. Used for payment tracing and reconciliation.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was received or processed by the port authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method or instrument used to make the payment. Indicates how funds were transferred (Electronic Funds Transfer, SWIFT wire, cheque, direct debit, letter of credit, or cash).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment in the accounts receivable lifecycle. Tracks payment from receipt through clearing and application to invoices.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of payment purpose. Distinguishes between advance payments, payments on account, invoice settlements, refunds, and credit note applications.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was first received or recorded in the system. Principal business event timestamp. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was reconciled. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment reconciliation against bank statements and invoice records. Tracks whether payment has been matched and verified.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment reference number provided by the payer or payment system. Used for reconciliation and customer inquiries.']
  remittance_advice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from the remittance advice document sent by the payer. Links payment to payer\'s accounts payable records.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was reversed or bounced. Null if payment has not been reversed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for payment reversal if payment status is reversed. Documents reason for chargeback, NSF (non-sufficient funds), or cancellation.']
  sap_clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI clearing document number generated when payment is applied against open invoice items. Links to SAP accounts receivable clearing process.']
  sap_payment_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI payment document number created during payment posting. Primary reference for financial audit trail in SAP S/4HANA.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount not yet allocated to invoices. Held on account for future invoice settlement or refund.']

  Note: 'Records of payments received from port customers against outstanding invoices. Captures payment reference number, payment date, payment method (EFT, SWIFT, cheque, direct debit, letter of credit), amount paid, currency, exchange rate, bank reference, clearing date, SAP clearing document number, payer account, payment status (pending/cleared/bounced/reversed), and allocation to invoices. SSOT for all inbound revenue cash receipts and accounts receivable clearing. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI payment clearing.'
}

  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment allocation record. Primary key for the payment allocation entity.']

This FK enables tracking of payments allocated to credit adjustments, completing the payment allocat']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Payment allocations clear AR receivables; cost centre attribution is required for cash application reporting and management accounting reconciliation in port finance. The denormalized cost_center plai']
  debit_note_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.debit_note. Business justification: Payment allocations can be applied against debit notes (additional charges). This FK enables tracking of payments allocated to debit notes, completing the payment allocation model (currently links to ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment allocations may hit different GL accounts for discounts, write-offs, or foreign exchange variances. Variance accounting requires GL-level tracking of allocation adjustments for financial state']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice receiving the payment allocation. Links to the invoice record generated from NAVIS N4 billing module.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific invoice line item receiving the allocation. Nullable when allocation applies to entire invoice rather than specific line.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Payment allocations create clearing journal entries in SAP FI when AR items are cleared; linking payment_allocation to its journal entry supports bank reconciliation, audit trail, and period-end AR cl']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment transaction being allocated. Links to the payment record in the payment processing system.']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Profit centre on payment allocations supports segment cash collection reporting — port operators need to track which business unit collected cash for treasury and segment reporting. The denormalized p']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Financial period (YYYY-MM format) to which this allocation is posted. Critical for period-end close, revenue recognition, and financial reporting.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount allocated from the payment to the invoice or invoice line. Represents the portion of payment applied to reduce outstanding balance.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The date when the payment was allocated to the invoice. Critical for accounts receivable aging and financial period assignment.']
  allocation_reference varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the payment allocation transaction. Used for tracking and reconciliation purposes across systems.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'System or process that created the allocation. Manual: user-entered; Automated: system-matched; EDI: electronic data interchange; Lockbox: bank lockbox service; API: external system integration.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the allocation. Pending: awaiting confirmation; Confirmed: successfully applied; Reversed: allocation undone; Cancelled: allocation voided; Disputed: under review for billing dispute.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the allocation was processed in the system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation timing.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation transaction. Full: entire invoice paid; Partial: portion of invoice paid; Advance: payment before invoice; Overpayment: payment exceeds invoice; On-account: unallocated payment held for future invoices; Credit-note: allocation from credit memo.']
  bank_reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number from bank statement used to match and reconcile this allocation. Critical for automated cash application and bank reconciliation processes.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Business area code for cross-company financial reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements across multiple legal entities.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the allocation is recorded. Critical for multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Supports multi-currency payment allocation for international trade transactions.']
  discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount deducted during allocation. Reflects payment terms incentives such as 2/10 net 30. Reduces allocated amount but clears full invoice value.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation is under dispute. True when customer has raised a billing dispute affecting this allocation. Impacts accounts receivable aging classification.']
  dispute_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute case if allocation is disputed. Links to dispute resolution workflow. Nullable when no dispute exists.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when payment currency differs from invoice currency. Used for multi-currency allocation and foreign exchange gain/loss calculation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the allocation belongs. Supports financial reporting and year-end close processes.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated amount converted to the company code local currency using the exchange rate. Used for financial reporting in local GAAP.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified the allocation record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the allocation record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the allocation. Used for documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or reconciliation details.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the invoice after this allocation is applied. Zero indicates invoice is fully paid. Critical for accounts receivable aging reports.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation was reversed or cancelled. Nullable for active allocations. Used for audit trail and financial period correction.']
  reversal_document varchar [note: 'SAP FI document number of the reversal transaction. Links to the offsetting entry that undoes the original allocation. Nullable for non-reversed allocations.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for reversing or cancelling the allocation. Nullable when allocation is not reversed. Examples: payment error, invoice dispute, incorrect amount, duplicate payment.']
  sap_clearing_document varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI clearing document number that links the payment and invoice in the financial system. Critical for financial close and reconciliation.']
  sap_clearing_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the SAP clearing document. Enables precise traceability to specific line-level clearing entries in SAP FI.']
  withholding_tax_deducted decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount withheld at source during payment allocation as per jurisdictional tax regulations. Common in international maritime trade transactions.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual payments to specific invoices or invoice lines, enabling many-to-many cash application where one payment covers multiple invoices or one invoice receives multiple partial payments. Captures allocation reference, allocation date, allocated amount, allocation type (full/partial/advance/overpayment/on-account), outstanding balance after allocation, discount taken, withholding tax deducted, SAP clearing item reference, allocation status (pending/confirmed/reversed), and reversal reason if applicable. Critical for accurate accounts receivable aging, bank reconciliation, cash application automation, and financial close in SAP S/4HANA FI.'
}

  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the credit note was formally approved by authorized personnel. Critical for audit trail and financial control compliance.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading reference number if the credit note relates to cargo handling or Terminal Handling Charge (THC) adjustments. Links billing adjustment to specific cargo shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number if the credit relates to container handling charges. Follows format AAAA1234567 with owner code and serial number.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit note record was first created in the billing system. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the credit being issued before tax adjustments. Represents the base revenue reversal amount in the port\'s operating currency.']
  credit_note_date date [note: 'Official issue date of the credit note document. Determines the accounting period for revenue reversal and financial reporting under IFRS 15 standards.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the credit note document. Follows port billing numbering convention CN-XXXXXXXX for audit trail and customer reference.']
  credit_note_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit note in the billing adjustment workflow. Tracks progression from draft creation through approval, application to accounts receivable, or cancellation.']
  credit_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized classification code indicating the business reason for issuing the credit note. Used for revenue adjustment analysis, dispute tracking, and service quality monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_error|service_failure|tariff_adjustment|commercial_concession|dispute_resolution|overcharge|damaged_cargo|delayed_service|contract_amendment|goodwill — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the specific circumstances justifying the credit adjustment. Provides business context for audit, dispute resolution, and customer communication.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit note transaction. Supports multi-currency billing operations at international port facilities.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether formal notification of the credit note has been sent to the customer via Port Community System or EDI. Tracks communication compliance.']
  customer_reference varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reference number or purchase order number associated with the original transaction. Facilitates customer reconciliation and accounts payable matching.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for the credit note. Enables monthly revenue adjustment tracking and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the credit note was issued for financial reporting and EBITDA analysis. Supports period-based revenue adjustment reporting.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential internal comments and notes regarding the credit note decision, approval rationale, or special handling instructions. Not shared with customer.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the credit note record. Maintains complete audit trail of changes to billing adjustments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credit note record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to the billing adjustment for audit and change management.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notification was transmitted. Provides audit trail for customer communication and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  original_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original billed amount from the source invoice before credit adjustment. Provides reference for calculating credit percentage and analyzing billing accuracy.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this credit note itself has been reversed or cancelled. Supports correction of erroneous credit notes and maintains complete audit trail.']
  sap_credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI credit memo document number generated upon posting to the financial accounting system. Provides integration reference between TOS billing and ERP financial records.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the credit memo was posted to SAP S/4HANA FI general ledger. Determines the accounting period for financial statement impact and EBITDA reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the port service category for which the credit is being issued. Enables revenue adjustment analysis by service line and operational performance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vessel_services|cargo_handling|storage|equipment_rental|pilotage|towage|mooring|wharfage|terminal_handling|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the credit adjustment, reversing previously charged VAT, GST, or other applicable port service taxes. Ensures compliance with tax authority reporting requirements.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit value including base credit amount and tax credit amount. Represents the full accounts receivable reduction applied to the customer account.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created the credit note record. Supports audit trail and accountability for billing adjustments.']

  Note: 'Formal credit documents issued to customers to reverse or reduce previously billed charges, arising from billing disputes, service failures, tariff adjustments, or commercial concessions. Captures credit note number, originating invoice reference, credit reason code, credit amount, tax credit amount, approval authority, approval date, credit status (draft/approved/applied/cancelled), and SAP credit memo document number. SSOT for all revenue reversals and billing adjustments in the port billing cycle.'
}

Table billing.debit_note {
  debit_note_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the debit note record. Primary key.']
  call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: Debit notes for additional vessel call charges (extended berth time, unplanned services) are issued against the originating call booking. Port finance teams reference the call booking to validate that']
  cargo_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Debit notes for cargo undercharges (actual weight/volume exceeding booked amount) reference the originating cargo booking. Port billing teams issue debit notes against specific cargo bookings when act']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Debit notes represent additional charges to customers; cost centre attribution is required for management accounting and cost allocation in port operations.   customs_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_hold.   cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Debit notes are issued within billing cycles for additional charges. This FK enables cycle-based debit note tracking and period reporting. No redundant columns - debit_note dates are issuance-specific']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dispute. Business justification: Debit notes can be disputed by customers. The debit_note table has dispute_flag and dispute_reason. Adding dispute_id FK enables direct linkage to the formal dispute record. Removes dispute_reason str']

  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this debit note supplements or adjusts. Links to the base billing document.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Debit notes often reference specific invoice line items for additional charges.   journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Debit memo postings create journal entries in SAP FI; linking debit_note to its journal entry supports audit trail, period-end close, and revenue recognition reporting.   port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit associated with this debit note, if applicable. Links debit to specific vessel operations.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Debit notes are issued when charges were under-applied against a tariff.   profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Profit centre on debit notes is required for segment revenue reporting in port operations — additional charges must be attributed to the correct business unit (e.g., container terminal, bulk terminal)']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Debit notes are often triggered by rate card corrections or under-billing against a contracted rate card. Billing correction workflows and customer dispute prevention require knowing which rate card a']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.run. Business justification: Debit notes can be batch-generated during billing runs (e.g., automated additional charge processing). This FK enables tracking which billing run generated each debit note, supporting batch processing']
  security_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Debit notes are issued to vessel operators or cargo owners for costs incurred due to security incidents (emergency response costs, additional inspection fees, damage assessments). Linking debit_note t']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Debit notes for underbilled vessel calls are issued to specific shipping lines; carrier-level debit note reporting, dunning workflows, and commercial account reconciliation require direct shipping lin']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: Debit notes for additional intermodal charges (demurrage, overweight penalties, additional handling) are raised against transport orders. debit_note currently links to port_call but not transport_orde']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: Debit notes for additional vessel charges (demurrage, extra services) require vessel master reference for charge validation and vessel account reconciliation. Critical for supplementary billing accura']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Compliance violations (MARPOL, ISPS, customs) result in penalty debit notes issued to vessel operators or cargo owners. Port authorities issue debit notes for regulatory penalties referencing the spec']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date when the customer formally acknowledged or responded to the debit note. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this debit note requires management approval before issuance. True if approval workflow is required, False if auto-approved.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the debit note was approved. Null if not yet approved or if approval is not required.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading reference number associated with the cargo or shipment related to this debit note. Links debit to specific cargo documentation.']
  charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross additional charge amount being debited to the customer before tax. Represents the base value of the supplementary billing.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number if the debit note relates to a specific container. Format: 4 letters (owner code) + 7 digits (serial number + check digit).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this debit note record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the debit note amounts. Indicates the monetary unit for all financial values.']
  customer_acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the debit note. Values: not_sent (not yet transmitted), sent (transmitted but not acknowledged), acknowledged (customer accepted), rejected (customer disputed), pending_review (under customer review).']
  debit_note_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the debit note, typically formatted as DN- followed by numeric sequence. Used for customer communication and reference.']
  debit_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for issuing the debit note. Common values: DMG (Demurrage adjustment), DET (Detention extension), THC_ADJ (Terminal Handling Charge adjustment), BAF_ADJ (Bunker Adjustment Factor correction), SURCHARGE (Additional surcharge), PORT_DUES (Supplementary port dues), LATE_FEE (Late payment fee), PENALTY (Contractual penalty). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DMG|DET|THC_ADJ|BAF_ADJ|SURCHARGE|PORT_DUES|LATE_FEE|PENALTY — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  debit_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the debit note was issued, providing context beyond the reason code. Includes specific circumstances, dates, and reference information.']
  debit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the debit note in the billing workflow. Tracks progression from draft through approval, issuance, customer acknowledgement, and potential dispute or reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|issued|acknowledged|disputed|cancelled|reversed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has disputed this debit note. True if disputed, False otherwise. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment of the debit note amount is expected from the customer. Calculated based on payment terms.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was formally issued to the customer. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this debit note record was last modified. Audit trail for record lifecycle and change management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to the debit note. Not visible to customer.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms applicable to this debit note (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD). Determines due date calculation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial period assignment and revenue recognition.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date when the debit note was reversed or cancelled. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this debit note has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed, False if active. Reversed debit notes do not contribute to accounts receivable.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the debit note was reversed. Null if not reversed. Documents the business justification for cancellation.']
  sap_debit_memo_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI debit memo document number generated when the debit note is posted to the financial system. Links billing document to accounting records.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the service period or event that triggered the additional charge. Used for calculating duration-based fees.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the service period or event that triggered the additional charge. Used for demurrage, detention, and time-based charges.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applicable to the additional charge. Calculated based on applicable tax rates and jurisdictional requirements.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of the debit note including all charges and taxes. Represents the net amount to be collected from the customer.']

  Note: 'Formal debit documents issued to customers for additional charges not captured in the original invoice, including demurrage adjustments, detention extensions, surcharge corrections, or supplementary port dues. Captures debit note number, originating invoice reference, debit reason code, additional charge amount, tax amount, approval status, SAP debit memo document number, and customer acknowledgement status. Complements credit_note as the upward billing adjustment mechanism.'
}

  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer describing the nature and basis of the dispute.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the dispute, used in customer communications and tracking. Format: DSP-YYYYNNNN.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the dispute was formally resolved and closed. Used for SLA compliance reporting and dispute lifecycle analytics.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the resolution decision, including rationale, policy references, and any corrective actions taken.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute resolution was recorded in the system, including time zone.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Final outcome classification of the dispute resolution. Indicates whether the dispute was accepted (full credit), rejected (no credit), partially accepted, withdrawn, or escalated.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the billing error or dispute. Used for continuous improvement and process optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|system_error|tariff_interpretation|customer_misunderstanding|process_failure|documentation_missing|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_actual_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Actual number of business days taken to resolve the dispute. Calculated from lodged date to resolution date for SLA compliance reporting.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the dispute resolution exceeded the SLA target resolution time. True indicates an SLA breach.']
  sla_target_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Number of business days within which the dispute should be resolved according to the applicable SLA. Used for performance tracking.']
  supporting_documents_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifiers or file paths to supporting documentation provided by the customer (e.g., Bill of Lading (BOL), Delivery Order (D/O), email correspondence).']

  Note: 'Formal records of customer-raised disputes against invoiced charges, tracking the full dispute lifecycle from lodgement through investigation to resolution. Captures dispute reference number, disputed invoice reference, dispute date, dispute reason (incorrect rate, wrong quantity, service not rendered, duplicate charge, tariff misapplication), disputed amount, customer contact, assigned dispute handler, investigation notes, resolution type (accepted/rejected/partial credit), resolution date, and credit note reference if applicable. Integrates with Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM service requests.'
}

Business justification: AR sub-ledger accounts must be scoped to a legal entity for statutory AR aging reports, intercompany reconciliation, and SAP FI-AR customer master integration. Multi-entity port operators require comp']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_broker. Business justification: Customs brokers are primary billing parties at ports, holding receivable accounts for customs-related charges on behalf of importers/exporters. Port finance teams maintain receivable accounts directly']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each customer receivable account has a default billing cycle. The receivable_account table has billing_cycle (STRING), and billing_cycle table has cycle_code. Adding billing_cycle_id FK enables JOIN t']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this customer in the port\'s EDI network. Used for automated invoice transmission via EDIFACT or X12 protocols.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each AR receivable account maps to a reconciliation GL account in the sub-ledger; this is a standard SAP FI-AR integration requirement. Port finance teams use this link for AR sub-ledger to GL reconci']
  haulier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.haulier. Business justification: Hauliers are billed customers for drayage and gate services. Ports maintain receivable accounts for hauliers to track outstanding balances, credit limits, payment terms, and aging. Essential for credi']
  icd_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.icd_facility. Business justification: ICD facilities are billed entities requiring dedicated AR accounts for storage, customs examination, and handling charges. receivable_account already tracks haulier and rail_operator AR; ICD facility ']
  parent_account_receivable_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent receivable account if this is a subsidiary or branch account. Enables hierarchical billing and consolidated statements.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: AR account management: every receivable_account in port billing maps to one participant_account for credit control, dunning, and collections. Maritime AR teams reconcile aging buckets and outstanding ']
  rail_operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.rail_operator. Business justification: Rail operators are billed for terminal services (loading, unloading, storage, track usage).
