  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Receivable accounts for contracted customers are associated with a specific rate card governing their billing terms. Customer account management, credit limit setting, and auto-billing configuration r']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.shipping_line. Business justification: Port AR credit management, carrier-level aging reports, and dunning processes require receivable accounts to be directly linked to shipping line master records. Maritime AR teams manage credit limits ']
  account_classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the account holder based on their role in the port community ecosystem. Determines applicable tariff structures and service entitlements.']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'Date when this receivable account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique alphanumeric code identifying the receivable account. Used for operational reference and reporting.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trading name of the customer organization or individual holding this receivable account.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'Date when this receivable account was first established and activated in the billing system.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receivable account. Active accounts can transact; suspended/blocked accounts require approval; closed accounts are archived.']
  aging_bucket_0_30_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 0 to 30 days past due date. Used for accounts receivable aging analysis and collections prioritization.']
  aging_bucket_31_60_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 31 to 60 days past due date. Indicates moderate collection risk.']
  aging_bucket_61_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged 61 to 90 days past due date. Indicates elevated collection risk requiring escalation.']
  aging_bucket_over_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding balance for invoices aged over 90 days past due date. Indicates high collection risk; may require legal action or write-off consideration.']
  auto_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enrolled in automatic payment via direct debit or standing order. True means invoices are auto-settled on due date.']
  average_days_to_pay decimal(5,1) [note: 'Rolling average number of days from invoice date to payment receipt over the last 12 months. Key metric for credit risk assessment.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the postal address to which invoices and statements are sent. Typically contains street number and name.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing postal address. Used for suite/floor/building details or additional address information.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality component of the billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address country.']
  billing_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for sending invoices, statements, and billing-related communications.']
  billing_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for billing inquiries and dispute resolution. Format varies by country.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code component of the billing address. Format varies by country.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region component of the billing address. Format varies by country.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices for this account should be consolidated with parent account for statement purposes. True enables group-level billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this receivable account record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is currently on credit hold. True means no new services can be rendered until outstanding balance is reduced.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for this account before credit hold is triggered. Expressed in the account\'s base currency.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit rating assessment for this account. Used to determine credit limits and payment terms. D indicates default risk. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|unrated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dispute_count_ytd varchar [note: 'Number of billing disputes raised by this account in the current calendar year. High counts indicate service quality or billing accuracy issues.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current collections escalation level (0=none, 1=reminder, 2=warning, 3=final notice, 4=legal action). Increments with each overdue period.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering invoices to the customer. EDI is common for large shipping lines; email for smaller customers.']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent invoice issued to this account. Used to identify dormant accounts.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received from this account in the account\'s base currency.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from this account. Used to calculate average days to pay and payment behavior trends.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this receivable account record was last updated. Updated automatically on any field change.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total amount owed by the customer across all unpaid invoices. Updated in real-time as invoices are issued and payments are received.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms defining the number of days from invoice date until payment is due. Net 7/14/30/60 are common port industry terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_14|net_30|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|cod — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for invoicing this account. All invoices will be issued in this currency unless overridden at transaction level.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA customer master number (KNA1/KNB1) linking this receivable account to the ERP financial accounting module. Ten-digit numeric identifier.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which account statements summarizing all invoices and payments are generated and sent to the customer.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary tax jurisdiction for this account. Determines applicable tax rates and compliance requirements.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Tax registration identifier for VAT/GST purposes. Required for invoicing and tax compliance reporting. Format varies by jurisdiction.']
  write_off_amount_ytd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount written off as bad debt for this account in the current calendar year. Impacts credit rating and future credit decisions.']

  Note: 'The single authoritative customer billing and accounts receivable master for each port community participant — THE unified customer record for all billing, credit management, and collections operations in this domain. Combines billing configuration (invoice delivery method, EDI partner ID, preferred currency, payment terms net 7/14/30/60, billing cycle vessel-call/weekly/monthly, account manager, billing address) with financial position tracking (credit limit, outstanding balance, aging buckets 0-30/31-60/61-90/90+ days, dunning level, credit hold flag, average days to pay). Captures account code, SAP customer account number (KNA1/KNB1), account name, VAT/tax registration, account classification (shipping line/freight forwarder/cargo owner/terminal operator/government), account status (active/suspended/blocked/closed), last payment date, credit rating, and EDI partner configuration. SSOT for customer identity, billing preferences, credit exposure, AR aging, and settlement terms. All invoices, payments, disputes, adjustments, dunning notices, and statements reference this single master.'
}

Table billing.revenue_event {
  revenue_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition event. Primary key.']
  anchorage_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.anchorage_area. Business justification: Anchorage dues revenue recognition requires linking revenue events to the specific anchorage area. Port authority revenue reporting segregates anchorage revenue by area; revenue_event has berth_id and']
  berth_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the berth or wharf where the revenue-generating service was delivered. Used for berth-level revenue analysis and capacity planning.']
  call_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.call_booking. Business justification: IFRS 15 revenue recognition requires linking recognized revenue to the performance obligation. For vessel call services, the call_booking represents the contract/performance obligation. Finance teams ']
  cargo_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.cargo_booking. Business justification: Cargo handling revenue (THC, wharfage) must be recognized against the cargo booking that constitutes the performance obligation under IFRS 15. Port finance teams require this link for cargo revenue re']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.channel. Business justification: Fairway/channel dues revenue recognition requires linking revenue events to the specific channel. Port authority financial reporting segregates channel/fairway revenue by channel; revenue_event has no']
  charge_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.charge_event. Business justification: Revenue events recognize revenue from underlying charge events in accordance with IFRS 15. Each revenue recognition event should reference the specific charge event(s) it recognizes. This link is crit']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Revenue events tied to specific operational cost centers for performance measurement. Earned revenue by terminal enables matching revenue recognition with operational cost incurrence for margin analys']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.customs_declaration. Business justification: Revenue recognition for customs-related services requires linking to declarations for compliance reporting and revenue stream analysis by cargo type. Supports regulatory reporting on customs service r']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Revenue recognition events are tied to billing cycles for IFRS 15 period accounting. The billing_cycle table contains fiscal_year, fiscal_period, revenue_recognition_date. This FK enables period-based']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition events directly drive GL postings under IFRS 15. Accrual-basis revenue accounting requires linking performance obligations to specific revenue GL accounts for compliance and financ']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: Terminal-level revenue recognition and P&L reporting require linking revenue events directly to the terminal where service was rendered. revenue_event has terminal_zone_id (zone level) but no terminal']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Revenue events for project-related port services (terminal construction phase billing, infrastructure project milestones) are settled against SAP internal orders. This link is required for project rev']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that triggered this revenue recognition event. Links to the billing invoice generated in NAVIS N4 or SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.tariff_item. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must link to the tariff item that defines the service and rate for IFRS 15 compliance, performance obligation tracking, and revenue stream categorization. Essential for fina']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Revenue recognition events post to the GL via journal entries; linking revenue_event to its journal entry is required for IFRS 15 revenue recognition audit trail and period-end close. Port operators m']
  marine_service_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying service order or work order that generated the revenue. May link to vessel services, terminal handling, or other port operations.']
  mooring_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marine.mooring_operation. Business justification: Revenue recognition for mooring services must reference the specific mooring operation confirming service delivery. Required for IFRS 15 compliance — revenue is recognised when the mooring service is ']
  original_revenue_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original revenue event that is being reversed or adjusted. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables audit trail for revenue corrections.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the port community participant account (customer, shipping line, freight forwarder, etc.) for whom revenue is being recognized.']
  pilotage_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marine.pilotage_assignment. Business justification: Revenue recognition for pilotage services must be tied to the specific pilotage assignment that earned the revenue. Port finance teams require per-assignment revenue recognition for IFRS 15 compliance']
  port_call_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vessel visit or call that generated the revenue. Links revenue events to specific vessel operations (berthing, cargo operations, pilotage, towage).']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Revenue recognition by profit center for segment reporting under IFRS 8. Divisional revenue recognition enables separate financial performance tracking for container, bulk, and passenger operations.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must be traceable to the rate card for revenue stream analysis by contract and period-end financial close. Finance teams require rate card attribution on revenue events for ']
  service_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.service_code. Business justification: Revenue recognition events must link to service catalog for correct GL account posting and revenue stream classification. Essential for financial accounting and revenue reporting compliance.']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the terminal or facility where the revenue-generating activity occurred. Supports multi-terminal port operations and terminal-level profitability reporting.']
  towage_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marine.towage_order. Business justification: Revenue recognition for towage services must reference the specific towage order. Port finance teams require per-order revenue recognition for towage revenue reporting, tug utilisation analysis, and I']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: IFRS 15/ASC 606 revenue recognition requires linking recognised revenue to the performance obligation. For intermodal services, the transport order is the performance obligation. revenue_event must re']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Warehouse storage revenue recognition requires linking revenue events to the specific warehouse facility. Revenue recognition for warehouse services (storage, handling) must be attributed to the wareh']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (YYYY-MM format) to which this revenue recognition event is allocated. Used for period-end financial close and EBITDA reporting.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment applied to the base revenue amount, such as discounts, surcharges (BAF, CAF), rebates, or contractual adjustments. Positive values represent surcharges; negative values represent discounts or credits.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of the reason for any revenue adjustment (e.g., volume discount applied, BAF surcharge for fuel cost, contractual rebate, billing correction, dispute resolution credit).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the revenue recognition event. Pending approval indicates awaiting finance manager review; Approved indicates cleared for GL posting; Rejected indicates the event was declined and must be corrected.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the revenue recognition event was approved for GL posting. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue event record was first created in the system. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the recognized and deferred revenue amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency port operations.']
  deferred_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue deferred to future accounting periods. Applicable for services delivered over time (e.g., storage, lease agreements, long-term contracts).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (YYYY) to which this revenue recognition event belongs. Supports multi-year financial analysis and annual reporting.']
  gl_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI document number assigned to the general ledger posting for this revenue recognition event. Used for financial audit and reconciliation. 10-digit numeric format.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue recognition event was posted to the SAP S/4HANA FI general ledger. May differ from recognition_date due to period-end close timing or batch processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this revenue event record was last modified. ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or audit notes related to the revenue recognition event. Used for documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or compliance annotations.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'The quantity of service units delivered that generated the revenue (e.g., number of TEUs handled, number of pilotage hours, number of storage days, tonnage moved). Unit of measure is context-dependent on service type.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue is recognized in the accounting system. This is the business event date for revenue realization, distinct from invoice date or service delivery date.']
  recognition_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the revenue recognition event, formatted as REV- followed by 10 digits. Used for audit trails and financial reporting.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'The method used to recognize revenue for this event. Point-in-time applies to discrete services (THC, pilotage, towage); Over-time applies to continuous services (storage, lease); Milestone applies to project-based services; Proportional applies to usage-based services; Straight-line applies to subscription or fixed-term contracts.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue recognition event. Pending indicates awaiting approval; Recognized indicates revenue has been posted to GL; Deferred indicates revenue is held for future periods; Reversed indicates a correction or cancellation; Adjusted indicates a modification post-recognition; Cancelled indicates the event was voided.']
  recognized_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of revenue recognized in this event, posted to the general ledger. Represents the portion of the invoice or service order value that is realized in the current accounting period.']
  revenue_stream_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the revenue stream for strategic reporting and EBITDA analysis. Vessel services include pilotage, towage, berth fees; Cargo handling includes THC, stevedoring; Storage/demurrage includes container yard charges; Marine services include launch, survey; Facility lease includes terminal, warehouse rental; Ancillary services include utilities, security.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this revenue event is a reversal or correction of a previously recognized revenue event. True indicates a reversal; False indicates a normal recognition event.']
  service_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the underlying port service was delivered or completed (e.g., vessel departure, container discharge completion, pilotage service completion). Used to determine point-in-time vs over-time recognition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this revenue event originated. NAVIS_N4 = NAVIS N4 Billing Module; SAP_S4HANA = SAP S/4HANA FI; MANUAL_ENTRY = Manual finance entry; PCS = Port Community System; TOPS = TOPS Expert TOS.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the transaction in the source system (e.g., NAVIS N4 billing transaction ID, SAP FI document number). Used for data lineage and reconciliation back to the source system.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the service quantity. TEU = Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit; FEU = Forty-foot Equivalent Unit; TON = Metric ton; CBM = Cubic meter; HOUR = Service hour; DAY = Calendar day; MOVE = Container move; UNIT = Generic unit count; KG = Kilogram; MT = Metric tonne. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TEU|FEU|TON|CBM|HOUR|DAY|MOVE|UNIT|KG|MT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'The rate per unit of measure applied to calculate the revenue amount. Represents the tariff or contracted price per TEU, per hour, per day, etc.']

  Note: 'Records the recognition of port service revenue against accounting periods in accordance with IFRS 15 / port revenue recognition policies. Captures recognition event date, invoice or service order reference, recognized revenue amount, deferred revenue amount, recognition method (point-in-time for THC/pilotage vs over-time for storage/lease), accounting period, SAP revenue account code, cost centre, profit centre, and recognition status. Enables EBITDA-level revenue reporting and period-end financial close in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.'
}

Table billing.run {
  run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing run batch execution record. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Billing runs are scoped to a legal entity in multi-entity port operators for SAP FI batch posting, GL period management, and statutory reporting. Company code on billing run is required for SAP integr']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Each billing run executes for a specific billing cycle. The billing_cycle table defines the authoritative billing period. Adding billing_cycle_id allows JOIN to retrieve cycle configuration and remove']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Billing runs are executed against a specific port tariff version. Tariff version control and billing run audit require knowing which port tariff governed a run — essential when tariff changes mid-peri']
  rerun_of_billing_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original billing run ID if this run is a rerun or correction of a previous failed or cancelled run. Null for original runs.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing run requires manual approval before invoices are released to customers. True for high-value or adjustment runs; false for routine periodic runs.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run was approved for invoice release. Null if approval is not required or not yet granted.']
  charge_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which billing charges were extracted: NAVIS-N4 (primary TOS), TOPS-EXPERT (alternate TOS), MANUAL (manual entry), or PCS (Port Community System for customs/trade charges).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was first created in the system. Represents the initial scheduling or setup of the billing run.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total invoice value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All invoices in a single run use the same base currency.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from run start to run end. Used for performance monitoring and capacity planning of billing batch jobs.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run execution completed or terminated. Null if run is still in-progress or suspended.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors or exceptions encountered during billing run execution. Zero indicates a clean run; non-zero requires review of error logs.']
  error_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary description of errors encountered during the billing run. Null if no errors occurred. Detailed error logs are maintained separately.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing run revenue was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Null if GL posting has not yet occurred.']
  invoice_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when invoices from this billing run were released and made available to customers via EDI, email, or customer portal. Null if not yet released.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this billing run record was last updated. Tracks any changes to status, approval, or other attributes throughout the run lifecycle.']
  navis_export_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the NAVIS N4 TOS charge export batch that sourced the billing data for this run. Provides traceability back to the Terminal Operating System.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by billing operators regarding special circumstances, manual adjustments, or issues encountered during the billing run execution.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number for the billing run, formatted as BR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for traceability and audit purposes.']
  revenue_recognition_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which revenue from this billing run is recognized for financial reporting and EBITDA calculation purposes.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing run: scheduled (queued for execution), in-progress (actively processing), completed (successfully finished), failed (error encountered), cancelled (manually aborted), or suspended (paused pending resolution).']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing run execution type: periodic (scheduled monthly/weekly), vessel-call (per vessel departure), ad-hoc (manual trigger), final (end-of-period close), adjustment (correction run), or reversal (cancellation run).']
  sap_posting_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the SAP S/4HANA FI posting batch where generated invoices were posted for financial accounting. Null if posting has not yet occurred or failed.']
  scheduled_run_date date [note: 'Date on which this billing run was originally scheduled to execute. May differ from actual run start date if the run was delayed or rescheduled.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the billing run execution began processing charges from NAVIS N4 Terminal Operating System (TOS). Represents the actual start of the batch job.']
  total_charge_lines_processed varchar [note: 'Total number of individual charge line items processed and aggregated into invoices during this billing run.']
  total_customers_billed varchar [note: 'Distinct count of customer accounts that received at least one invoice in this billing run.']
  total_invoice_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all invoices generated in this billing run, representing gross revenue before adjustments. Used for EBITDA-level revenue reporting.']
  total_invoices_generated varchar [note: 'Total number of invoices successfully generated and created during this billing run execution.']

  Note: 'Batch billing execution records capturing each scheduled or ad-hoc billing cycle run that generates invoices from NAVIS N4 TOS charge data. Captures billing run reference, run type (periodic/vessel-call/ad-hoc/final), billing period start and end dates, run date and time, total invoices generated, total invoice value, currency, run status (scheduled/in-progress/completed/failed/cancelled), operator ID, NAVIS N4 export batch reference, and SAP posting batch reference. Provides full audit trail of invoice generation cycles.'
}

  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cargo.shipment. Business justification: Charge events capture billable activities; linking to shipment enables direct cargo-to-charge traceability for freight charges, surcharges, and ancillary fees. Supports charge validation against shipm']
  slot_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.slot_booking. Business justification: Slot bookings generate booking fees, cancellation charges, and no-show penalties as distinct billing events. charge_event must reference slot_booking to correctly attribute these charges in port reven']
  slot_reservation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.slot_reservation. Business justification: Slot reservation fees are charge events generated when container slots are reserved on a vessel. Terminal billing teams must link each slot charge event to its slot_reservation to validate the fee aga']
  storage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.storage_tariff. Business justification: Storage charge events must reference the storage tariff that governed the daily rate and free storage days. Cargo owner disputes about storage charges require direct traceability to the applicable sto']
  surcharge_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.surcharge_rule. Business justification: Surcharge charge events (BAF, CAF, peak season) must reference the surcharge rule governing their calculation for regulatory audit and customer dispute resolution. Port billing teams require this trac']
  terminal_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal_zone. Business justification: Zone-specific surcharges (reefer zone, bonded zone, hazmat zone) are applied at terminal_zone level. charge_event is the billing trigger requiring zone context for zone-specific tariff application. in']
  thc_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.thc_schedule. Business justification: THC (Terminal Handling Charge) events are the highest-volume charge type in container port billing. Each THC charge event must reference the THC schedule that governed the rate for billing audit, cust']
  towage_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.towage_tariff. Business justification: Towage charge events must reference the towage tariff for vessel operator invoice verification and port authority revenue reporting. Towage is a regulated service; billing audit requires direct tariff']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.transport_order. Business justification: THC, inland haulage, and multimodal service charges are raised against transport orders as the primary commercial document. Linking charge_event to transport_order enables revenue reporting by order, ']
  truck_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.truck_appointment. Business justification: Ports bill no-show fees, slot reservation charges, and appointment cancellation penalties directly against truck appointments. charge_event needs this link to raise appointment-specific charges distin']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO number assigned to the vessel associated with this charge event, used for statutory charge calculations.']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: Compliance violations directly generate charge events for re-inspection fees, penalty surcharges, and remediation service charges. Port billing systems link the charge event to the specific violation ']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.warehouse. Business justification: Warehouse storage and handling charges (storage fees, demurrage) are generated per warehouse facility. charge_event is the upstream billing trigger; the specific warehouse determines applicable storag']
  weighing_station_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.weighing_station. Business justification: SOLAS VGM weighing services generate a charge per container weighed at a specific weighing station. charge_event is the billing trigger for VGM weighing fees; the weighing station reference is require']
  wharfage_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.wharfage_schedule. Business justification: Wharfage charge events must reference the wharfage schedule governing the rate. Port revenue reporting and cargo owner billing disputes require direct traceability from each wharfage charge event to t']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event was approved for billing by an authorized user or automated workflow.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current billing status of the charge event indicating whether it has been invoiced, is pending billing, has been disputed, waived, cancelled, or reversed.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with the cargo related to this charge event, if applicable.']
  cargo_volume_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'Volume of the cargo in cubic meters associated with this charge event, used for volume-based wharfage calculations.']
  cargo_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Weight of the cargo in kilograms associated with this charge event, used for wharfage and weight-based charge calculations.']
  charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated charge amount for this event before any adjustments, discounts, or taxes. Represents the base charge value.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO 6346 standard container identification number associated with this charge event, if applicable. Null for non-container charges.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daily_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Daily rate applied for demurrage or detention charges per excess day.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discount amount applied to this charge event based on customer agreements or promotional tariffs.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is currently under dispute by the customer.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description or code indicating the reason for the billing dispute, if the dispute flag is true.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the chargeable service event occurred in the operational system. This is the business event time, not the record creation time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the charge event indicating whether it is a commercial service charge, statutory/regulatory charge, demurrage, detention, wharfage, or port dues.']
  excess_days varchar [note: 'Number of days beyond the free time period for which demurrage or detention charges are calculated.']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is exempt from billing due to contractual agreements, regulatory exemptions, or special circumstances.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or code indicating the reason for charge exemption, if the exemption flag is true.']
  free_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of free days allowed before demurrage or detention charges begin to accrue, as per tariff or customer agreement.']
  free_time_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the free time period ends and chargeable demurrage or detention time begins.']
  free_time_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the free time period begins for demurrage or detention calculation.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is related to hazardous cargo handling, which may attract additional charges per IMDG Code requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge event record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  net_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net charge amount after applying discounts and adding taxes. This is the final billable amount for this charge event.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the service unit rendered (e.g., number of moves, number of days, number of connections).']
  reefer_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge event is related to a refrigerated (reefer) container requiring power connection and monitoring services.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the charge event, used for tracking and reconciliation across systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that originated this charge event record (e.g., NAVIS N4, VTMS, PCS, TOPS, SAP S/4HANA, or MANUAL entry).']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or reference key from the source operational system that generated this charge event, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  statutory_authority_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the statutory authority, regulation, or legal framework under which statutory charges (wharfage, port dues, pilotage levies, navigation fees) are assessed.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applied to this charge event based on applicable tax regulations and jurisdictions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the service quantity (e.g., move, day, hour, TEU, FEU, ton, CBM, connection, lift, each). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: move|day|hour|teu|feu|ton|cbm|connection|lift|each — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Rate per unit of measure applied from the tariff schedule to calculate the charge amount.']
  vessel_grt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross Registered Tonnage of the vessel, used as a basis for calculating port dues and other vessel-based statutory charges.']
  vessel_nrt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net Registered Tonnage of the vessel, used for certain statutory charge calculations and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Granular chargeable service events captured from NAVIS N4 TOS and other operational systems, representing all raw billable activities before invoice consolidation. Covers commercial charges (container moves, gate transactions, crane hire, reefer connections, hazardous handling, storage days) and statutory/regulatory charges (wharfage assessments based on cargo weight/volume, port dues based on vessel GRT/NRT, pilotage levies, navigation fees). Also captures demurrage and detention charge calculations including free time start/end, excess days, and daily rates. Captures charge event reference, event type, event timestamp, container number, vessel call ID, vessel IMO/GRT/NRT, BOL number, cargo weight/volume, equipment ID, quantity, unit of measure, applicable tariff code, calculated charge amount, currency, billing status (unbilled/billed/disputed/waived), exemption flag, free time days allowed, excess days, statutory authority reference, and source system reference. SSOT for all pre-invoice chargeable activity including commercial, statutory, and demurrage/detention charges.'
}

Table billing.statement_of_account {
  statement_of_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the statement of account record. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Statements of account are issued per legal entity in multi-entity port operators; company code scoping is required for statutory AR reporting and customer communication from the correct legal entity. ']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Statements of account are generated per billing cycle. The billing_cycle table contains the authoritative period dates and fiscal period information. Adding billing_cycle_id normalizes period tracking']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer receivable account for which this statement is issued. Links to the billing account master.']
  prior_statement_of_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on statement_of_account (prior_statement_of_account_id)']
  receivable_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.receivable_account. Business justification: A statement of account is a periodic AR summary document issued to a specific customer\'s receivable account. The receivable_account is the SSOT for customer billing identity, contact details, and AR b']
  run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing run batch process during which this statement was generated as part of the month-end or period-end billing close.']
  acknowledged_by_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the customer contact person who acknowledged the statement on behalf of the customer organization.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer formally acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the statement, raised a dispute, or has not responded.']
  aging_31_60_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 31-60 days from their due date.']
  aging_61_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by 61-90 days from their due date.']
  aging_current decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are current or overdue by 0-30 days from their due date.']
  aging_over_90_days decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the closing balance representing invoices that are overdue by more than 90 days from their due date. Indicates high collection risk.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date on which the statement was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for cancelling the statement, if the statement status is cancelled. Used for audit and process improvement.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the end of the statement period. Calculated as opening balance plus invoices and debit adjustments minus payments and credit adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  credit_adjustment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of credit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this statement are denominated.']
  debit_adjustment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of debit adjustment transactions applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The channel through which the statement was delivered to the customer. Aligns with customer communication preferences.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement was successfully delivered to the customer through the specified delivery method.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has raised a dispute or discrepancy regarding the statement contents or balances.']
  dispute_reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number of the billing dispute case raised by the customer in relation to this statement, if applicable.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which the statement period falls.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the statement period falls, used for financial reporting and period alignment.']
  invoice_count varchar [note: 'The total number of invoices issued to the customer during the statement period.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the statement record was last modified in the system. Audit trail for record updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional remarks, special instructions, or contextual information related to the statement.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The outstanding accounts receivable balance at the beginning of the statement period. Represents carried-forward balance from prior period.']
  payment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of payment transactions received from the customer during the statement period.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI document number associated with the statement generation or posting transaction in the financial system.']
  statement_date date [note: 'The date on which the statement was generated and issued to the customer. Represents the as-of date for all balances and transactions included.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'The periodic cadence at which this statement is generated for the customer account. Aligns with billing cycle configuration.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique statement reference number issued to the customer. Format: SOA-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the statement. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, customer acknowledgement, and closure.']
  total_credit_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all credit notes, credit memos, and favorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  total_debit_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all debit notes, debit memos, and unfavorable adjustments applied to the customer account during the statement period.']
  total_invoices_raised decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all invoices issued to the customer during the statement period. Increases the receivable balance.']
  total_payments_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate value of all payments received from the customer during the statement period. Reduces the receivable balance.']

  Note: 'Periodic accounts receivable statement documents issued to port customers summarizing all billing activity (invoices raised, payments received, credit/debit memos applied, outstanding balance) for a defined statement period. Captures statement number, customer account reference, statement date, statement period (monthly/quarterly), opening balance, total invoices raised, total payments received, total adjustments, closing balance, aging summary (current/30/60/90/90+ days), statement delivery method, and customer acknowledgement. Supports regulatory requirements for periodic AR disclosure, customer reconciliation, and dispute prevention. Generated as part of month-end billing close process.'
}

Table billing.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_cycle']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In multi-entity port operators (DP World, APM Terminals), billing cycles are scoped to a legal entity for statutory reporting, intercompany billing, and VAT period management. Company code on billing ']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marine terminal or port facility for which this billing cycle applies. Links to terminal master data.']
  parent_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent billing cycle if this is an adjustment or sub-cycle. Used to maintain hierarchical relationships between regular and adjustment cycles.']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Billing cycle configuration: billing cycles are set at the participant_account level (account has billing_cycle plain attribute). Port billing operations configure monthly/weekly cycles per account. T']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity or business unit responsible for issuing invoices in this billing cycle. Used for multi-entity port operations.']
  port_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.port. Business justification: Port authority billing cycles exist for port-level dues (port dues, conservancy dues) that are not terminal-specific. billing_cycle has terminal_id but no port_id; port-level billing cycles for port a']
  primary_prior_billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_cycle (prior_billing_cycle_id)']
  service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to intermodal.service. Business justification: Billing cycles in port operations are configured per intermodal service (e.g., weekly rail service billing, daily truck service billing). Linking billing_cycle to service enables service-specific bill']
  auto_invoice_generation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices for this billing cycle are generated automatically by the billing system or require manual review and approval.']
  billing_cycle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the billing cycle, including any special conditions, adjustments, or notes relevant to this cycle.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle was closed and finalized, preventing further transaction posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary transactions in this billing cycle. Typically USD for international maritime trade.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the billing cycle, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows port authority naming conventions.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the billing cycle, such as \'Monthly Container Services - January 2024\' or \'Quarterly Vessel Operations Q1-2024\'.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle. Active cycles are open for transaction posting; closed cycles are finalized for revenue recognition.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing cycle frequency. Determines the recurring pattern for invoice generation and revenue recognition.']
  dispute_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date during which customers can raise billing disputes for invoices in this cycle. Typically 30-90 days.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last date of the billing cycle period. All transactions with service dates on or before this date are eligible for inclusion in this cycle.']
  external_cycle_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier used by upstream systems (NAVIS N4, SAP) to identify this billing cycle. Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (1-12 for monthly, 1-4 for quarterly). Used for period-based financial reporting.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this billing cycle belongs, used for quarterly financial reporting and analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this billing cycle belongs, used for annual financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  gl_period varchar [note: 'The general ledger accounting period in YYYYMM format to which this billing cycle is assigned for financial reporting.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which financial transactions for this billing cycle are posted to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger for accounting purposes.']
  integration_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system from which billing cycle data originates, typically NAVIS N4 TOS or SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  invoice_generation_date date [note: 'The date on which invoices for this billing cycle are generated and issued to customers. Typically occurs after the cycle end date.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing cycle record is currently active and available for use. Inactive cycles are retained for historical reference but not used in current operations.']
  is_adjustment_cycle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a special adjustment or correction cycle used to process chargebacks, credit memos, or billing corrections outside the regular billing schedule.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this billing cycle record was last updated or modified.']
  late_fee_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether late payment fees are applicable for invoices in this billing cycle if payment is received after the due date.']
  late_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to overdue invoice amounts as a late payment penalty. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 1.5 for 1.5% per month).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes for internal use, capturing operational comments, exceptions, or special handling instructions for this billing cycle.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment for invoices in this billing cycle must be received to avoid late fees or penalties. Calculated based on payment terms.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from invoice date to payment due date for this billing cycle. Common values are 30, 45, or 60 days.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which revenue for this billing cycle is recognized in the general ledger for financial reporting and EBITDA calculation.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'The primary category of port services covered by this billing cycle. Used to segment revenue streams and align with Terminal Operating System (TOS) NAVIS N4 service classifications.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first date of the billing cycle period. All transactions with service dates on or after this date are eligible for inclusion in this cycle.']
  total_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount billed across all invoices in this billing cycle, before adjustments or payments. Used for revenue forecasting and EBITDA reporting.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied across all invoices in this billing cycle, including volume discounts, early payment discounts, and promotional adjustments.']
  total_invoice_count varchar [note: 'The total number of invoices generated for this billing cycle. Used for cycle reconciliation and billing volume tracking.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total net amount due across all invoices in this billing cycle after applying discounts and taxes. Represents the actual revenue to be collected.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount (VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes) included in all invoices for this billing cycle.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_cycle. Referenced by billing_cycle_id.'
}

Table booking.call_booking {
  call_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for call_booking']
  contact_person_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact_person. Business justification: A specific contact person (vessel agent, shipping line rep) is responsible for a call booking and receives ETA alerts, confirmations, and invoices. Port notification workflows and CRM case management ']
  cost_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Vessel call operations (pilotage, towage, berth services) incur direct costs allocated to marine operations cost centres. Essential for operational cost tracking and variance analysis in port P&L repo']
  facility_security_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to security.facility_security_plan. Business justification: ISPS Code requires port to verify vessel call against the active facility security plan before confirming the booking. call_booking.isps_security_level is a snapshot; the FK enables ISPS compliance ve']
  flag_state_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.flag_state. Business justification: Flag state determines PSC targeting, ISPS compliance, MARPOL compliance, and sanctions screening — all referenced in call_booking. Port state control and customs clearance processes require flag state']
  infrastructure_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.terminal. Business justification: A vessel call booking is made for a specific terminal. Terminal resource allocation, port dues calculation, operational scheduling, and billing are all organized at terminal level. Port community syst']
  last_port_un_locode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.un_locode. Business justification: last_port_of_call field exists. FK to un_locode master enables port validation, voyage tracking, customs reporting, health declaration processing, and quarantine requirement determination for vessel c']
  participant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.participant_account. Business justification: Call bookings are placed by shipping lines/agents under a participant_account. Port billing, credit limit checks, and account-level vessel call reporting all require this link. A maritime ops expert e']
  port_community_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the local shipping agent representing the shipping line for this vessel call. Responsible for pre-arrival coordination and documentation.']
  port_dues_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_dues_schedule. Business justification: Port dues are assessed on every vessel call at berth. call_booking is the primary vessel call record; the port dues schedule governs the specific dues calculation for billing and regulatory reporting.']
  port_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.port_tariff. Business justification: Vessel call bookings must reference the applicable port tariff schedule to determine vessel dues, berth charges, and service rates. Core business process: tariff application at booking confirmation an']
  primary_port_call_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vessel.call. Business justification: Vessel call booking represents the reservation/request for a port call, must link to the actual vessel.call record for berth planning, VTS coordination, and operational execution. Port operations team']
  profit_centre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Vessel call revenue (port dues, berth charges, service fees) is tracked by profit centre (container terminal, bulk terminal, marine services) for business unit P&L reporting and performance management']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to tariff.rate_card. Business justification: Commercial vessel calls for regular shipping line customers operate under negotiated rate cards with preferential pricing. Real business process: contracted rate application for vessel services and ca']
  sanctions_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_screening. Business justification: Vessel call bookings undergo sanctions screening of vessel, owner, operator, and flag state. Port denies entry to sanctioned vessels. ISPS and national security requirement.']
  shipping_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping line (carrier) operating the vessel for this call. Links to the customer/shipping line master record.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_profile. Business justification: Port call bookings are governed by SLA profiles defining vessel turnaround time and berth productivity targets. The SLA profile drives penalty calculations and performance measurement for each call. M']
  vessel_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_master. Business justification: imo_number exists on call_booking. FK to vessel_master enables vessel specification lookup for berth assignment, pilotage requirement determination, towage calculation, safety compliance verification,']
  vessel_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to masterdata.vessel_type. Business justification: Vessel type drives pilotage_required, towage_required, berth compatibility, and tariff_category_code — all referenced in call_booking. Port planners use vessel type at booking stage to determine servi']
  voyage_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to booking.voyage_nomination. Business justification: A call_booking is formally initiated from a voyage_nomination submitted by the shipping line. Linking call_booking.voyage_nomination_id -> voyage_nomination.voyage_nomination_id establishes the upstre']
  ata timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel arrived at the port anchorage or pilot boarding area. Populated after arrival event occurs.']
  atb timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel berthed at the terminal. Populated after berthing event occurs.']
  atd timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the vessel departed from the berth. Populated after departure event occurs.']
  booking_cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking was cancelled by either the shipping line or port authority.']
  booking_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking was officially confirmed by port operations and berth allocation was finalized.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique booking reference number assigned to the vessel call booking. Used for communication with shipping lines and agents.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vessel call booking. Tracks progression from initial nomination through confirmation to completion or cancellation.']
  booking_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vessel call booking request was initially submitted by the shipping line or agent to the port.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the vessel call booking was cancelled (e.g., schedule change, weather, operational constraints).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel call booking record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance process for the vessel and its cargo. Determines whether cargo operations can commence.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel is carrying IMDG-classified dangerous goods requiring special handling and safety protocols.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to arrive at the port anchorage or pilot boarding area.']
  etb timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to berth at the assigned terminal.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the vessel is expected to depart from the berth and leave the port.']
  expected_container_moves varchar [note: 'Estimated total number of container handling moves (load + discharge) for this vessel call. Used for resource planning and berth time estimation.']
  expected_teu varchar [note: 'Estimated total TEU volume (standardized container unit) for this vessel call. Used for yard planning and capacity management.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level applicable to the vessel call. Level 1 (normal), Level 2 (heightened), Level 3 (exceptional).']
  next_port_of_call varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the next port the vessel will visit after departing this port. Used for cargo planning and customs clearance.']
  pilotage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage service is required for the vessel to navigate into and out of the port.']
  reefer_container_count varchar [note: 'Number of refrigerated containers requiring power supply and temperature monitoring during the vessel call.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or coordination details related to the vessel call booking.']
  service_requirements varchar [note: 'Summary of requested port services for the vessel call (e.g., pilotage, towage, mooring, bunkering, waste disposal, fresh water supply).']
  service_rotation varchar [note: 'Shipping line service route or rotation code that this vessel call is part of (e.g., Asia-Europe Express, Pacific Loop).']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tug boat assistance is required for berthing and unberthing operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vessel call booking record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Core master record for a vessel call booking request submitted by a shipping line or agent. Represents the commercial intake of a vessel visit to the port, capturing vessel identity (IMO number, vessel name), expected arrival/departure windows (ETA/ETD/ETB), requested berth, service requirements summary, voyage details (voyage number, service rotation, last/next port), booking status lifecycle (nominated, pending, confirmed, cancelled, completed), and pre-arrival coordination data. Central SSOT for all vessel call bookings — all other booking domain entities reference this record. Aligned with DCSA booking model concepts. Sourced from Port Community System (PCS) and NAVIS N4.'
}

  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the service after completion. Used for final billing and financial reconciliation.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the service execution was completed. Used for billing, performance measurement, and SLA compliance.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the service execution began. Used for operational tracking and Service Level Agreement (SLA) measurement.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service order requires management or operational approval before confirmation. True if approval is required, False otherwise.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service order was approved. Used for audit trail and workflow tracking.']

  assigned_crew_count varchar [note: 'Number of crew members or personnel assigned to execute the service. Used for resource planning and cost allocation.']

  confirmed_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the port confirmed the service would be completed. Represents the committed service appointment end.']
  confirmed_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the port confirmed the service would begin. Represents the committed service appointment start.']

  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the service order. Influences resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']

  requested_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer or vessel agent requested the service to begin. Represents the initial service window request.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the service order. Used for customer communication and operational tracking.']
  service_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantitative measure of the service being provided. Unit depends on service type (e.g., cubic meters for bunkering, hours for pilotage, number of containers for stevedoring).']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service order. Tracks the progression from initial request through to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|confirmed|scheduled|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  service_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the service quantity. Defines how the service is quantified for billing and operational tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|cubic_meters|metric_tons|teu|feu|moves|trips|units — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_actual_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes taken to complete the service from the confirmed start time. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  sla_actual_response_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes taken to respond to and confirm the service request. Used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service order met all SLA commitments (response time and completion time). True if compliant, False if breached.']
  sla_target_completion_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual target time in minutes for the service to be completed from the confirmed start time. Part of the SLA commitment.']
  sla_target_response_minutes varchar [note: 'The contractual target time in minutes for the port to respond to and confirm the service request. Part of the SLA commitment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this service order record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any special instructions, requirements, or notes related to the service execution. Used for operational coordination.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for a specific port service request (pilotage, towage, mooring, stevedoring, crane allocation, bunkering, fresh water, waste reception) linked to a vessel call booking. Captures service type, requested and confirmed time windows, assigned resources/crew, service status lifecycle (requested, confirmed, scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled, no-show), priority, SLA parameters, and actual execution timestamps. Consolidates both the service request and the confirmed appointment into a single lifecycle entity — there is no separate appointment record. Aligned with SMDG port service message standards. Sourced from NAVIS N4 and VTMS.'
}

  draft_meters decimal(6,2) [note: 'Vertical distance between the waterline and the bottom of the vessel hull in meters. Critical for under-keel clearance calculations.']
  dwt_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight capacity of the vessel including cargo, fuel, water, stores, and crew in metric tonnes. Used for berth load capacity validation.']
  etb timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is expected to berth and commence mooring operations. Used for berth slot planning and resource allocation.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp when the vessel is expected to complete cargo operations and depart the berth. Used for berth slot release planning.']
  expected_teu_volume varchar [note: 'Anticipated number of TEU containers to be handled during this berth visit. Used for resource planning and berth productivity forecasting.']
  high_tide_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the next high tide event relevant to this berth reservation. Used for tidal window planning when vessel draft requires maximum water depth.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification code for dangerous goods carried by the vessel. Determines berth safety zone requirements and segregation rules.']
  isps_security_level varchar [note: 'ISPS security level applicable to this berth reservation. Determines security protocols and access controls during the vessel visit.']
  loa_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum length of the vessel from bow to stern in meters. Used to validate berth length capacity and ensure adequate berth space allocation.']
  low_tide_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the next low tide event relevant to this berth reservation. Used to validate minimum under-keel clearance during the berthing period.']
  mooring_gang_size varchar [note: 'Number of mooring personnel required for line handling operations during berthing and unberthing. Based on vessel size and mooring configuration.']
  number_of_tugs varchar [note: 'Number of tugboats required to assist with berthing and unberthing operations. Determined by vessel size, weather conditions, and port regulations.']
  pilotage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether marine pilotage services are mandatory for this vessel berthing operation based on vessel size, cargo type, or port regulations. True if pilot is required.']
  planning_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or module that created or last updated this berth reservation. Tracks data lineage for berth planning decisions.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this berth reservation. Influences berth allocation decisions during conflicts or capacity constraints.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'General free-text notes and comments related to this berth reservation. Used for operational coordination and information sharing among port stakeholders.']
  reservation_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth reservation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and booking lead time analysis.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique business identifier for the berth reservation. Used for external communication and tracking. Format: BR followed by 10 digits.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the berth reservation. Tracks progression from initial booking through vessel departure or cancellation.']
  reservation_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this berth reservation record was last modified. Tracks changes to berth allocation, timing, or status throughout the booking lifecycle.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of cargo service or vessel operation associated with this berth reservation. Determines required berth facilities and handling equipment.']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, equipment, or operational requirements for this berth reservation. Examples include reefer power, fresh water supply, or security protocols.']
  tidal_window_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vessel requires specific tidal conditions for safe berthing or departure due to draft constraints. True if tidal window planning is mandatory.']
  towage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tugboat assistance is required for safe berthing or unberthing operations. True if towage services are needed.']
  under_keel_clearance_meters decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required clearance between the vessel keel and the seabed in meters. Ensures safe navigation and berthing operations considering tidal variations.']
  weather_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this berth reservation is subject to weather-related operational restrictions. True if weather conditions may impact berthing operations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a berth slot reservation linked to a vessel call booking. Stores the assigned berth identifier, planned and actual berthing/unberthing timestamps (ETB/ATB/ETD/ATD), berth face allocation (port/starboard side-to), LOA and DWT constraints, beam restrictions, tidal window requirements, under-keel clearance parameters, reservation status (tentative, confirmed, occupied, departed, cancelled), and planning source (TOPS Expert or NAVIS N4 berth planner). SSOT for berth slot assignments within the booking lifecycle. Aligned with SMDG berth allocation message standards.'
}
